<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```md
# Sales Pipeline — Onboarding Guide

Welcome to the Holo Hive Sales Pipeline! This guide walks you through everything you need to know to manage your outreach, close deals, and keep your pipeline healthy.

---

## Table of Contents

1. [Overview — How the Pipeline Works](#1-overview--how-the-pipeline-works)
2. [Getting Started — Adding Opportunities](#2-getting-started--adding-opportunities)
3. [Understanding Stages](#3-understanding-stages)
4. [The Actions Tab — Your Daily To-Do List](#4-the-actions-tab--your-daily-to-do-list)
5. [Cold DM Outreach & The Bump System](#5-cold-dm-outreach--the-bump-system)
6. [Moving Through the Pipeline](#6-moving-through-the-pipeline)
7. [The Opportunity Detail Panel](#7-the-opportunity-detail-panel)
8. [Pipeline View — Table & Kanban](#8-pipeline-view--table--kanban)
9. [Overall Tab — Your Big Picture View](#9-overall-tab--your-big-picture-view)
10. [Orbit — Managing Shelved Deals](#10-orbit--managing-shelved-deals)
11. [Templates — Quick Message Copy](#11-templates--quick-message-copy)
12. [Dashboard & Metrics](#12-dashboard--metrics)
13. [Tips & Best Practices](#13-tips--best-practices)

---

## 1. Overview — How the Pipeline Works

The Sales Pipeline tracks every potential deal from first contact to closed-won. Each opportunity flows through **stages**, and the system tells you exactly what to do next via **actions**.

### The Stage Flow

\```
Cold DM → Warm → TG Intro → Booked → Discovery Done → Proposal Call → Contract → Closed Won
                                                                          ↓
                                                                        Orbit (shelved)
                                                                          ↓
                                                                      Closed Lost
\```

At the top of the page, you'll see **alert cards** that highlight anything needing immediate attention:

| Card | What It Means |
|------|--------------|
| **Booking Needed** | Deals past discovery with no future meeting set (BAMFAM violation) |
| **Overdue** | A meeting happened but you haven't logged the outcome |
| **Stale (7d+)** | Deals with no contact in 7+ days |
| **At Risk** | Closing-stage deals with a low temperature score |
| **Meetings** | How many meetings you have today / this week |

---

## 2. Getting Started — Adding Opportunities

Click the **"+ New"** button at the top right to create an opportunity.

### Required Fields
- **Name** — The company or contact name

### Recommended Fields
- **POC Platform & Handle** — Where you first reached out (Twitter, Instagram, Telegram, etc.) and their handle
- **Owner** — Who on the team owns this deal
- **Source** — How you found them:
  - *Cold Outreach* — You reached out first (affiliate field is hidden for this source)
  - *Referral* — Someone introduced you (a "Referrer" field appears)
  - *Inbound, Event, Twitter, LinkedIn, Telegram, Website*

### Affiliate (for non-cold-outreach sources)
If the deal came through an affiliate or partner, use the searchable dropdown to select one. If the affiliate doesn't exist yet, just type their name and click **"Add [name] as new affiliate"** — it's created instantly.

### Notes
Add any context that will help you or your team understand the deal.

---

## 3. Understanding Stages

Each stage represents where the deal currently sits in your sales process:

| Stage | What's Happening |
|-------|-----------------|
| **Cold DM** | You've identified a lead and are reaching out via DM |
| **Warm** | They've responded to your outreach |
| **TG Intro** | You've connected on Telegram |
| **Booked** | A discovery call is scheduled |
| **Discovery Done** | The discovery call happened, you understand their needs |
| **Proposal Call** | Time to present your proposal on a call |
| **Contract** | Proposal accepted, chasing the signature |
| **Closed Won** | Deal signed! |
| **Orbit** | Deal shelved — not the right time, but may revisit |
| **Closed Lost** | Deal didn't work out |
| **Nurture** | Long-term relationship, periodic check-ins |

### Color Coding
Each stage has its own color throughout the app — in badges, kanban columns, and table headers — so you can quickly identify where deals are at a glance.

---

## 4. The Actions Tab — Your Daily To-Do List

The **Actions tab** is the heart of the pipeline. It scans all your opportunities and tells you exactly what needs to happen next, ranked by priority.

### Priority Levels

| Icon | Priority | Meaning |
|------|----------|---------|
| Red triangle | **Urgent** | Do this now — overdue meetings, BAMFAM violations |
| Amber lightning | **High** | Should do today — bumps due, follow-ups needed |
| Blue clock | **Medium** | Can wait a bit — periodic check-ins |
| Gray clock | **Low/Wait** | No action needed yet — meeting is upcoming, cooling period |

### How Actions Work

Each opportunity gets **one primary action** based on its stage and status. For example:

- A cold DM with no bumps → **"Send First DM"**
- A cold DM bumped 4 days ago → **"Bump #2"**
- A warm/interested deal → **"Get TG Handle"**
- A booked deal with a past meeting → **"Discovery Done"**
- A proposal_call deal → **"Send Proposal"**

### Quick Actions & Alternatives

Next to the primary action button, you'll often see:
- **Quick action** (green button) — A common shortcut. For example, "Replied!" instantly moves a cold DM to warm.
- **Dropdown arrow** — More options like "Orbit", "Lost", or stage-specific alternatives.

### Filtering Actions

Use the tabs at the top to focus:
- **All** — Everything actionable
- **Outreach** — Cold DM, Warm, TG Intro, Booked stages
- **Closing** — Discovery Done, Proposal Call, Contract stages
- **Orbit** — Deals in orbit that may need attention
- **Waiting** — Deals in a cooling period (bump cooldowns, nurturing)

You can also filter by:
- **My Actions** — Only your deals
- **All Actions** — Everyone's deals

### Sorting

Sort your actions by Priority (default), Stage, Temperature, Deal Value, Name, Newest, or Oldest. Your sort preference is saved automatically.

---

## 5. Cold DM Outreach & The Bump System

The **Outreach tab** is dedicated to your cold DM pipeline with advanced filtering, pagination, and bulk operations.

### The Bump Lifecycle

Bumps track how many times you've reached out to a lead:

1. **Bump #0** → Action: "Send First DM"
2. **After sending** → bump_number becomes 1, 3-day cooldown starts
3. **During cooldown** → Action: "Wait Xd" (shows days remaining)
4. **After 3 days** → Action: "Bump #2"
5. **Repeat** up to 4 bumps
6. **After 4 bumps with no reply** → Action: "Review → Orbit"

> **Important:** During the "Wait" period, you can still mark a lead as "Replied!" if they respond — the action stays visible with a quick "Replied!" button.

### If They Reply

At any point, if a lead replies, click **"Replied!"** to move them to the **Warm** stage.

### Outreach Filters

- **Owner tabs** — Filter by team member or view all
- **Search** — Find opportunities by name
- **Path** — Closer (Path A) or SDR (Path B)
- **Bucket** — A, B, or C priority
- **Bump Status** — No bumps, 1-2 bumps, 3+ bumps

### Bulk Operations

Select multiple opportunities using the checkboxes, then:
- **Bump All** — Record a bump for all selected
- **Move to Warm** — Advance all to warm (they replied!)
- **Delete** — Remove selected opportunities

---

## 6. Moving Through the Pipeline

As deals progress, different prompts and actions guide you:

### Warm → TG Intro: "Got TG!"

When you click "Got TG!" (either as a quick action or from the dropdown), a dialog asks you to **enter their Telegram handle**. This saves the handle and moves the deal forward.

### Booked → Discovery Done: Bucket Assignment

After marking a deal as "Discovery Done" (the discovery call happened), you're prompted to **assign a bucket**:

| Bucket | Meaning |
|--------|---------|
| **A — Hot** | High intent, likely to close |
| **B — Warm** | Interested but needs nurturing |
| **C — Low** | Lower priority, longer timeline |

This helps prioritize your pipeline and shows up in the temperature score calculation.

### Proposal Call → Send Proposal → Contract

The proposal call stage has a two-step flow:
1. **"Send Proposal"** — Click this after the call to mark the proposal as sent. An activity log popup lets you record details.
2. **"To Contract"** — After the proposal is sent, this becomes the next action to move the deal into the contract stage.

### Contract → Closed Won: "Chase Signature"

In the contract stage, "Chase Signature" is your primary action (records a follow-up activity). Use the **"Signed!"** quick action when the deal closes.

Need to schedule another call? Use **"Schedule Call"** from the dropdown.

### Moving to Orbit

When a deal stalls, you can move it to **Orbit** from any stage. You'll be asked to select a reason:
- No Response
- Bad Timing
- No Budget
- Went with Competitor
- Other

Deals in orbit for 90+ days will surface a "Resurrect" action to re-engage them.

### Closing a Deal as Lost

Moving to "Closed Lost" prompts you for an optional reason. This helps track why deals don't close.

---

## 7. The Opportunity Detail Panel

Click any opportunity name to open the **slide-over panel** on the right side. This is your command center for a single deal.

### What You'll See

- **Header** — Name, stage badge, bucket, path, deal value
- **Details card** — Temperature score, owner, POC info, TG handle, source, next meeting, last contacted
- **Notes** — Any context about the deal

### Editing

Click the **pencil icon** to enter edit mode. You can update:
- Name, POC, owner, source, stage
- Deal value, currency
- TG handle, meeting date/time
- Notes and all other fields

### Bump Controls (Cold DM only)

A visual progress tracker shows bumps 1-4 as dots. Use the **+** and **-** buttons to manually adjust the bump count.

### Quick Actions

- **Move to Orbit** — Orange button at the bottom
- **Delete** — Red button (use carefully!)
- **Move to Stage** — Dropdown to manually move to any stage

### Activity Timeline

The bottom of the panel shows a chronological timeline of all activities:
- Notes, calls, messages, meetings, proposals, bumps
- Each has a type icon, title, description, outcome, and next steps

**To add an activity**, fill in the form at the top of the timeline section:
1. Select the type (Note, Call, Message, Meeting, Proposal)
2. Add a title and optional details
3. Set outcome, next step, and dates as needed
4. Click "Add"

---

## 8. Pipeline View — Table & Kanban

The **Pipeline tab** shows all active opportunities (non-orbit, non-closed) in two views:

### Table View (Default)

- Opportunities grouped by stage with collapsible headers
- Each header shows the stage name, count, and total deal value
- **Drag and drop** rows to reorder within a stage (use the grip handle on the left)
- **Click a name or deal value** to edit it inline
- **Click a row** to open the detail panel

### Kanban View

- Vertical columns for each stage
- **Drag cards** between columns to change stages
- Cards show key info: name, value, bucket, temperature, bump progress
- Collapsible columns with chevrons
- Drop zones for "Orbit" and "Lost" on the sides

### Path Filter

Use the dropdown to filter by:
- **All Paths** — See everything
- **Path A (Closer)** — Direct closer pipeline
- **Path B (SDR)** — SDR-led pipeline

---

## 9. Overall Tab — Your Big Picture View

The **Overall tab** gives you a consolidated view with three collapsible sections:

1. **Outreach** (blue) — Your full cold DM pipeline
2. **Pipeline** (green) — All active deals in table view
3. **Orbit** (amber) — Shelved deals

The tab badge shows the **total count of active opportunities** (excluding closed won and closed lost).

Click any section header to expand/collapse it. This is great for a quick daily review without switching between tabs.

---

## 10. Orbit — Managing Shelved Deals

The **Orbit tab** groups shelved deals by their orbit reason (No Response, Bad Timing, No Budget, etc.).

Each group shows:
- The reason label and count
- A table with deal details, time in orbit, and last contacted date

### What Happens Over Time

- Deals in orbit for **90+ days** get a "Resurrect" action in the Actions tab
- You can manually resurrect a deal from the orbit tab's menu → moves it back to Cold DM
- Or mark it as Lost if it's truly dead

---

## 11. Templates — Quick Message Copy

The **Templates tab** stores reusable DM and message templates for each pipeline stage.

### Browsing Templates

Use the **stage filter pills** at the top to filter:
- All, Cold DM, Warm, TG Intro, Booked, Discovery Done, Proposal Call, Contract, Bumps

Each template card shows:
- Name and stage/sub-type badges
- Content preview
- Variable placeholders (e.g., `[KOL_NAME]`, `[PROJECT_NAME]`)

### Using a Template

Click the **copy icon** on any template card to copy the content to your clipboard. Then paste it into your DM and replace the placeholders.

### Creating a Template

1. Click **"+ New Template"**
2. Fill in:
   - **Name** — A descriptive label (e.g., "Cold DM — Crypto Project Intro")
   - **Stage** — Which stage this template is for
   - **Sub-type** — For bumps: Bump 1, Bump 2, Bump 3. For others: General, Initial, Follow-up
   - **Content** — Your message. Use `[KOL_NAME]`, `[PROJECT_NAME]`, etc. as placeholders
3. Click **"Create Template"**

### Editing & Deleting

- **Edit** — Click the pencil icon on a template card
- **Delete** — Click the trash icon (immediate, no confirmation)

### Pre-loaded Templates

The system comes with default templates:
- Cold DM — Initial Outreach
- Bump 1, 2, 3 — Escalating follow-ups
- Warm — Reply follow-up
- TG Intro — First Telegram message

---

## 12. Dashboard & Metrics

Click the **"Sales Dashboard"** header at the top of the page to expand detailed analytics.

### Pipeline Health

| Metric | What It Shows |
|--------|--------------|
| Pipeline Value | Total deal value of all active opportunities |
| Weighted Pipeline | Deal values weighted by stage probability (Cold DM = 5%, Contract = 90%) |
| Active Deals | Count of non-closed/non-orbit deals |
| Revenue | Total value of closed-won deals |
| Deals Won | Number of closed-won deals |
| In Orbit | Number of shelved deals |

### Conversion Funnel

A visual bar chart showing how many deals pass through each stage — from DMs sent all the way to closed won. Helps identify where deals drop off.

### Key Rates

- **Response Rate** — % of cold DMs that got a reply
- **Close Rate** — Won / (Won + Lost)
- **Avg Deal Size** — Mean value of closed-won deals
- **Avg Close Time** — Days from created to closed
- **Qualified %** — Bucket A + B as % of total
- **Bucket A %** — Top-tier deals as % of total

### Bottleneck Analysis

Shows you:
- **Biggest Drop-off** — The stage where you lose the most deals
- **Slowest Stage** — Where deals sit the longest
- A full conversion table with stage-by-stage breakdown

### Temperature Score

Each opportunity has an auto-calculated temperature (0-100) based on:
- Bucket assignment (A = 40 base, B = 25, C = 10)
- How recently you've been in contact
- Number of logged activities
- Whether a meeting is booked
- Stage progression bonuses
- Penalties for stale deals, bump exhaustion, or silent warm leads

Use the **"Recalc Scores"** button to refresh all scores.

---

## 13. Tips & Best Practices

### Daily Workflow
1. Open the **Actions tab** — this is your to-do list
2. Work through urgent items first (red), then high (amber)
3. Check the **alert cards** at the top for anything critical
4. Log activities as you go — the system tracks everything

### BAMFAM Rule
**B**ook **A** **M**eeting **F**rom **A** **M**eeting. After any call past discovery, always schedule the next meeting before hanging up. The system enforces this — deals without a future meeting get flagged.

### Keep It Moving
- Don't let deals sit in a stage for too long without action
- The "Stale 7d+" alert catches deals you might have forgotten
- Temperature scores drop when deals go cold — use them as a health check

### Use Templates
Don't write every DM from scratch. Copy a template, personalize the placeholders, and send. Consistency + speed.

### Activity Logging
Every time you interact with a lead (DM, call, meeting, proposal), log it. This builds a timeline that helps your whole team understand where things stand — and it improves your temperature scores.

### When to Orbit
If a lead goes silent after 3-4 bumps, or if timing/budget isn't right, move them to Orbit rather than keeping them clogging your pipeline. They'll surface again in 90 days via the "Resurrect" action.

---

*Happy selling! If you have questions, reach out to your team lead or check the in-app guidance hints that appear with each action.*

```

File created successfully at: /Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/docs/sales-pipeline-onboarding.md
```
</details></tool-use>

1. **Initial Plan Implementation** - User asked to implement a plan with 3 main changes:
   - Show total opportunity count badge on "Overall" tab

2. **Files read**: Sidebar.tsx, salesPipelineService.ts, sales-pipeline/page.tsx (large file, read in sections)

3. **DB Migration**: Created `sales_dm_templates` table with RLS policies and seed data via Supabase MCP

4. **Sidebar change**: Removed Pipeline link block (lines 282-296)

5. **Service methods**: Added SalesDmTemplate interface, CreateSalesDmTemplateData, and CRUD methods (getTemplates, createTemplate, updateTemplate, deleteTemplate)

6. **UI changes**: 
   - Added count badge to Overall tab
   - Added Templates tab with stage filter pills, template cards grid, create/edit dialog
   - Extended activeTab type to include 'templates'
   - Added template-related state variables
   - Fetched templates in fetchData

7. **Teal color buttons**: User asked to make template buttons teal (#3e8692) - applied to "New Template" and "Create Template"/"Save Changes" buttons

8. **Actions conditions review**: User asked to see all action conditions - I showed the full getNextAction logic

9. **Proposal sent removal**: User said "We do not need proposal sent anymore" - wanted proposal_call to have meeting outcome → proposal sent flow. Multiple rounds of changes:
   - First changed proposal_call action to target proposal_sent
   - Then removed proposal_sent from PIPELINE_STAGES, PATH_A_STAGES, PATH_B_STAGES
   - Updated all references throughout the page (BAMFAM checks, forecasting, funnel, metrics, stage selects)
   - Kept in ALL_V2_STAGES for existing data
   - Deleted existing deals in proposal_sent stage
   - Updated discovery_done action to target proposal_call instead

10. **Log Meeting Outcome stuck issue**: After logging meeting outcome, the action kept showing. Root cause: `confirmActivityLog` never updated `last_contacted_at`. Fixed by always updating `last_contacted_at` when confirming activity log.

11. **Proposal call flow refinement**: User wanted proposal_call to show "Send Proposal" action after logging meeting. Implemented two sub-states using `proposal_sent_at`:
    - !proposal_sent_at → "Send Proposal" (open_detail, sets proposal_sent_at)
    - proposal_sent_at → "To Contract" (stage_change → v2_contract)
    - Added `skipGlobalChecks` for proposal_call without proposal_sent_at to bypass BAMFAM and past-meeting checks

12. **Test data**: Deleted all opportunities and created 15 test opportunities across all stages

13. **Wait state "Replied!" fix**: User noted cold DM opportunities in wait state had no way to mark as replied. Fixed by making wait state `isActionable: true` with "Replied!" and "Orbit" alternatives.

14. **Warm "Follow Up" popup**: Changed warm stage "Follow Up" action from `open_detail` to `bump` to show same activity log popup as "Send First DM"

15. **"Got TG!" prompt**: User asked for TG handle input prompt. Created:
    - tgHandlePrompt/tgHandleValue state
    - Intercepted `tg_intro` stage change in handleStageChange
    - confirmTgHandle function
    - renderTgHandlePrompt dialog
    - Also changed "Get TG Handle" from open_detail to bump for activity popup

16. **Chase Signature popup + Schedule Call**: Changed chase signature from open_detail to bump, added "Schedule Call" alternative

17. **Remove proposal_sent from side panel dropdown**: Filtered ALL_V2_STAGES to exclude proposal_sent in the "Move to Stage" select

18. **Bucket assignment after discovery call**: Created bucket prompt dialog that appears when moving to discovery_done. Three-button selector (A/B/C).

19. **Double popup fix**: Discovery call popup showed twice because handleActionExecute opened activity log AND confirmBucket also opened one. Fixed by adding `stagesWithOwnPrompts` array to skip activity log for stages with custom prompts.

20. **Affiliate dropdown**: User wanted:
    - Hide affiliate when source is cold_outreach
    - Hide details section
    - Make affiliate searchable with ability to add new
    - Changed from datalist to Popover+Command pattern matching public link log's client dropdown

21. **Onboarding doc**: Created comprehensive markdown guide at docs/sales-pipeline-onboarding.md
</analysis>

<summary>
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user requested implementation of a multi-part plan and then iteratively refined the sales pipeline page. The core requests were:
   - Add total opportunity count badge on the "Overall" tab
   - Hide the old "Pipeline" link from the sidebar (keep "Sales")
   - Add a stage-specific DM/message templates system with DB table, service methods, and UI tab
   - Remove `proposal_sent` as a pipeline stage, restructuring the flow so `proposal_call` handles meeting outcome → send proposal → contract
   - Fix multiple action flow issues (stuck actions, missing popups, double popups)
   - Add interactive prompts for TG handle input, bucket assignment after discovery call
   - Improve the new opportunity dialog (hide affiliate for cold outreach, hide details section, use Command dropdown for affiliates with inline creation)
   - Make various action buttons show the activity log popup consistently
   - Create a comprehensive onboarding guide markdown document

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Next.js app router with `'use client'` components
   - Supabase for database, RLS policies, and MCP-based migrations
   - shadcn/ui components (Tabs, Dialog, Command/Popover combo dropdown, Badge, Select, etc.)
   - Action priority system with `getNextAction()` computing stage-specific actions
   - Bump system with 3-day cooldown for cold DM outreach
   - BAMFAM rule (Book A Meeting From A Meeting) enforced via global checks
   - Global pre-checks in `getNextAction` (past-meeting check, BAMFAM check) that run before the stage switch
   - `skipGlobalChecks` pattern to let specific stages bypass global checks
   - `stagesWithOwnPrompts` pattern to prevent double activity log popups
   - Temperature score auto-calculation via Supabase RPC
   - DnD (drag and drop) with @dnd-kit for kanban and table views

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - **`components/Sidebar.tsx`**
     - Removed the Pipeline link block (lines 282-296) to hide `/crm/pipeline` from navigation
     - The Sales Pipeline link (`/crm/sales-pipeline`) remains

   - **`lib/salesPipelineService.ts`**
     - Added `SalesDmTemplate` and `CreateSalesDmTemplateData` interfaces
     - Added template CRUD methods: `getTemplates()`, `createTemplate()`, `updateTemplate()`, `deleteTemplate()`
     - Removed `proposal_sent` from `PIPELINE_STAGES`, `PATH_A_STAGES`, `PATH_B_STAGES`
     - Kept `proposal_sent` in `ALL_V2_STAGES` and type definition for backward compatibility
     - Removed `proposal_sent` from BAMFAM violations and metrics post-discovery arrays
     - Updated weighted pipeline probability: `proposal_call: 0.7` (was 0.75 with proposal_sent at 0.6)

   - **`app/crm/sales-pipeline/page.tsx`** (main file, ~4900+ lines)
     
     **Imports added:**
     ```tsx
     import { Command, CommandEmpty, CommandGroup, CommandInput, CommandItem, CommandList } from '@/components/ui/command';
     // Added to lucide imports: Copy, Check, ChevronsUpDown
     // Added from salesPipelineService: SalesDmTemplate, CreateSalesDmTemplateData
     ```

     **State additions:**
     ```tsx
     // Templates
     const [templates, setTemplates] = useState<SalesDmTemplate[]>([]);
     const [templateStageFilter, setTemplateStageFilter] = useState<string>('all');
     const [isTemplateDialogOpen, setIsTemplateDialogOpen] = useState(false);
     const [editingTemplate, setEditingTemplate] = useState<SalesDmTemplate | null>(null);
     const [templateForm, setTemplateForm] = useState<CreateSalesDmTemplateData>({ name: '', stage: 'cold_dm', sub_type: 'general', content: '' });
     const [isTemplateSubmitting, setIsTemplateSubmitting] = useState(false);

     // TG handle prompt
     const [tgHandlePrompt, setTgHandlePrompt] = useState<{ oppId: string; oppName: string } | null>(null);
     const [tgHandleValue, setTgHandleValue] = useState('');

     // Bucket assignment prompt
     const [bucketPrompt, setBucketPrompt] = useState<{ oppId: string; oppName: string } | null>(null);
     const [bucketValue, setBucketValue] = useState<Bucket>('B');

     // Affiliate popover
     const [affiliatePopoverOpen, setAffiliatePopoverOpen] = useState(false);
     const [affiliateSearch, setAffiliateSearch] = useState('');
     ```

     **activeTab type extended:**
     ```tsx
     const [activeTab, setActiveTab] = useState<'actions' | 'outreach' | 'pipeline' | 'orbit' | 'overview' | 'templates'>('actions');
     ```

     **handleStageChange - intercepts for custom prompts:**
     ```tsx
     if (newStage === 'tg_intro') {
       setTgHandlePrompt({ oppId, oppName });
       setTgHandleValue('');
       return;
     }
     if (newStage === 'discovery_done') {
       setBucketPrompt({ oppId, oppName });
       setBucketValue('B');
       return;
     }
     // Also always sets last_contacted_at on stage change
     const updateData: any = { stage: newStage as OpportunityStage };
     updateData.last_contacted_at = new Date().toISOString();
     ```

     **getNextAction - key changes:**
     - Added `skipGlobalChecks` for proposal_call without proposal_sent_at:
     ```tsx
     const skipGlobalChecks = opp.stage === 'proposal_call' && !opp.proposal_sent_at;
     ```
     - proposal_call case with two sub-states:
     ```tsx
     case 'proposal_call': {
       if (opp.proposal_sent_at) {
         return { label: 'To Contract', ... targetStage: 'v2_contract', ... };
       }
       return { label: 'Send Proposal', ... actionType: 'open_detail', ... };
     }
     ```
     - discovery_done targets proposal_call instead of proposal_sent
     - Wait state made actionable with Replied!/Orbit alternatives
     - warm "Follow Up" changed to actionType: 'bump'
     - warm "Get TG Handle" changed to actionType: 'bump'
     - v2_contract "Chase Signature" changed to actionType: 'bump', added "Schedule Call" alternative

     **handleActionExecute - stagesWithOwnPrompts:**
     ```tsx
     const stagesWithOwnPrompts = ['orbit', 'v2_closed_lost', 'tg_intro', 'discovery_done'];
     if (!stagesWithOwnPrompts.includes(action.targetStage)) {
       // show activity log popup
     }
     ```
     
     **"Send Proposal" handler:**
     ```tsx
     } else if (action.label === 'Send Proposal') {
       await SalesPipelineService.update(oppId, { proposal_sent_at: new Date().toISOString() } as any);
       openActivityLogPrompt(oppId, opp.name, 'proposal', 'Proposal sent');
       await fetchData();
     }
     ```

     **confirmActivityLog fix - always updates last_contacted_at:**
     ```tsx
     const activityUpdate: any = { last_contacted_at: new Date().toISOString() };
     if (activityLogPrompt.showMeetingPicker && activityLogForm.meeting_date) {
       // ... set meeting date
       activityUpdate.next_meeting_at = meetingDate.toISOString();
     }
     await SalesPipelineService.update(activityLogPrompt.oppId, activityUpdate);
     ```

     **New opportunity dialog changes:**
     - Affiliate hidden when `form.source === 'cold_outreach'`
     - Affiliate uses Popover+Command dropdown with inline "Add new affiliate"
     - Details section wrapped in `{isEdit && <details className="group hidden">...}`
     - Move to Stage dropdown filters out proposal_sent: `ALL_V2_STAGES.filter(s => s !== 'proposal_sent')`

   - **DB Migration: `sales_dm_templates` table**
     - UUID primary key, name, stage, sub_type, content, variables (JSONB), is_active, created_by, timestamps
     - RLS: authenticated SELECT, admin INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE
     - 6 seed templates: Cold DM initial, Bump 1/2/3, Warm follow-up, TG Intro first message

   - **`docs/sales-pipeline-onboarding.md`**
     - Comprehensive onboarding guide covering all pipeline features
     - 13 sections: Overview, Adding Opportunities, Stages, Actions Tab, Bump System, Moving Through Pipeline, Detail Panel, Pipeline Views, Overall Tab, Orbit, Templates, Dashboard & Metrics, Tips & Best Practices

4. Errors and Fixes:
   - **"Log Meeting Outcome" stuck after logging**: `confirmActivityLog` never updated `last_contacted_at`, so the global past-meeting check kept firing. Fixed by always setting `last_contacted_at = now` in confirmActivityLog.
   - **Proposal_sent still showing as stage**: User kept seeing it because (a) existing deals were in that stage, (b) it was in pipeline stage arrays, (c) it was in various UI dropdowns. Fixed by removing from all stage arrays, deleting existing deals via SQL, filtering from UI selects.
   - **BAMFAM/past-meeting checks blocking proposal_call**: After removing proposal_sent, the global BAMFAM check would fire "Book Next Meeting!" and the past-meeting check would fire "Log Meeting Outcome" before the switch case could show "Send Proposal". Fixed by adding `skipGlobalChecks` flag for `proposal_call` when `!proposal_sent_at`.
   - **Discovery Done popup showing twice**: Both `handleActionExecute` (via stageActivityMap) and `confirmBucket` each opened an activity log popup. Fixed by adding `stagesWithOwnPrompts` array to skip the activity log in handleActionExecute for stages that have custom prompts.
   - **Affiliate dropdown not matching app patterns**: Initially used HTML datalist, user requested it match the public link log's Command dropdown pattern. Replaced with Popover+Command component.

5. Problem Solving:
   - Traced the full action execution flow to identify where duplicate popups and stuck states occurred
   - Identified that global checks (past-meeting, BAMFAM) in `getNextAction` override stage-specific actions and needed conditional bypassing
   - Resolved the proposal_sent removal by keeping it in ALL_V2_STAGES/type for backward compatibility while removing from all visible UI and pipeline flow
   - Created 15 test opportunities spanning all stages with varying states to enable thorough testing

     **activeTab type extended:**

     **handleStageChange - intercepts for custom prompts:**
     ```tsx
     if (newStage === 'tg_intro') {
       setTgHandlePrompt({ oppId, oppName });
       setTgHandleValue('');
       return;

       setBucketPrompt({ oppId, oppName });
       setBucketValue('B');
       return;
     }
     // Also always sets last_contacted_at on stage change

     updateData.last_contacted_at = new Date().toISOString();
     ```

     **getNextAction - key changes:**

     ```
     - proposal_call case with two sub-states:

       if (opp.proposal_sent_at) {

       }
       return { label: 'Send Proposal', ... actionType: 'open_detail', ... };
     }
     ```

     ```

     }

4. Errors and Fixes:
   - **"Log Meeting Outcome" stuck after logging**: `confirmActivityLog` never updated `last_contacted_at`, so the global past-meeting check kept firing. Fixed by always setting `last_contacted_at = now` in confirmActivityLog.
   - **Proposal_sent still showing as stage**: User kept seeing it because (a) existing deals were in that stage, (b) it was in pipeline stage arrays, (c) it was in various UI dropdowns. Fixed by removing from all stage arrays, deleting existing deals via SQL, filtering from UI selects.
   - **BAMFAM/past-meeting checks blocking proposal_call**: After removing proposal_sent, the global BAMFAM check would fire "Book Next Meeting!" and the past-meeting check would fire "Log Meeting Outcome" before the switch case could show "Send Proposal". Fixed by adding `skipGlobalChecks` flag for `proposal_call` when `!proposal_sent_at`.
   - **Discovery Done popup showing twice**: Both `handleActionExecute` (via stageActivityMap) and `confirmBucket` each opened an activity log popup. Fixed by adding `stagesWithOwnPrompts` array to skip the activity log in handleActionExecute for stages that have custom prompts.
   - **Affiliate dropdown not matching app patterns**: Initially used HTML datalist, user requested it match the public link log's Command dropdown pattern. Replaced with Popover+Command component.

5. Problem Solving:
   - Traced the full action execution flow to identify where duplicate popups and stuck states occurred
   - Identified that global checks (past-meeting, BAMFAM) in `getNextAction` override stage-specific actions and needed conditional bypassing
   - Resolved the proposal_sent removal by keeping it in ALL_V2_STAGES/type for backward compatibility while removing from all visible UI and pipeline flow
   - Created 15 test opportunities spanning all stages with varying states to enable thorough testing

 `./docs/sales-pipeline-onboarding.md`

```text
     1→# Sales Pipeline — Onboarding Guide
     2→
     3→Welcome to the Holo Hive Sales Pipeline! This guide walks you through everything you need to know to manage your outreach, close deals, and keep your pipeline healthy.
     4→
     5→---
     6→
     7→## Table of Contents
     8→
     9→1. [Overview — How the Pipeline Works](#1-overview--how-the-pipeline-works)
    10→2. [Getting Started — Adding Opportunities](#2-getting-started--adding-opportunities)
    11→3. [Understanding Stages](#3-understanding-stages)
    12→4. [The Actions Tab — Your Daily To-Do List](#4-the-actions-tab--your-daily-to-do-list)
    13→5. [Cold DM Outreach & The Bump System](#5-cold-dm-outreach--the-bump-system)
    14→6. [Moving Through the Pipeline](#6-moving-through-the-pipeline)
    15→7. [The Opportunity Detail Panel](#7-the-opportunity-detail-panel)
    16→8. [Pipeline View — Table & Kanban](#8-pipeline-view--table--kanban)
    17→9. [Overall Tab — Your Big Picture View](#9-overall-tab--your-big-picture-view)
    18→10. [Orbit — Managing Shelved Deals](#10-orbit--managing-shelved-deals)
    19→11. [Templates — Quick Message Copy](#11-templates--quick-message-copy)
    20→12. [Dashboard & Metrics](#12-dashboard--metrics)
    21→13. [Tips & Best Practices](#13-tips--best-practices)
    22→
    23→---
    24→
    25→## 1. Overview — How the Pipeline Works
    26→
    27→The Sales Pipeline tracks every potential deal from first contact to closed-won. Each opportunity flows through **stages**, and the system tells you exactly what to do next via **actions**.
    28→
    29→### The Stage Flow
    30→
    31→```
    32→Cold DM → Warm → TG Intro → Booked → Discovery Done → Proposal Call → Contract → Closed Won
    33→                                                                          ↓
    34→                                                                        Orbit (shelved)
    35→                                                                          ↓
    36→                                                                      Closed Lost
    37→```
    38→
    39→At the top of the page, you'll see **alert cards** that highlight anything needing immediate attention:
    40→
    41→| Card | What It Means |
    42→|------|--------------|
    43→| **Booking Needed** | Deals past discovery with no future meeting set (BAMFAM violation) |
    44→| **Overdue** | A meeting happened but you haven't logged the outcome |
    45→| **Stale (7d+)** | Deals with no contact in 7+ days |
    46→| **At Risk** | Closing-stage deals with a low temperature score |
    47→| **Meetings** | How many meetings you have today / this week |
    48→
    49→---
    50→
    51→## 2. Getting Started — Adding Opportunities
    52→
    53→Click the **"+ New"** button at the top right to create an opportunity.
    54→
    55→### Required Fields
    56→- **Name** — The company or contact name
    57→
    58→### Recommended Fields
    59→- **POC Platform & Handle** — Where you first reached out (Twitter, Instagram, Telegram, etc.) and their handle
    60→- **Owner** — Who on the team owns this deal
    61→- **Source** — How you found them:
    62→  - *Cold Outreach* — You reached out first (affiliate field is hidden for this source)
    63→  - *Referral* — Someone introduced you (a "Referrer" field appears)
    64→  - *Inbound, Event, Twitter, LinkedIn, Telegram, Website*
    65→
    66→### Affiliate (for non-cold-outreach sources)
    67→If the deal came through an affiliate or partner, use the searchable dropdown to select one. If the affiliate doesn't exist yet, just type their name and click **"Add [name] as new affiliate"** — it's created instantly.
    68→
    69→### Notes
    70→Add any context that will help you or your team understand the deal.
    71→
    72→---
    73→
    74→## 3. Understanding Stages
    75→
    76→Each stage represents where the deal currently sits in your sales process:
    77→
    78→| Stage | What's Happening |
    79→|-------|-----------------|
    80→| **Cold DM** | You've identified a lead and are reaching out via DM |
    81→| **Warm** | They've responded to your outreach |
    82→| **TG Intro** | You've connected on Telegram |
    83→| **Booked** | A discovery call is scheduled |
    84→| **Discovery Done** | The discovery call happened, you understand their needs |
    85→| **Proposal Call** | Time to present your proposal on a call |
    86→| **Contract** | Proposal accepted, chasing the signature |
    87→| **Closed Won** | Deal signed! |
    88→| **Orbit** | Deal shelved — not the right time, but may revisit |
    89→| **Closed Lost** | Deal didn't work out |
    90→| **Nurture** | Long-term relationship, periodic check-ins |
    91→
    92→### Color Coding
    93→Each stage has its own color throughout the app — in badges, kanban columns, and table headers — so you can quickly identify where deals are at a glance.
    94→
    95→---
    96→
    97→## 4. The Actions Tab — Your Daily To-Do List
    98→
    99→The **Actions tab** is the heart of the pipeline. It scans all your opportunities and tells you exactly what needs to happen next, ranked by priority.
   100→
   101→### Priority Levels
   102→
   103→| Icon | Priority | Meaning |
   104→|------|----------|---------|
   105→| Red triangle | **Urgent** | Do this now — overdue meetings, BAMFAM violations |
   106→| Amber lightning | **High** | Should do today — bumps due, follow-ups needed |
   107→| Blue clock | **Medium** | Can wait a bit — periodic check-ins |
   108→| Gray clock | **Low/Wait** | No action needed yet — meeting is upcoming, cooling period |
   109→
   110→### How Actions Work
   111→
   112→Each opportunity gets **one primary action** based on its stage and status. For example:
   113→
   114→- A cold DM with no bumps → **"Send First DM"**
   115→- A cold DM bumped 4 days ago → **"Bump #2"**
   116→- A warm/interested deal → **"Get TG Handle"**
   117→- A booked deal with a past meeting → **"Discovery Done"**
   118→- A proposal_call deal → **"Send Proposal"**
   119→
   120→### Quick Actions & Alternatives
   121→
   122→Next to the primary action button, you'll often see:
   123→- **Quick action** (green button) — A common shortcut. For example, "Replied!" instantly moves a cold DM to warm.
   124→- **Dropdown arrow** — More options like "Orbit", "Lost", or stage-specific alternatives.
   125→
   126→### Filtering Actions
   127→
   128→Use the tabs at the top to focus:
   129→- **All** — Everything actionable
   130→- **Outreach** — Cold DM, Warm, TG Intro, Booked stages
   131→- **Closing** — Discovery Done, Proposal Call, Contract stages
   132→- **Orbit** — Deals in orbit that may need attention
   133→- **Waiting** — Deals in a cooling period (bump cooldowns, nurturing)
   134→
   135→You can also filter by:
   136→- **My Actions** — Only your deals
   137→- **All Actions** — Everyone's deals
   138→
   139→### Sorting
   140→
   141→Sort your actions by Priority (default), Stage, Temperature, Deal Value, Name, Newest, or Oldest. Your sort preference is saved automatically.
   142→
   143→---
   144→
   145→## 5. Cold DM Outreach & The Bump System
   146→
   147→The **Outreach tab** is dedicated to your cold DM pipeline with advanced filtering, pagination, and bulk operations.
   148→
   149→### The Bump Lifecycle
   150→
   151→Bumps track how many times you've reached out to a lead:
   152→
   153→1. **Bump #0** → Action: "Send First DM"
   154→2. **After sending** → bump_number becomes 1, 3-day cooldown starts
   155→3. **During cooldown** → Action: "Wait Xd" (shows days remaining)
   156→4. **After 3 days** → Action: "Bump #2"
   157→5. **Repeat** up to 4 bumps
   158→6. **After 4 bumps with no reply** → Action: "Review → Orbit"
   159→
   160→> **Important:** During the "Wait" period, you can still mark a lead as "Replied!" if they respond — the action stays visible with a quick "Replied!" button.
   161→
   162→### If They Reply
   163→
   164→At any point, if a lead replies, click **"Replied!"** to move them to the **Warm** stage.
   165→
   166→### Outreach Filters
   167→
   168→- **Owner tabs** — Filter by team member or view all
   169→- **Search** — Find opportunities by name
   170→- **Path** — Closer (Path A) or SDR (Path B)
   171→- **Bucket** — A, B, or C priority
   172→- **Bump Status** — No bumps, 1-2 bumps, 3+ bumps
   173→
   174→### Bulk Operations
   175→
   176→Select multiple opportunities using the checkboxes, then:
   177→- **Bump All** — Record a bump for all selected
   178→- **Move to Warm** — Advance all to warm (they replied!)
   179→- **Delete** — Remove selected opportunities
   180→
   181→---
   182→
   183→## 6. Moving Through the Pipeline
   184→
   185→As deals progress, different prompts and actions guide you:
   186→
   187→### Warm → TG Intro: "Got TG!"
   188→
   189→When you click "Got TG!" (either as a quick action or from the dropdown), a dialog asks you to **enter their Telegram handle**. This saves the handle and moves the deal forward.
   190→
   191→### Booked → Discovery Done: Bucket Assignment
   192→
   193→After marking a deal as "Discovery Done" (the discovery call happened), you're prompted to **assign a bucket**:
   194→
   195→| Bucket | Meaning |
   196→|--------|---------|
   197→| **A — Hot** | High intent, likely to close |
   198→| **B — Warm** | Interested but needs nurturing |
   199→| **C — Low** | Lower priority, longer timeline |
   200→
   201→This helps prioritize your pipeline and shows up in the temperature score calculation.
   202→
   203→### Proposal Call → Send Proposal → Contract
   204→
   205→The proposal call stage has a two-step flow:
   206→1. **"Send Proposal"** — Click this after the call to mark the proposal as sent. An activity log popup lets you record details.
   207→2. **"To Contract"** — After the proposal is sent, this becomes the next action to move the deal into the contract stage.
   208→
   209→### Contract → Closed Won: "Chase Signature"
   210→
   211→In the contract stage, "Chase Signature" is your primary action (records a follow-up activity). Use the **"Signed!"** quick action when the deal closes.
   212→
   213→Need to schedule another call? Use **"Schedule Call"** from the dropdown.
   214→
   215→### Moving to Orbit
   216→
   217→When a deal stalls, you can move it to **Orbit** from any stage. You'll be asked to select a reason:
   218→- No Response
   219→- Bad Timing
   220→- No Budget
   221→- Went with Competitor
   222→- Other
   223→
   224→Deals in orbit for 90+ days will surface a "Resurrect" action to re-engage them.
   225→
   226→### Closing a Deal as Lost
   227→
   228→Moving to "Closed Lost" prompts you for an optional reason. This helps track why deals don't close.
   229→
   230→---
   231→
   232→## 7. The Opportunity Detail Panel
   233→
   234→Click any opportunity name to open the **slide-over panel** on the right side. This is your command center for a single deal.
   235→
   236→### What You'll See
   237→
   238→- **Header** — Name, stage badge, bucket, path, deal value
   239→- **Details card** — Temperature score, owner, POC info, TG handle, source, next meeting, last contacted
   240→- **Notes** — Any context about the deal
   241→
   242→### Editing
   243→
   244→Click the **pencil icon** to enter edit mode. You can update:
   245→- Name, POC, owner, source, stage
   246→- Deal value, currency
   247→- TG handle, meeting date/time
   248→- Notes and all other fields
   249→
   250→### Bump Controls (Cold DM only)
   251→
   252→A visual progress tracker shows bumps 1-4 as dots. Use the **+** and **-** buttons to manually adjust the bump count.
   253→
   254→### Quick Actions
   255→
   256→- **Move to Orbit** — Orange button at the bottom
   257→- **Delete** — Red button (use carefully!)
   258→- **Move to Stage** — Dropdown to manually move to any stage
   259→
   260→### Activity Timeline
   261→
   262→The bottom of the panel shows a chronological timeline of all activities:
   263→- Notes, calls, messages, meetings, proposals, bumps
   264→- Each has a type icon, title, description, outcome, and next steps
   265→
   266→**To add an activity**, fill in the form at the top of the timeline section:
   267→1. Select the type (Note, Call, Message, Meeting, Proposal)
   268→2. Add a title and optional details
   269→3. Set outcome, next step, and dates as needed
   270→4. Click "Add"
   271→
   272→---
   273→
   274→## 8. Pipeline View — Table & Kanban
   275→
   276→The **Pipeline tab** shows all active opportunities (non-orbit, non-closed) in two views:
   277→
   278→### Table View (Default)
   279→
   280→- Opportunities grouped by stage with collapsible headers
   281→- Each header shows the stage name, count, and total deal value
   282→- **Drag and drop** rows to reorder within a stage (use the grip handle on the left)
   283→- **Click a name or deal value** to edit it inline
   284→- **Click a row** to open the detail panel
   285→
   286→### Kanban View
   287→
   288→- Vertical columns for each stage
   289→- **Drag cards** between columns to change stages
   290→- Cards show key info: name, value, bucket, temperature, bump progress
   291→- Collapsible columns with chevrons
   292→- Drop zones for "Orbit" and "Lost" on the sides
   293→
   294→### Path Filter
   295→
   296→Use the dropdown to filter by:
   297→- **All Paths** — See everything
   298→- **Path A (Closer)** — Direct closer pipeline
   299→- **Path B (SDR)** — SDR-led pipeline
   300→
   301→---
   302→
   303→## 9. Overall Tab — Your Big Picture View
   304→
   305→The **Overall tab** gives you a consolidated view with three collapsible sections:
   306→
   307→1. **Outreach** (blue) — Your full cold DM pipeline
   308→2. **Pipeline** (green) — All active deals in table view
   309→3. **Orbit** (amber) — Shelved deals
   310→
   311→The tab badge shows the **total count of active opportunities** (excluding closed won and closed lost).
   312→
   313→Click any section header to expand/collapse it. This is great for a quick daily review without switching between tabs.
   314→
   315→---
   316→
   317→## 10. Orbit — Managing Shelved Deals
   318→
   319→The **Orbit tab** groups shelved deals by their orbit reason (No Response, Bad Timing, No Budget, etc.).
   320→
   321→Each group shows:
   322→- The reason label and count
   323→- A table with deal details, time in orbit, and last contacted date
   324→
   325→### What Happens Over Time
   326→
   327→- Deals in orbit for **90+ days** get a "Resurrect" action in the Actions tab
   328→- You can manually resurrect a deal from the orbit tab's menu → moves it back to Cold DM
   329→- Or mark it as Lost if it's truly dead
   330→
   331→---
   332→
   333→## 11. Templates — Quick Message Copy
   334→
   335→The **Templates tab** stores reusable DM and message templates for each pipeline stage.
   336→
   337→### Browsing Templates
   338→
   339→Use the **stage filter pills** at the top to filter:
   340→- All, Cold DM, Warm, TG Intro, Booked, Discovery Done, Proposal Call, Contract, Bumps
   341→
   342→Each template card shows:
   343→- Name and stage/sub-type badges
   344→- Content preview
   345→- Variable placeholders (e.g., `[KOL_NAME]`, `[PROJECT_NAME]`)
   346→
   347→### Using a Template
   348→
   349→Click the **copy icon** on any template card to copy the content to your clipboard. Then paste it into your DM and replace the placeholders.
   350→
   351→### Creating a Template
   352→
   353→1. Click **"+ New Template"**
   354→2. Fill in:
   355→   - **Name** — A descriptive label (e.g., "Cold DM — Crypto Project Intro")
   356→   - **Stage** — Which stage this template is for
   357→   - **Sub-type** — For bumps: Bump 1, Bump 2, Bump 3. For others: General, Initial, Follow-up
   358→   - **Content** — Your message. Use `[KOL_NAME]`, `[PROJECT_NAME]`, etc. as placeholders
   359→3. Click **"Create Template"**
   360→
   361→### Editing & Deleting
   362→
   363→- **Edit** — Click the pencil icon on a template card
   364→- **Delete** — Click the trash icon (immediate, no confirmation)
   365→
   366→### Pre-loaded Templates
   367→
   368→The system comes with default templates:
   369→- Cold DM — Initial Outreach
   370→- Bump 1, 2, 3 — Escalating follow-ups
   371→- Warm — Reply follow-up
   372→- TG Intro — First Telegram message
   373→
   374→---
   375→
   376→## 12. Dashboard & Metrics
   377→
   378→Click the **"Sales Dashboard"** header at the top of the page to expand detailed analytics.
   379→
   380→### Pipeline Health
   381→
   382→| Metric | What It Shows |
   383→|--------|--------------|
   384→| Pipeline Value | Total deal value of all active opportunities |
   385→| Weighted Pipeline | Deal values weighted by stage probability (Cold DM = 5%, Contract = 90%) |
   386→| Active Deals | Count of non-closed/non-orbit deals |
   387→| Revenue | Total value of closed-won deals |
   388→| Deals Won | Number of closed-won deals |
   389→| In Orbit | Number of shelved deals |
   390→
   391→### Conversion Funnel
   392→
   393→A visual bar chart showing how many deals pass through each stage — from DMs sent all the way to closed won. Helps identify where deals drop off.
   394→
   395→### Key Rates
   396→
   397→- **Response Rate** — % of cold DMs that got a reply
   398→- **Close Rate** — Won / (Won + Lost)
   399→- **Avg Deal Size** — Mean value of closed-won deals
   400→- **Avg Close Time** — Days from created to closed
   401→- **Qualified %** — Bucket A + B as % of total
   402→- **Bucket A %** — Top-tier deals as % of total
   403→
   404→### Bottleneck Analysis
   405→
   406→Shows you:
   407→- **Biggest Drop-off** — The stage where you lose the most deals
   408→- **Slowest Stage** — Where deals sit the longest
   409→- A full conversion table with stage-by-stage breakdown
   410→
   411→### Temperature Score
   412→
   413→Each opportunity has an auto-calculated temperature (0-100) based on:
   414→- Bucket assignment (A = 40 base, B = 25, C = 10)
   415→- How recently you've been in contact
   416→- Number of logged activities
   417→- Whether a meeting is booked
   418→- Stage progression bonuses
   419→- Penalties for stale deals, bump exhaustion, or silent warm leads
   420→
   421→Use the **"Recalc Scores"** button to refresh all scores.
   422→
   423→---
   424→
   425→## 13. Tips & Best Practices
   426→
   427→### Daily Workflow
   428→1. Open the **Actions tab** — this is your to-do list
   429→2. Work through urgent items first (red), then high (amber)
   430→3. Check the **alert cards** at the top for anything critical
   431→4. Log activities as you go — the system tracks everything
   432→
   433→### BAMFAM Rule
   434→**B**ook **A** **M**eeting **F**rom **A** **M**eeting. After any call past discovery, always schedule the next meeting before hanging up. The system enforces this — deals without a future meeting get flagged.
   435→
   436→### Keep It Moving
   437→- Don't let deals sit in a stage for too long without action
   438→- The "Stale 7d+" alert catches deals you might have forgotten
   439→- Temperature scores drop when deals go cold — use them as a health check
   440→
   441→### Use Templates
   442→Don't write every DM from scratch. Copy a template, personalize the placeholders, and send. Consistency + speed.
   443→
   444→### Activity Logging
   445→Every time you interact with a lead (DM, call, meeting, proposal), log it. This builds a timeline that helps your whole team understand where things stand — and it improves your temperature scores.
   446→
   447→### When to Orbit
   448→If a lead goes silent after 3-4 bumps, or if timing/budget isn't right, move them to Orbit rather than keeping them clogging your pipeline. They'll surface again in 90 days via the "Resurrect" action.
   449→
   450→---
   451→
   452→*Happy selling! If you have questions, reach out to your team lead or check the in-app guidance hints that appear with each action.*
   453→
```
</details></tool-use>
