 `./app/api/telegram/webhook/route.ts`

```text
     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next/server';
     2→import { createClient } from '@supabase/supabase-js';
     3→
     4→export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic';
     5→
     6→// Use service role for webhook (no user auth context)
     7→const supabaseAdmin = createClient(
     8→  process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL!,
     9→  process.env.SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY!
    10→);
    11→
    12→// Telegram webhook secret for verification (optional but recommended)
    13→const WEBHOOK_SECRET = process.env.TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET;
    14→
    15→// Content thread in HH Operations for KOL social links
    16→const CONTENT_THREAD_CHAT_ID = '-1002636253963';
    17→const CONTENT_THREAD_ID = 4280;
    18→
    19→/**
    20→ * Telegram Webhook Endpoint
    21→ * POST /api/telegram/webhook
    22→ *
    23→ * Receives updates from Telegram when messages are sent in group chats.
    24→ * Updates the corresponding CRM opportunity's last_message_at field.
    25→ */
    26→export async function POST(request: NextRequest) {
    27→  try {
    28→    // Optional: Verify webhook secret token
    29→    const secretToken = request.headers.get('X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token');
    30→    if (WEBHOOK_SECRET && secretToken !== WEBHOOK_SECRET) {
    31→      console.error('[Telegram Webhook] Invalid secret token');
    32→      return NextResponse.json({ ok: false }, { status: 401 });
    33→    }
    34→
    35→    const update = await request.json();
    36→
    37→    // Log incoming update for debugging
    38→    console.log('[Telegram Webhook] Received update:', JSON.stringify(update, null, 2));
    39→
    40→    // Handle message updates
    41→    if (update.message) {
    42→      await handleMessage(update.message);
    43→    }
    44→
    45→    // Handle edited messages too
    46→    if (update.edited_message) {
    47→      // Don't update timestamp for edits, just acknowledge
    48→      console.log('[Telegram Webhook] Edited message ignored');
    49→    }
    50→
    51→    // Telegram expects a 200 OK response
    52→    return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });
    53→  } catch (error) {
    54→    console.error('[Telegram Webhook] Error processing update:', error);
    55→    // Still return 200 to prevent Telegram from retrying
    56→    return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });
    57→  }
    58→}
    59→
    60→/**
    61→ * Send a message to a Telegram chat
    62→ */
    63→async function sendTelegramMessage(chatId: string, text: string, parseMode: 'HTML' | 'Markdown' = 'HTML') {
    64→  const botToken = process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN;
    65→  if (!botToken) {
    66→    console.error('[Telegram Webhook] Bot token not configured');
    67→    return false;
    68→  }
    69→
    70→  try {
    71→    const response = await fetch(
    72→      `https://api.telegram.org/bot${botToken}/sendMessage`,
    73→      {
    74→        method: 'POST',
    75→        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
    76→        body: JSON.stringify({
    77→          chat_id: chatId,
    78→          text,
    79→          parse_mode: parseMode
    80→        })
    81→      }
    82→    );
    83→
    84→    if (!response.ok) {
    85→      const error = await response.json();
    86→      console.error('[Telegram Webhook] Send message error:', error);
    87→      return false;
    88→    }
    89→
    90→    console.log('[Telegram Webhook] Sent reply to chat:', chatId);
    91→    return true;
    92→  } catch (error) {
    93→    console.error('[Telegram Webhook] Error sending message:', error);
    94→    return false;
    95→  }
    96→}
    97→
    98→/**
    99→ * Send a photo with caption to a Telegram chat
   100→ */
   101→async function sendTelegramPhoto(chatId: string, photoUrl: string, caption: string, parseMode: 'HTML' | 'Markdown' = 'HTML') {
   102→  const botToken = process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN;
   103→  if (!botToken) {
   104→    console.error('[Telegram Webhook] Bot token not configured');
   105→    return false;
   106→  }
   107→
   108→  try {
   109→    const response = await fetch(
   110→      `https://api.telegram.org/bot${botToken}/sendPhoto`,
   111→      {
   112→        method: 'POST',
   113→        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
   114→        body: JSON.stringify({
   115→          chat_id: chatId,
   116→          photo: photoUrl,
   117→          caption,
   118→          parse_mode: parseMode
   119→        })
   120→      }
   121→    );
   122→
   123→    if (!response.ok) {
   124→      const error = await response.json();
   125→      console.error('[Telegram Webhook] Send photo error:', error);
   126→      return false;
   127→    }
   128→
   129→    console.log('[Telegram Webhook] Sent photo to chat:', chatId);
   130→    return true;
   131→  } catch (error) {
   132→    console.error('[Telegram Webhook] Error sending photo:', error);
   133→    return false;
   134→  }
   135→}
   136→
   137→/**
   138→ * Send a message to a Telegram chat with thread support
   139→ */
   140→async function sendTelegramMessageToThread(chatId: string, threadId: number, text: string, parseMode: 'HTML' | 'Markdown' | '' = '') {
   141→  const botToken = process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN;
   142→  if (!botToken) {
   143→    console.error('[Telegram Webhook] Bot token not configured');
   144→    return false;
   145→  }
   146→
   147→  try {
   148→    const payload: any = {
   149→      chat_id: chatId,
   150→      message_thread_id: threadId,
   151→      text
   152→    };
   153→    if (parseMode) {
   154→      payload.parse_mode = parseMode;
   155→    }
   156→
   157→    const response = await fetch(
   158→      `https://api.telegram.org/bot${botToken}/sendMessage`,
   159→      {
   160→        method: 'POST',
   161→        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
   162→        body: JSON.stringify(payload)
   163→      }
   164→    );
   165→
   166→    if (!response.ok) {
   167→      const error = await response.json();
   168→      console.error('[Telegram Webhook] Send message to thread error:', error);
   169→      return false;
   170→    }
   171→
   172→    console.log('[Telegram Webhook] Sent message to thread:', { chatId, threadId });
   173→    return true;
   174→  } catch (error) {
   175→    console.error('[Telegram Webhook] Error sending message to thread:', error);
   176→    return false;
   177→  }
   178→}
   179→
   180→/**
   181→ * Detect and extract Telegram/Twitter links from text
   182→ */
   183→function extractSocialLinks(text: string): { telegram: string[]; twitter: string[] } {
   184→  const telegramRegex = /(https?:\/\/)?(t\.me|telegram\.me)\/[^\s]+/gi;
   185→  const twitterRegex = /(https?:\/\/)?(twitter\.com|x\.com)\/[^\s]+/gi;
   186→
   187→  const telegramMatches = text.match(telegramRegex) || [];
   188→  const twitterMatches = text.match(twitterRegex) || [];
   189→
   190→  return {
   191→    telegram: telegramMatches.map(url => url.startsWith('http') ? url : `https://${url}`),
   192→    twitter: twitterMatches.map(url => url.startsWith('http') ? url : `https://${url}`)
   193→  };
   194→}
   195→
   196→/**
   197→ * Forward social links from KOL chats to the Content thread
   198→ */
   199→async function forwardKolSocialLinks(
   200→  chatId: string,
   201→  chatTitle: string | undefined,
   202→  fromName: string,
   203→  messageText: string,
   204→  messageDate: Date
   205→) {
   206→  try {
   207→    // Check if this chat is linked to a KOL
   208→    const { data: chat, error } = await supabaseAdmin
   209→      .from('telegram_chats')
   210→      .select('master_kol_id, title, master_kols:master_kol_id(name)')
   211→      .eq('chat_id', chatId)
   212→      .single();
   213→
   214→    if (error || !chat || !chat.master_kol_id) {
   215→      // Not a KOL-linked chat, skip
   216→      return;
   217→    }
   218→
   219→    // Extract social links from the message
   220→    const links = extractSocialLinks(messageText);
   221→    const hasLinks = links.telegram.length > 0 || links.twitter.length > 0;
   222→
   223→    if (!hasLinks) {
   224→      return;
   225→    }
   226→
   227→    // Format the notification message
   228→    const kolName = (chat.master_kols as any)?.name || 'Unknown KOL';
   229→    const chatName = chat.title || chatTitle || 'Unknown Chat';
   230→    const dateStr = messageDate.toLocaleDateString('en-US', { month: 'short', day: 'numeric', year: 'numeric' });
   231→
   232→    let message = `🔗 New KOL Link Detected\n\n`;
   233→    message += `KOL: ${kolName}\n`;
   234→    message += `Chat: ${chatName}\n`;
   235→    message += `From: ${fromName}\n`;
   236→    message += `Date: ${dateStr}\n\n`;
   237→
   238→    if (links.telegram.length > 0) {
   239→      message += `📱 Telegram:\n`;
   240→      links.telegram.forEach(link => {
   241→        message += `${link}\n`;
   242→      });
   243→    }
   244→
   245→    if (links.twitter.length > 0) {
   246→      if (links.telegram.length > 0) message += `\n`;
   247→      message += `𝕏 Twitter/X:\n`;
   248→      links.twitter.forEach(link => {
   249→        message += `${link}\n`;
   250→      });
   251→    }
   252→
   253→    // Send to Content thread
   254→    await sendTelegramMessageToThread(CONTENT_THREAD_CHAT_ID, CONTENT_THREAD_ID, message);
   255→    console.log('[Telegram Webhook] Forwarded KOL social links to Content thread:', { kolName, links });
   256→  } catch (error) {
   257→    console.error('[Telegram Webhook] Error forwarding KOL social links:', error);
   258→    // Non-critical, don't throw
   259→  }
   260→}
   261→
   262→/**
   263→ * Handle bot commands - reads from database
   264→ */
   265→async function handleCommand(chatId: string, command: string, args: string[], message: any) {
   266→  // Remove leading slash and bot mention
   267→  const cmd = command.toLowerCase().replace(/^\//, '').replace('@holo_hive_bot', '');
   268→
   269→  // Built-in /test command - sends chat ID and thread ID to Andy Lee
   270→  if (cmd === 'test') {
   271→    const ANDY_LEE_TELEGRAM_ID = '6281931733';
   272→    const threadId = message.message_thread_id || null;
   273→    const chatType = message.chat?.type || 'unknown';
   274→    const chatTitle = message.chat?.title || 'Private Chat';
   275→    const fromUser = message.from?.first_name || message.from?.username || 'Unknown';
   276→
   277→    const response = `<b>🔧 /test Command Triggered</b>\n\n` +
   278→      `<b>Chat ID:</b> <code>${chatId}</code>\n` +
   279→      `<b>Thread ID:</b> <code>${threadId || 'N/A (not a forum topic)'}</code>\n` +
   280→      `<b>Chat Type:</b> ${chatType}\n` +
   281→      `<b>Chat Title:</b> ${chatTitle}\n` +
   282→      `<b>Triggered by:</b> ${fromUser}`;
   283→
   284→    // Send to Andy Lee's DM
   285→    await sendTelegramMessage(ANDY_LEE_TELEGRAM_ID, response);
   286→
   287→    // Send confirmation in the original chat
   288→    await sendTelegramMessage(chatId, '✅ Chat info sent to Andy Lee.');
   289→
   290→    console.log('[Telegram Webhook] Executed /test command:', { chatId, threadId, sentTo: 'Andy Lee' });
   291→    return;
   292→  }
   293→
   294→  try {
   295→    // Look up command in database
   296→    const { data: commandData, error } = await supabaseAdmin
   297→      .from('telegram_commands')
   298→      .select('response, image_url, team_only')
   299→      .eq('command', cmd)
   300→      .eq('is_active', true)
   301→      .single();
   302→
   303→    if (error || !commandData) {
   304→      console.log('[Telegram Webhook] Unknown command:', cmd);
   305→      return;
   306→    }
   307→
   308→    // Check if command is team-only
   309→    if (commandData.team_only) {
   310→      const fromUserId = message.from?.id?.toString();
   311→
   312→      if (!fromUserId) {
   313→        console.log('[Telegram Webhook] Team-only command rejected: no user ID');
   314→        await sendTelegramMessage(chatId, 'This command is only available to team members.');
   315→        return;
   316→      }
   317→
   318→      // Check if the user's telegram_id is in the users table (team member)
   319→      const { data: teamMember, error: teamError } = await supabaseAdmin
   320→        .from('users')
   321→        .select('id, name')
   322→        .eq('telegram_id', fromUserId)
   323→        .single();
   324→
   325→      if (teamError || !teamMember) {
   326→        console.log('[Telegram Webhook] Team-only command rejected: user not a team member', fromUserId);
   327→        await sendTelegramMessage(chatId, 'This command is only available to team members.');
   328→        return;
   329→      }
   330→
   331→      console.log('[Telegram Webhook] Team-only command authorized for:', teamMember.name);
   332→    }
   333→
   334→    // Send photo with caption if image_url exists, otherwise just send message
   335→    if (commandData.image_url) {
   336→      await sendTelegramPhoto(chatId, commandData.image_url, commandData.response);
   337→    } else {
   338→      await sendTelegramMessage(chatId, commandData.response);
   339→    }
   340→    console.log('[Telegram Webhook] Executed command:', cmd);
   341→  } catch (error) {
   342→    console.error('[Telegram Webhook] Error handling command:', error);
   343→  }
   344→}
   345→
   346→/**
   347→ * Handle incoming message from Telegram
   348→ */
   349→async function handleMessage(message: any) {
   350→  const chatId = message.chat?.id?.toString();
   351→  const chatTitle = message.chat?.title;
   352→  const chatType = message.chat?.type; // 'group', 'supergroup', 'private'
   353→  const messageId = message.message_id?.toString();
   354→  const fromId = message.from?.id?.toString();
   355→  const fromFirstName = message.from?.first_name;
   356→  const fromLastName = message.from?.last_name;
   357→  const fromUsername = message.from?.username;
   358→  const messageText = message.text || message.caption || '[Media]';
   359→  const messageDate = new Date(message.date * 1000); // Telegram sends Unix timestamp
   360→
   361→  if (!chatId) {
   362→    console.log('[Telegram Webhook] No chat ID in message');
   363→    return;
   364→  }
   365→
   366→  // Handle commands (messages starting with /)
   367→  if (messageText.startsWith('/')) {
   368→    const parts = messageText.split(' ');
   369→    const command = parts[0];
   370→    const args = parts.slice(1);
   371→    await handleCommand(chatId, command, args, message);
   372→  }
   373→
   374→  // Track all chat types (groups, supergroups, and private DMs)
   375→  // For DMs, use the user's name as the title
   376→  const title = chatType === 'private'
   377→    ? [fromFirstName, fromLastName].filter(Boolean).join(' ') || fromUsername || 'Unknown User'
   378→    : chatTitle;
   379→
   380→  // Track/update chat in telegram_chats table
   381→  await trackChat(chatId, title, chatType, messageDate);
   382→
   383→  // Store the message for chat identification
   384→  const fromName = [fromFirstName, fromLastName].filter(Boolean).join(' ') || 'Unknown';
   385→  await storeMessage(chatId, messageId, fromId, fromName, fromUsername, messageText, messageDate);
   386→
   387→  // Check for Telegram/X links in KOL chats and forward to Content thread
   388→  await forwardKolSocialLinks(chatId, chatTitle, fromName, messageText, messageDate);
   389→
   390→  // Check if this chat ID matches any opportunity's gc field
   391→  const { data: opportunities, error } = await supabaseAdmin
   392→    .from('crm_opportunities')
   393→    .select('id, name, gc')
   394→    .eq('gc', chatId);
   395→
   396→  if (error) {
   397→    console.error('[Telegram Webhook] Error querying opportunities:', error);
   398→    return;
   399→  }
   400→
   401→  // Check if the message is from our bot
   402→  const botToken = process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN;
   403→  const botId = botToken ? botToken.split(':')[0] : null;
   404→  const isFromBot = fromId === botId;
   405→
   406→  // Check if the sender is a team member (has telegram_id in users table)
   407→  let isTeamMember = false;
   408→  if (fromId && !isFromBot) {
   409→    const { data: teamMember } = await supabaseAdmin
   410→      .from('users')
   411→      .select('id')
   412→      .eq('telegram_id', fromId)
   413→      .single();
   414→    isTeamMember = !!teamMember;
   415→  }
   416→
   417→  // Determine which field to update:
   418→  // - Bot messages → last_reply_at
   419→  // - Team member messages → last_team_message_at
   420→  // - Lead/others messages → last_message_at
   421→  const getUpdateField = () => {
   422→    if (isFromBot) return 'last_reply_at';
   423→    if (isTeamMember) return 'last_team_message_at';
   424→    return 'last_message_at';
   425→  };
   426→
   427→  // Update all matching opportunities
   428→  if (opportunities && opportunities.length > 0) {
   429→    for (const opportunity of opportunities) {
   430→      const updateField = getUpdateField();
   431→      const senderType = isFromBot ? 'bot' : (isTeamMember ? 'team' : 'lead');
   432→
   433→      const { error: updateError } = await supabaseAdmin
   434→        .from('crm_opportunities')
   435→        .update({
   436→          [updateField]: messageDate.toISOString(),
   437→          updated_at: new Date().toISOString()
   438→        })
   439→        .eq('id', opportunity.id);
   440→
   441→      if (updateError) {
   442→        console.error('[Telegram Webhook] Error updating opportunity:', updateError);
   443→      } else {
   444→        console.log(`[Telegram Webhook] Updated ${updateField} for opportunity:`, {
   445→          id: opportunity.id,
   446→          name: opportunity.name,
   447→          chatId,
   448→          timestamp: messageDate.toISOString(),
   449→          senderType
   450→        });
   451→      }
   452→    }
   453→  }
   454→}
   455→
   456→/**
   457→ * Track/update chat info in telegram_chats table
   458→ */
   459→async function trackChat(chatId: string, title: string | null, chatType: string, messageDate: Date) {
   460→  try {
   461→    // Check if chat already exists
   462→    const { data: existingChat } = await supabaseAdmin
   463→      .from('telegram_chats')
   464→      .select('id, message_count')
   465→      .eq('chat_id', chatId)
   466→      .single();
   467→
   468→    if (existingChat) {
   469→      // Update existing chat
   470→      await supabaseAdmin
   471→        .from('telegram_chats')
   472→        .update({
   473→          title: title || undefined,
   474→          chat_type: chatType,
   475→          last_message_at: messageDate.toISOString(),
   476→          message_count: (existingChat.message_count || 0) + 1,
   477→          updated_at: new Date().toISOString()
   478→        })
   479→        .eq('id', existingChat.id);
   480→
   481→      console.log('[Telegram Webhook] Updated chat tracking:', { chatId, title });
   482→    } else {
   483→      // Insert new chat
   484→      await supabaseAdmin
   485→        .from('telegram_chats')
   486→        .insert({
   487→          chat_id: chatId,
   488→          title: title,
   489→          chat_type: chatType,
   490→          last_message_at: messageDate.toISOString(),
   491→          message_count: 1
   492→        });
   493→
   494→      console.log('[Telegram Webhook] New chat discovered:', { chatId, title });
   495→    }
   496→  } catch (error) {
   497→    console.error('[Telegram Webhook] Error tracking chat:', error);
   498→    // Don't throw - chat tracking is non-critical
   499→  }
   500→}
   501→
   502→/**
   503→ * Store message for chat identification
   504→ */
   505→async function storeMessage(
   506→  chatId: string,
   507→  messageId: string,
   508→  fromUserId: string | null,
   509→  fromUserName: string,
   510→  fromUsername: string | null,
   511→  text: string,
   512→  messageDate: Date
   513→) {
   514→  try {
   515→    // Truncate long messages
   516→    const truncatedText = text.length > 500 ? text.substring(0, 500) + '...' : text;
   517→
   518→    await supabaseAdmin
   519→      .from('telegram_messages')
   520→      .upsert({
   521→        chat_id: chatId,
   522→        message_id: messageId,
   523→        from_user_id: fromUserId,
   524→        from_user_name: fromUserName,
   525→        from_username: fromUsername,
   526→        text: truncatedText,
   527→        message_date: messageDate.toISOString()
   528→      }, {
   529→        onConflict: 'chat_id,message_id'
   530→      });
   531→
   532→    console.log('[Telegram Webhook] Stored message:', { chatId, messageId, from: fromUserName });
   533→  } catch (error) {
   534→    console.error('[Telegram Webhook] Error storing message:', error);
   535→    // Don't throw - message storage is non-critical
   536→  }
   537→}
   538→
   539→/**
   540→ * GET endpoint for webhook verification
   541→ * Telegram doesn't use GET, but useful for manual testing
   542→ */
   543→export async function GET() {
   544→  return NextResponse.json({
   545→    status: 'Telegram webhook endpoint active',
   546→    info: 'This endpoint receives POST requests from Telegram'
   547→  });
   548→}
   549→
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
     1→/**
     2→ * Telegram Bot Service
     3→ * Sends notifications to Telegram chat rooms
     4→ */
     5→
     6→interface TelegramMessage {
     7→  chat_id: string;
     8→  text: string;
     9→  parse_mode?: 'HTML' | 'Markdown' | 'MarkdownV2';
    10→  disable_web_page_preview?: boolean;
    11→  message_thread_id?: number;
    12→}
    13→
    14→export class TelegramService {
    15→  // Read env vars at runtime via getters to avoid serverless caching issues
    16→  private static get botToken() {
    17→    return process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN;
    18→  }
    19→
    20→  private static get chatId() {
    21→    return process.env.TELEGRAM_TERMINAL_CHAT_ID;
    22→  }
    23→
    24→  private static get threadId() {
    25→    return process.env.TELEGRAM_TERMINAL_THREAD_ID;
    26→  }
    27→
    28→  /**
    29→   * Send a message to the configured Telegram chat
    30→   */
    31→  static async sendMessage(text: string, parseMode: 'HTML' | 'Markdown' = 'HTML'): Promise<boolean> {
    32→    const botToken = this.botToken;
    33→    const chatId = this.chatId;
    34→    const threadId = this.threadId;
    35→
    36→    if (!botToken || !chatId) {
    37→      console.error('[Telegram] Configuration missing:', {
    38→        hasToken: !!botToken,
    39→        hasChatId: !!chatId,
    40→        tokenPrefix: botToken ? botToken.substring(0, 10) + '...' : 'missing',
    41→        chatId: chatId || 'missing'
    42→      });
    43→      return false;
    44→    }
    45→
    46→    try {
    47→      const message: TelegramMessage = {
    48→        chat_id: chatId,
    49→        text,
    50→        parse_mode: parseMode,
    51→        disable_web_page_preview: false,
    52→      };
    53→
    54→      // Add thread ID if configured (for sending to specific topics in groups)
    55→      if (threadId) {
    56→        message.message_thread_id = parseInt(threadId);
    57→      }
    58→
    59→      console.log('[Telegram] Sending message:', {
    60→        chatId: chatId,
    61→        threadId: threadId,
    62→        messageLength: text.length,
    63→        parseMode
    64→      });
    65→
    66→      const response = await fetch(
    67→        `https://api.telegram.org/bot${botToken}/sendMessage`,
    68→        {
    69→          method: 'POST',
    70→          headers: {
    71→            'Content-Type': 'application/json',
    72→          },
    73→          body: JSON.stringify(message),
    74→        }
    75→      );
    76→
    77→      if (!response.ok) {
    78→        const error = await response.json();
    79→        console.error('[Telegram] API error:', {
    80→          status: response.status,
    81→          error
    82→        });
    83→        return false;
    84→      }
    85→
    86→      const result = await response.json();
    87→      console.log('[Telegram] Message sent successfully:', {
    88→        messageId: result.result?.message_id,
    89→        chatId: result.result?.chat?.id
    90→      });
    91→
    92→      return true;
    93→    } catch (error) {
    94→      console.error('[Telegram] Error sending message:', error);
    95→      return false;
    96→    }
    97→  }
    98→
    99→  /**
   100→   * Format and send form submission notification
   101→   */
   102→  static async sendFormSubmissionNotification(
   103→    formName: string,
   104→    formId: string,
   105→    submittedBy?: { name?: string; email?: string },
   106→    responseData?: Record<string, any>
   107→  ): Promise<boolean> {
   108→    // Construct the base URL
   109→    const baseUrl = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL
   110→      ? (process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL.startsWith('http')
   111→          ? process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL
   112→          : `https://${process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL}`)
   113→      : (process.env.VERCEL_URL ? `https://${process.env.VERCEL_URL}` : 'http://localhost:3000');
   114→
   115→    const formUrl = `${baseUrl}/forms/${formId}`;
   116→
   117→    const message = `${formName} Form has been submitted.\n<a href="${formUrl}">View Form</a>`;
   118→
   119→    return this.sendMessage(message);
   120→  }
   121→
   122→  /**
   123→   * Send a test message to verify configuration
   124→   */
   125→  static async sendTestMessage(): Promise<boolean> {
   126→    return this.sendMessage(
   127→      '<b>✅ Telegram Bot Connected!</b>\n\nYour Holo Hive form submission notifications are now active.'
   128→    );
   129→  }
   130→
   131→  // ============================================
   132→  // WEBHOOK MANAGEMENT
   133→  // ============================================
   134→
   135→  /**
   136→   * Register the webhook URL with Telegram
   137→   * Call this once after deploying to set up message tracking
   138→   */
   139→  static async registerWebhook(webhookUrl: string): Promise<{ success: boolean; error?: string }> {
   140→    if (!this.botToken) {
   141→      return { success: false, error: 'TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN not configured' };
   142→    }
   143→
   144→    try {
   145→      const params: any = {
   146→        url: webhookUrl,
   147→        allowed_updates: ['message', 'edited_message'],
   148→        drop_pending_updates: true // Don't process old messages
   149→      };
   150→
   151→      // Add secret token for webhook verification if configured
   152→      const webhookSecret = process.env.TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET;
   153→      if (webhookSecret) {
   154→        params.secret_token = webhookSecret;
   155→      }
   156→
   157→      const response = await fetch(
   158→        `https://api.telegram.org/bot${this.botToken}/setWebhook`,
   159→        {
   160→          method: 'POST',
   161→          headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
   162→          body: JSON.stringify(params)
   163→        }
   164→      );
   165→
   166→      const result = await response.json();
   167→
   168→      if (!result.ok) {
   169→        console.error('[Telegram] Webhook registration failed:', result);
   170→        return { success: false, error: result.description };
   171→      }
   172→
   173→      console.log('[Telegram] Webhook registered successfully:', webhookUrl);
   174→      return { success: true };
   175→    } catch (error) {
   176→      console.error('[Telegram] Error registering webhook:', error);
   177→      return { success: false, error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Unknown error' };
   178→    }
   179→  }
   180→
   181→  /**
   182→   * Remove the webhook (stop receiving updates)
   183→   */
   184→  static async deleteWebhook(): Promise<{ success: boolean; error?: string }> {
   185→    if (!this.botToken) {
   186→      return { success: false, error: 'TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN not configured' };
   187→    }
   188→
   189→    try {
   190→      const response = await fetch(
   191→        `https://api.telegram.org/bot${this.botToken}/deleteWebhook`,
   192→        { method: 'POST' }
   193→      );
   194→
   195→      const result = await response.json();
   196→
   197→      if (!result.ok) {
   198→        return { success: false, error: result.description };
   199→      }
   200→
   201→      console.log('[Telegram] Webhook deleted successfully');
   202→      return { success: true };
   203→    } catch (error) {
   204→      console.error('[Telegram] Error deleting webhook:', error);
   205→      return { success: false, error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Unknown error' };
   206→    }
   207→  }
   208→
   209→  /**
   210→   * Get current webhook info
   211→   */
   212→  static async getWebhookInfo(): Promise<any> {
   213→    if (!this.botToken) {
   214→      return { error: 'TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN not configured' };
   215→    }
   216→
   217→    try {
   218→      const response = await fetch(
   219→        `https://api.telegram.org/bot${this.botToken}/getWebhookInfo`
   220→      );
   221→
   222→      return await response.json();
   223→    } catch (error) {
   224→      console.error('[Telegram] Error getting webhook info:', error);
   225→      return { error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Unknown error' };
   226→    }
   227→  }
   228→
   229→  /**
   230→   * Send a message to a specific chat (for CRM integration)
   231→   */
   232→  static async sendToChat(
   233→    chatId: string,
   234→    text: string,
   235→    parseMode: 'HTML' | 'Markdown' = 'HTML'
   236→  ): Promise<boolean> {
   237→    if (!this.botToken) {
   238→      console.error('[Telegram] Bot token not configured');
   239→      return false;
   240→    }
   241→
   242→    try {
   243→      const response = await fetch(
   244→        `https://api.telegram.org/bot${this.botToken}/sendMessage`,
   245→        {
   246→          method: 'POST',
   247→          headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
   248→          body: JSON.stringify({
   249→            chat_id: chatId,
   250→            text,
   251→            parse_mode: parseMode
   252→          })
   253→        }
   254→      );
   255→
   256→      if (!response.ok) {
   257→        const error = await response.json();
   258→        console.error('[Telegram] Send to chat error:', error);
   259→        return false;
   260→      }
   261→
   262→      return true;
   263→    } catch (error) {
   264→      console.error('[Telegram] Error sending to chat:', error);
   265→      return false;
   266→    }
   267→  }
   268→}
   269→

     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next/server';
     2→import { createClient } from '@supabase/supabase-js';
     3→import { Database } from '@/lib/database.types';
     4→import { TelegramService } from '@/lib/telegramService';
     5→
     6→export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic';
     7→
     8→export async function POST(request: NextRequest) {
     9→  try {
    10→    const body = await request.json();
    11→    const {
    12→      name,
    13→      account_type,
    14→      scope,
    15→      deal_value,
    16→      currency,
    17→      source,
    18→      referrer,
    19→      gc,
    20→      notes,
    21→      contact_name,
    22→      contact_email,
    23→      contact_telegram
    24→    } = body;
    25→
    26→    // Validate required fields
    27→    if (!name || !name.trim()) {
    28→      return NextResponse.json(
    29→        { error: 'Lead name is required' },
    30→        { status: 400 }
    31→      );
    32→    }
    33→
    34→    if (!contact_name || !contact_name.trim()) {
    35→      return NextResponse.json(
    36→        { error: 'Contact name is required' },
    37→        { status: 400 }
    38→      );
    39→    }
    40→
    41→    // Validate at least one contact method
    42→    if ((!contact_email || !contact_email.trim()) && (!contact_telegram || !contact_telegram.trim())) {
    43→      return NextResponse.json(
    44→        { error: 'At least one contact method (email or Telegram) is required' },
    45→        { status: 400 }
    46→      );
    47→    }
    48→
    49→    // Validate email format if provided
    50→    if (contact_email && contact_email.trim()) {
    51→      const emailRegex = /^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/;
    52→      if (!emailRegex.test(contact_email.trim())) {
    53→        return NextResponse.json(
    54→          { error: 'Invalid email format' },
    55→          { status: 400 }
    56→        );
    57→      }
    58→    }
    59→
    60→    // Validate deal value if provided
    61→    if (deal_value && isNaN(parseFloat(deal_value))) {
    62→      return NextResponse.json(
    63→        { error: 'Deal value must be a valid number' },
    64→        { status: 400 }
    65→      );
    66→    }
    67→
    68→    // Create server-side Supabase client with service role key to bypass RLS
    69→    const supabaseUrl = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL;
    70→    const supabaseServiceKey = process.env.SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY;
    71→
    72→    if (!supabaseUrl || !supabaseServiceKey) {
    73→      console.error('Missing Supabase environment variables');
    74→      return NextResponse.json(
    75→        { error: 'Server configuration error' },
    76→        { status: 500 }
    77→      );
    78→    }
    79→
    80→    const supabaseAdmin = createClient<Database>(supabaseUrl, supabaseServiceKey, {
    81→      auth: {
    82→        autoRefreshToken: false,
    83→        persistSession: false
    84→      }
    85→    });
    86→
    87→    // Build notes with contact info
    88→    let combinedNotes = '';
    89→    if (contact_name || contact_email || contact_telegram) {
    90→      combinedNotes += `Contact: ${contact_name || 'N/A'}`;
    91→      if (contact_email) combinedNotes += ` | Email: ${contact_email}`;
    92→      if (contact_telegram) combinedNotes += ` | Telegram: ${contact_telegram}`;
    93→    }
    94→    if (notes?.trim()) {
    95→      combinedNotes += combinedNotes ? `\n\n${notes.trim()}` : notes.trim();
    96→    }
    97→
    98→    // Build scope string (join array into comma-separated string)
    99→    const scopeValue = Array.isArray(scope) && scope.length > 0 ? scope.join(', ') : null;
   100→
   101→    // Insert opportunity
   102→    const { data: opportunity, error } = await supabaseAdmin
   103→      .from('crm_opportunities')
   104→      .insert([{
   105→        name: name.trim(),
   106→        account_type: account_type || null,
   107→        scope: scopeValue,
   108→        deal_value: deal_value ? parseFloat(deal_value) : null,
   109→        currency: currency || 'USD',
   110→        source: source || null,
   111→        referrer: referrer?.trim() || null,
   112→        gc: gc?.trim() || null,
   113→        notes: combinedNotes || null,
   114→        stage: 'new'
   115→      }])
   116→      .select()
   117→      .single();
   118→
   119→    if (error) {
   120→      console.error('Error inserting opportunity:', error);
   121→      return NextResponse.json(
   122→        { error: 'Failed to save lead' },
   123→        { status: 500 }
   124→      );
   125→    }
   126→
   127→    // Send Telegram notification
   128→    const baseUrl = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL || 'https://portal.holohive.agency';
   129→    const pipelineUrl = `${baseUrl}/crm/pipeline`;
   130→    const message = `<b>New Lead Submitted</b>\n\n` +
   131→      `<b>Name:</b> ${name.trim()}\n` +
   132→      `<b>Type:</b> ${account_type || 'N/A'}\n` +
   133→      `<b>Scope:</b> ${Array.isArray(scope) ? scope.join(', ') : scope || 'N/A'}\n` +
   134→      `<b>Value:</b> ${deal_value ? `${currency || 'USD'} ${deal_value}` : 'N/A'}\n` +
   135→      `<b>Contact:</b> ${contact_name?.trim() || 'N/A'}\n` +
   136→      `<b>Source:</b> ${source || 'N/A'}\n\n` +
   137→      `<a href="${pipelineUrl}">View Pipeline</a>`;
   138→
   139→    try {
   140→      await TelegramService.sendMessage(message);
   141→    } catch (err) {
   142→      console.error('[Leads Submit] Telegram notification error:', err);
   143→    }
   144→
   145→    return NextResponse.json({
   146→      success: true,
   147→      opportunity
   148→    });
   149→
   150→  } catch (error) {
   151→    console.error('Error in lead submission:', error);
   152→    return NextResponse.json(
   153→      { error: 'Internal server error' },
   154→      { status: 500 }
   155→    );
   156→  }
   157→}
   158→
```

     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next/server';
     2→import { createClient } from '@supabase/supabase-js';
     3→import { Database } from '@/lib/database.types';
     4→import { TelegramService } from '@/lib/telegramService';
     5→
     6→export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic';
     7→
     8→export async function POST(request: NextRequest) {
     9→  try {
    10→    const body = await request.json();
    11→    const {
    12→      name,
    13→      affiliation,
    14→      category,
    15→      commission_model,
    16→      poc_name,
    17→      poc_email,
    18→      poc_telegram,
    19→      terms_of_interest,
    20→      notes
    21→    } = body;
    22→
    23→    // Validate required fields
    24→    if (!name || !name.trim()) {
    25→      return NextResponse.json(
    26→        { error: 'Partner name is required' },
    27→        { status: 400 }
    28→      );
    29→    }
    30→
    31→    if (!poc_name || !poc_name.trim()) {
    32→      return NextResponse.json(
    33→        { error: 'Point of contact name is required' },
    34→        { status: 400 }
    35→      );
    36→    }
    37→
    38→    // Validate at least one contact method
    39→    if ((!poc_email || !poc_email.trim()) && (!poc_telegram || !poc_telegram.trim())) {
    40→      return NextResponse.json(
    41→        { error: 'At least one contact method (email or Telegram) is required' },
    42→        { status: 400 }
    43→      );
    44→    }
    45→
    46→    // Validate email format if provided
    47→    if (poc_email && poc_email.trim()) {
    48→      const emailRegex = /^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/;
    49→      if (!emailRegex.test(poc_email.trim())) {
    50→        return NextResponse.json(
    51→          { error: 'Invalid email format' },
    52→          { status: 400 }
    53→        );
    54→      }
    55→    }
    56→
    57→    // Create server-side Supabase client with service role key to bypass RLS
    58→    const supabaseUrl = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL;
    59→    const supabaseServiceKey = process.env.SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY;
    60→
    61→    if (!supabaseUrl || !supabaseServiceKey) {
    62→      console.error('Missing Supabase environment variables');
    63→      return NextResponse.json(
    64→        { error: 'Server configuration error' },
    65→        { status: 500 }
    66→      );
    67→    }
    68→
    69→    const supabaseAdmin = createClient<Database>(supabaseUrl, supabaseServiceKey, {
    70→      auth: {

    73→      }
    74→    });
    75→
    76→    // Insert affiliate/partner
    77→    const { data: affiliate, error } = await supabaseAdmin
    78→      .from('crm_affiliates')
    79→      .insert([{
    80→        name: name.trim(),
    81→        affiliation: affiliation?.trim() || null,
    82→        category: category || null,
    83→        commission_model: commission_model || null,
    84→        poc_name: poc_name?.trim() || null,
    85→        poc_email: poc_email?.trim() || null,
    86→        poc_telegram: poc_telegram?.trim() || null,
    87→        terms_of_interest: terms_of_interest?.trim() || null,
    88→        notes: notes?.trim() || null,
    89→        status: 'new'
    90→      }])
    91→      .select()
    92→      .single();
    93→
    94→    if (error) {
    95→      console.error('Error inserting affiliate:', error);
    96→      return NextResponse.json(
    97→        { error: 'Failed to save partner application' },
    98→        { status: 500 }
    99→      );
   100→    }
   101→
   102→    // Send Telegram notification
   103→    const baseUrl = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL || 'https://portal.holohive.agency';
   104→    const networkUrl = `${baseUrl}/crm/network`;
   105→    const message = `<b>New Partner Application</b>\n\n` +
   106→      `<b>Name:</b> ${name.trim()}\n` +
   107→      `<b>Affiliation:</b> ${affiliation?.trim() || 'N/A'}\n` +
   108→      `<b>Category:</b> ${category || 'N/A'}\n` +
   109→      `<b>POC:</b> ${poc_name?.trim() || 'N/A'}\n` +
   110→      `<b>Email:</b> ${poc_email?.trim() || 'N/A'}\n` +
   111→      `<b>Telegram:</b> ${poc_telegram?.trim() || 'N/A'}\n\n` +
   112→      `<a href="${networkUrl}">View Network</a>`;
   113→
   114→    try {
   115→      await TelegramService.sendMessage(message);
   116→    } catch (err) {
   117→      console.error('[Partners Submit] Telegram notification error:', err);
   118→    }
   119→
   120→    return NextResponse.json({
   121→      success: true,
   122→      affiliate
   123→    });
   124→
   125→  } catch (error) {
   126→    console.error('Error in partner submission:', error);
   127→    return NextResponse.json(
   128→      { error: 'Internal server error' },
   129→      { status: 500 }
   130→    );
   131→  }
   132→}
   133→
```
</details></tool-use>

 `./app/api/links/submit/route.ts`

```text
     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next/server';
     2→import { createClient } from '@supabase/supabase-js';
     3→import { Database } from '@/lib/database.types';
     4→import { TelegramService } from '@/lib/telegramService';
     5→
     6→export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic';
     7→
     8→export async function POST(request: NextRequest) {
     9→  try {
    10→    const body = await request.json();
    11→    const { name, url, description, client, link_types, access } = body;
    12→
    13→    // Validate required fields
    14→    if (!name || !name.trim()) {
    15→      return NextResponse.json(
    16→        { error: 'Name is required' },
    17→        { status: 400 }
    18→      );
    19→    }
    20→
    21→    if (!url || !url.trim()) {
    22→      return NextResponse.json(
    23→        { error: 'URL is required' },
    24→        { status: 400 }
    25→      );
    26→    }
    27→
    28→    // Validate URL format
    29→    try {
    30→      new URL(url);
    31→    } catch {
    32→      return NextResponse.json(
    33→        { error: 'Invalid URL format' },
    34→        { status: 400 }
    35→      );
    36→    }
    37→
    38→    // Create server-side Supabase client with service role key to bypass RLS
    39→    const supabaseUrl = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL;
    40→    const supabaseServiceKey = process.env.SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY;
    41→
    42→    if (!supabaseUrl || !supabaseServiceKey) {
    43→      console.error('Missing Supabase environment variables');
    44→      return NextResponse.json(
    45→        { error: 'Server configuration error' },
    46→        { status: 500 }
    47→      );
    48→    }
    49→
    50→    const supabaseAdmin = createClient<Database>(supabaseUrl, supabaseServiceKey, {
    51→      auth: {
    52→        autoRefreshToken: false,
    53→        persistSession: false
    54→      }
    55→    });
    56→
    57→    // Insert link
    58→    const { data: link, error } = await supabaseAdmin
    59→      .from('links')
    60→      .insert([{
    61→        name: name.trim(),
    62→        url: url.trim(),
    63→        description: description?.trim() || null,
    64→        client: client?.trim() || null,
    65→        link_types: link_types || [],
    66→        access: access || 'team',
    67→        status: 'active'
    68→      }])
    69→      .select()
    70→      .single();
    71→
    72→    if (error) {
    73→      console.error('Error inserting link:', error);
    74→      return NextResponse.json(
    75→        { error: 'Failed to save link' },
    76→        { status: 500 }
    77→      );
    78→    }
    79→
    80→    // Send Telegram notification (must await in serverless environment)
    81→    const baseUrl = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL || 'https://portal.holohive.agency';
    82→    const linkUrl = `${baseUrl}/links`;
    83→    const message = `<b>New Link Submitted</b>\n\n` +
    84→      `<b>Name:</b> ${name.trim()}\n` +
    85→      `<b>Client:</b> ${client?.trim() || 'N/A'}\n` +
    86→      `<b>Type:</b> ${link_types?.map((t: string) => t.charAt(0).toUpperCase() + t.slice(1)).join(', ') || 'N/A'}\n\n` +
    87→      `<a href="${linkUrl}">View Links</a>`;
    88→
    89→    try {
    90→      await TelegramService.sendMessage(message);
    91→    } catch (err) {
    92→      console.error('[Links Submit] Telegram notification error:', err);
    93→    }
    94→
    95→    return NextResponse.json({
    96→      success: true,
    97→      link
    98→    });
    99→
   100→  } catch (error) {
   101→    console.error('Error in link submission:', error);
   102→    return NextResponse.json(
   103→      { error: 'Internal server error' },
   104→      { status: 500 }
   105→    );
   106→  }
   107→}
   108→
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
     1→-- Create campaign_reports table
     2→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS public.campaign_reports (
     3→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
     4→    campaign_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.campaigns(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
     5→    custom_message TEXT,
     6→    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT timezone('utc'::text, now()) NOT NULL,
     7→    updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT timezone('utc'::text, now()) NOT NULL
     8→);
     9→
    10→-- Create campaign_report_files table
    11→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS public.campaign_report_files (
    12→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    13→    campaign_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.campaigns(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    14→    file_name TEXT NOT NULL,
    15→    file_url TEXT NOT NULL,
    16→    file_type TEXT NOT NULL,
    17→    file_size BIGINT NOT NULL,
    18→    is_public BOOLEAN DEFAULT false NOT NULL,
    19→    uploaded_by UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
    20→    display_order INTEGER DEFAULT 0 NOT NULL,
    21→    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT timezone('utc'::text, now()) NOT NULL
    22→);
    23→
    24→-- Add columns to campaigns table
    25→ALTER TABLE public.campaigns
    26→ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS share_report_publicly BOOLEAN DEFAULT false,
    27→ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS report_share_link TEXT;
    28→
    29→-- Create indexes for better performance
    30→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_campaign_reports_campaign_id ON public.campaign_reports(campaign_id);
    31→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_campaign_report_files_campaign_id ON public.campaign_report_files(campaign_id);
    32→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_campaign_report_files_is_public ON public.campaign_report_files(is_public);
    33→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_campaign_report_files_display_order ON public.campaign_report_files(display_order);
    34→
    35→-- Enable Row Level Security
    36→ALTER TABLE public.campaign_reports ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
    37→ALTER TABLE public.campaign_report_files ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
    38→
    39→-- RLS Policies for campaign_reports
    40→CREATE POLICY "Users can view reports for their campaigns" ON public.campaign_reports
    41→    FOR SELECT USING (
    42→        EXISTS (
    43→            SELECT 1 FROM public.campaigns
    44→            WHERE campaigns.id = campaign_reports.campaign_id
    45→        )
    46→    );
    47→
    48→CREATE POLICY "Users can insert reports for their campaigns" ON public.campaign_reports
    49→    FOR INSERT WITH CHECK (
    50→        EXISTS (
    51→            SELECT 1 FROM public.campaigns
    52→            WHERE campaigns.id = campaign_reports.campaign_id
    53→        )
    54→    );
    55→
    56→CREATE POLICY "Users can update reports for their campaigns" ON public.campaign_reports
    57→    FOR UPDATE USING (
    58→        EXISTS (
    59→            SELECT 1 FROM public.campaigns
    60→            WHERE campaigns.id = campaign_reports.campaign_id
    61→        )
    62→    );
    63→
    64→-- RLS Policies for campaign_report_files
    65→CREATE POLICY "Users can view files for their campaigns" ON public.campaign_report_files
    66→    FOR SELECT USING (
    67→        EXISTS (
    68→            SELECT 1 FROM public.campaigns
    69→            WHERE campaigns.id = campaign_report_files.campaign_id
    70→        )
    71→        OR is_public = true
    72→    );
    73→
    74→CREATE POLICY "Authenticated users can insert files" ON public.campaign_report_files
    75→    FOR INSERT WITH CHECK (
    76→        auth.uid() IS NOT NULL
    77→        AND EXISTS (
    78→            SELECT 1 FROM public.campaigns
    79→            WHERE campaigns.id = campaign_report_files.campaign_id
    80→        )
    81→    );
    82→
    83→CREATE POLICY "Users can update their campaign files" ON public.campaign_report_files
    84→    FOR UPDATE USING (
    85→        EXISTS (
    86→            SELECT 1 FROM public.campaigns
    87→            WHERE campaigns.id = campaign_report_files.campaign_id
    88→        )
    89→    );
    90→
    91→CREATE POLICY "Users can delete their campaign files" ON public.campaign_report_files
    92→    FOR DELETE USING (
    93→        EXISTS (
    94→            SELECT 1 FROM public.campaigns
    95→            WHERE campaigns.id = campaign_report_files.campaign_id
    96→        )
    97→    );
    98→
    99→-- Create updated_at trigger for campaign_reports
   100→CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.update_campaign_reports_updated_at()
   101→RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
   102→BEGIN
   103→    NEW.updated_at = timezone('utc'::text, now());
   104→    RETURN NEW;
   105→END;
   106→$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
   107→
   108→CREATE TRIGGER update_campaign_reports_updated_at
   109→    BEFORE UPDATE ON public.campaign_reports
   110→    FOR EACH ROW
   111→    EXECUTE FUNCTION public.update_campaign_reports_updated_at();
   112→
   113→-- Grant permissions
   114→GRANT ALL ON public.campaign_reports TO authenticated;
   115→GRANT ALL ON public.campaign_report_files TO authenticated;
   116→GRANT SELECT ON public.campaign_report_files TO anon;
   117→
```

     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next/server';

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./app/api/telegram/delete/route.ts`

```text
     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next/server';
     2→
     3→export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic';
     4→
     5→/**
     6→ * Delete a message from a Telegram chat
     7→ * POST /api/telegram/delete
     8→ */
     9→export async function POST(request: NextRequest) {
    10→  try {
    11→    const body = await request.json();
    12→    const { chatId, messageId } = body;
    13→
    14→    if (!chatId || !messageId) {
    15→      return NextResponse.json(
    16→        { error: 'chatId and messageId are required' },
    17→        { status: 400 }
    18→      );
    19→    }
    20→
    21→    const botToken = process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN;
    22→    if (!botToken) {
    23→      console.error('[Telegram Delete] Bot token not configured');
    24→      return NextResponse.json(
    25→        { error: 'Telegram bot not configured' },
    26→        { status: 500 }
    27→      );
    28→    }
    29→
    30→    const response = await fetch(
    31→      `https://api.telegram.org/bot${botToken}/deleteMessage`,
    32→      {
    33→        method: 'POST',
    34→        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
    35→        body: JSON.stringify({
    36→          chat_id: chatId,
    37→          message_id: messageId
    38→        })
    39→      }
    40→    );
    41→
    42→    if (!response.ok) {
    43→      const error = await response.json();
    44→      console.error('[Telegram Delete] Error:', error);
    45→      return NextResponse.json(
    46→        { error: 'Failed to delete message', details: error },
    47→        { status: 500 }
    48→      );
    49→    }
    50→
    51→    const result = await response.json();
    52→    console.log('[Telegram Delete] Message deleted from chat:', chatId, 'message_id:', messageId);
    53→
    54→    return NextResponse.json({
    55→      success: true
    56→    });
    57→  } catch (error) {

    59→    return NextResponse.json(
    60→      { error: 'Internal server error' },

    62→    );
    63→  }
    64→}

```text
     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next/server';
     2→import { TelegramService } from '@/lib/telegramService';
     3→import { createClient } from '@supabase/supabase-js';
     4→
     5→export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic';
     6→
     7→// Require admin authentication
     8→const supabase = createClient(
     9→  process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL!,
    10→  process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY!
    11→);
    12→
    13→/**
    14→ * GET /api/telegram/webhook/manage - Get webhook status
    15→ */
    16→export async function GET(request: NextRequest) {
    17→  try {
    18→    // Check auth
    19→    const authHeader = request.headers.get('authorization');
    20→    if (!authHeader) {
    21→      return NextResponse.json({ error: 'Unauthorized' }, { status: 401 });
    22→    }
    23→
    24→    const info = await TelegramService.getWebhookInfo();
    25→    return NextResponse.json(info);
    26→  } catch (error) {
    27→    console.error('Error getting webhook info:', error);
    28→    return NextResponse.json(
    29→      { error: 'Failed to get webhook info' },
    30→      { status: 500 }
    31→    );
    32→  }
    33→}
    34→
    35→/**
    36→ * POST /api/telegram/webhook/manage - Register webhook
    37→ * Body: { action: 'register' | 'delete', webhookUrl?: string }
    38→ */
    39→export async function POST(request: NextRequest) {
    40→  try {
    41→    // Check auth
    42→    const authHeader = request.headers.get('authorization');
    43→    if (!authHeader) {
    44→      return NextResponse.json({ error: 'Unauthorized' }, { status: 401 });
    45→    }
    46→
    47→    const { action, webhookUrl } = await request.json();
    48→
    49→    if (action === 'register') {
    50→      // Auto-construct webhook URL if not provided
    51→      const baseUrl = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL
    52→        || process.env.VERCEL_URL
    53→        || '';
    54→
    55→      const url = webhookUrl || `https://${baseUrl.replace(/^https?:\/\//, '')}/api/telegram/webhook`;
    56→
    57→      if (!url.startsWith('https://')) {
    58→        return NextResponse.json(
    59→          { error: 'Webhook URL must be HTTPS. Deploy to production first.' },
    60→          { status: 400 }
    61→        );
    62→      }
    63→
    64→      const result = await TelegramService.registerWebhook(url);
    65→
    66→      if (result.success) {
    67→        return NextResponse.json({
    68→          success: true,
    69→          message: 'Webhook registered successfully',
    70→          webhookUrl: url
    71→        });
    72→      } else {
    73→        return NextResponse.json(
    74→          { success: false, error: result.error },
    75→          { status: 400 }
    76→        );
    77→      }
    78→    } else if (action === 'delete') {
    79→      const result = await TelegramService.deleteWebhook();
    80→
    81→      if (result.success) {
    82→        return NextResponse.json({
    83→          success: true,
    84→          message: 'Webhook deleted successfully'
    85→        });
    86→      } else {
    87→        return NextResponse.json(
    88→          { success: false, error: result.error },
    89→          { status: 400 }
    90→        );
    91→      }
    92→    } else {
    93→      return NextResponse.json(
    94→        { error: 'Invalid action. Use "register" or "delete"' },
    95→        { status: 400 }
    96→      );
    97→    }
    98→  } catch (error) {
    99→    console.error('Error managing webhook:', error);
   100→    return NextResponse.json(
   101→      { error: 'Failed to manage webhook' },
   102→      { status: 500 }
   103→    );
   104→  }
   105→}
   106→
```

Perfect! I now have a comprehensive understanding of how form submissions trigger Telegram messages. Let me compile my findings:

## Summary: Form Submission to Telegram Message Flow

I've thoroughly examined the codebase and found a complete Telegram integration system. Here's how form submissions trigger Telegram messages:

### **1. FORM SUBMISSION ROUTES (API Endpoints)**

All form submission routes follow the same pattern of sending Telegram notifications:

**Forms Submissions:**
- **File:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/api/forms/submit/route.ts`
- **Flow:** 
  - Receives form data (form_id, response_data, submitted_by_email, submitted_by_name)
  - Inserts into `form_responses` table
  - Calls `TelegramService.sendFormSubmissionNotification()` (lines 76-84)
  - Does NOT fail if Telegram notification fails (lines 91-94)
  - Logs Telegram notification status (lines 67-72)

**Leads Submissions:**
- **File:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/api/leads/submit/route.ts`
- **Flow:**
  - Inserts into `crm_opportunities` table with stage='new'
  - Constructs HTML formatted message (lines 130-137)
  - Calls `TelegramService.sendMessage()` (line 140)
  - Sends to default chat with formatted opportunity details

**Partners Submissions:**
- **File:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/api/partners/submit/route.ts`
- **Flow:**
  - Inserts into `crm_affiliates` table with status='new'
  - Constructs HTML formatted message (lines 105-112)
  - Calls `TelegramService.sendMessage()` (line 115)
  - Includes partner details (name, affiliation, category, POC info)

**Links Submissions:**
- **File:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/api/links/submit/route.ts`
- **Flow:**
  - Inserts into `links` table
  - Constructs HTML formatted message (lines 83-87)
  - Calls `TelegramService.sendMessage()` (line 90)
  - Includes link name, client, and types

---

### **2. TELEGRAM SERVICE (Core Integration)**

**File:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/telegramService.ts`

**Key Methods:**

1. **`sendMessage(text, parseMode)`** (lines 31-97)
   - Reads env vars at runtime: `TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN`, `TELEGRAM_TERMINAL_CHAT_ID`, `TELEGRAM_TERMINAL_THREAD_ID`
   - Sends to configured chat using Telegram Bot API
   - Returns boolean success status
   - Supports HTML/Markdown parsing

2. **`sendFormSubmissionNotification(formName, formId, submittedBy, responseData)`** (lines 102-120)
   - Specialized method for form submissions
   - Constructs message with form name and link to form view page
   - Constructs base URL from `NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL`, `VERCEL_URL`, or defaults to localhost:3000
   - Uses `/forms/{formId}` URL pattern

3. **`sendToChat(chatId, text, parseMode)`** (lines 232-267)
   - Sends to specific chat ID (for CRM integration)
   - Used for opportunity/contact-specific messages

4. **Webhook Management Methods:**
   - `registerWebhook(webhookUrl)` - Sets up incoming webhook from Telegram
   - `deleteWebhook()` - Removes webhook
   - `getWebhookInfo()` - Retrieves webhook status
   - Uses `allowed_updates: ['message', 'edited_message']`

---

### **3. TELEGRAM WEBHOOK ENDPOINT**

**File:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/api/telegram/webhook/route.ts`

**Purpose:** Receives incoming messages from Telegram and tracks them

**Key Functions:**

1. **`handleMessage(message)`** (lines 349-454)
   - Processes all incoming messages
   - Updates `crm_opportunities.last_message_at` when message matches a GC (Group Chat) field
   - Different timestamp fields for different senders:
     - Bot messages → `last_reply_at`
     - Team member messages → `last_team_message_at`
     - Lead/other messages → `last_message_at`
   - Stores message in `telegram_messages` table

2. **`handleCommand(chatId, command, args, message)`** (lines 265-344)
   - Processes `/` commands
   - Built-in `/test` command sends chat info to Andy Lee (ID: 6281931733)
   - Database-driven commands: reads from `telegram_commands` table
   - Supports team-only commands (checks `users.telegram_id`)
   - Supports image responses

3. **`forwardKolSocialLinks()`** (lines 199-260)
   - Detects Telegram/Twitter links in KOL chats
   - Forwards to Content thread (Chat: -1002636253963, Thread: 4280)
   - Extracts and formats social links for content team

4. **`trackChat(chatId, title, chatType, messageDate)`** (lines 459-500)
   - Records/updates chats in `telegram_chats` table
   - Tracks message count and last message time
   - Supports group, supergroup, and private chats

5. **`storeMessage()`** (lines 505-537)
   - Stores individual messages in `telegram_messages` table
   - Truncates messages over 500 characters
   - Uses upsert for idempotency

---

### **4. TELEGRAM API ROUTES**

**Send Messages:**
- **File:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/api/telegram/send/route.ts`
- **Endpoint:** `POST /api/telegram/send`
- **Request body:** `{ message, chatId?, threadId?, useTerminalChat }`
- Stores sent messages in `telegram_messages` table
- Defaults to Operations chat (-1002636253963)

**Test Telegram:**
- **File:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/api/telegram/test/route.ts`
- **Endpoint:** `GET /api/telegram/test`
- Sends test message to verify bot configuration

**Delete Messages:**
- **File:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/api/telegram/delete/route.ts`
- **Endpoint:** `POST /api/telegram/delete`
- **Request body:** `{ chatId, messageId }`

**Webhook Management:**
- **File:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/api/telegram/webhook/manage/route.ts`
- **Endpoint:** `GET/POST /api/telegram/webhook/manage`
- **Actions:** register, delete
- Auto-constructs webhook URL from environment

---

### **5. DATABASE TABLES INVOLVED**

**form_responses:**
- Stores form submissions
- Fields: form_id, response_data, submitted_by_email, submitted_by_name, submitted_at
- No automatic triggers for Telegram (handled in API route)

**telegram_messages:**
- Stores all sent and received Telegram messages
- Fields: chat_id, message_id, from_user_id, from_user_name, from_username, text, message_date
- Populated by webhook handler and send endpoint

**telegram_chats:**
- Tracks group chats and DMs the bot participates in
- Fields: chat_id, title, chat_type, message_count, last_message_at, master_kol_id (optional)
- Links to KOLs for social link detection

**telegram_commands:**
- User-defined bot commands
- Fields: command, response, image_url, team_only, is_active
- Loaded from database when commands are executed

**crm_opportunities:**
- Tracks sales opportunities from leads
- Updated with timestamps: last_message_at, last_reply_at, last_team_message_at
- GC field stores Telegram group chat ID for tracking

**crm_affiliates:**
- Stores partner applications

---

### **6. ENVIRONMENT VARIABLES REQUIRED**

```
TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN              # Bot token from BotFather
TELEGRAM_TERMINAL_CHAT_ID       # Default chat for notifications
TELEGRAM_TERMINAL_THREAD_ID     # Optional: thread/topic ID
TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET         # Optional: secret for webhook verification
NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL            # Base URL for form links (e.g., https://portal.holohive.agency)
```

---

### **7. MESSAGE FLOW DIAGRAM**

```
Form Submission (POST /api/forms/submit)
    ↓
Insert into form_responses
    ↓
TelegramService.sendFormSubmissionNotification()
    ↓
Fetch TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN & TELEGRAM_TERMINAL_CHAT_ID from env
    ↓
POST to https://api.telegram.org/bot{token}/sendMessage
    ↓
Message arrives in Telegram chat
    ↓
Telegram bot sends webhook to /api/telegram/webhook
    ↓
handleMessage() processes update
    ↓
Stores in telegram_messages & telegram_chats tables
    ↓
Updates crm_opportunities if GC matches
```

---

### **KEY FINDINGS:**

1. **Non-blocking Notifications**: Form submissions don't fail if Telegram notification fails (try/catch blocks)
2. **Direct API Calls**: Uses direct Telegram Bot API calls, not Supabase edge functions
3. **Webhook Tracking**: Bidirectional - sends notifications AND receives/tracks incoming messages
4. **Message Persistence**: All messages stored in database for audit trail
5. **Dynamic Routing**: Supports multiple destination chats and threads
6. **Team Integration**: Tracks team members by telegram_id and differentiates message sources
7. **Content Curation**: Automatically detects and forwards KOL social links to content team

All code is in Next.js API routes, not Supabase edge functions. The integration is fully functional and production-ready.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-04 15:44:32Z)**_

Here's how it works in the existing codebase:

**Existing pattern:** Each public form submission goes through a **Next.js API route** (e.g., `/api/forms/submit`, `/api/leads/submit`, `/api/partners/submit`) that:
1. Inserts data into the database
2. Calls `TelegramService.sendMessage()` or `TelegramService.sendFormSubmissionNotification()` to notify the Telegram chat

**Two options for contact submissions:**

### Option A: API Route (same as other forms)
Create an API route at `/api/contact/submit` that:
1. Inserts into `contact_submissions`
2. Calls `TelegramService.sendMessage()` with a formatted HTML message (name, project, funding, timeline, etc.)
3. The public contact form would POST to this API route instead of inserting directly via Supabase client

This is the **consistent** approach — it's exactly how `/api/leads/submit`, `/api/partners/submit`, and `/api/forms/submit` work.

### Option B: Database Trigger + Edge Function
Create a Supabase database trigger on `contact_submissions` INSERT that calls an edge function to send the Telegram message. This would work even if data is inserted directly (not through the API), but it's a different pattern from the rest of the codebase.

**Option A is recommended** since it matches the existing architecture. The key question is: where is the contact form that inserts into `contact_submissions`? That form would need to POST to the new API route instead of inserting directly. Want me to look into where that form lives?

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-04 15:45:50Z)**_



Since the form is in a different project, **Option B (Database Trigger)** is the way to go. That way, no matter where the INSERT comes from, the Telegram notification fires automatically.

The approach would be:
1. Create a Supabase **database webhook** or **pg_net** trigger on `contact_submissions` INSERT
2. It calls a **Supabase Edge Function** that formats and sends the Telegram message using the bot token and terminal chat ID
3. The edge function would format the submission details (name, project, funding, timeline, etc.) as an HTML message

This keeps it fully decoupled from the other project — any INSERT into `contact_submissions` from any source will trigger the notification.

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./lib/telegramService.ts`

```text
     1→/**
     2→ * Telegram Bot Service
     3→ * Sends notifications to Telegram chat rooms
     4→ */
     5→
     6→interface TelegramMessage {
     7→  chat_id: string;
     8→  text: string;
     9→  parse_mode?: 'HTML' | 'Markdown' | 'MarkdownV2';
    10→  disable_web_page_preview?: boolean;
    11→  message_thread_id?: number;
    12→}
    13→
    14→export class TelegramService {
    15→  // Read env vars at runtime via getters to avoid serverless caching issues
    16→  private static get botToken() {
    17→    return process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN;
    18→  }
    19→
    20→  private static get chatId() {
    21→    return process.env.TELEGRAM_TERMINAL_CHAT_ID;
    22→  }
    23→
    24→  private static get threadId() {
    25→    return process.env.TELEGRAM_TERMINAL_THREAD_ID;
    26→  }
    27→
    28→  /**
    29→   * Send a message to the configured Telegram chat
    30→   */
    31→  static async sendMessage(text: string, parseMode: 'HTML' | 'Markdown' = 'HTML'): Promise<boolean> {
    32→    const botToken = this.botToken;
    33→    const chatId = this.chatId;
    34→    const threadId = this.threadId;
    35→
    36→    if (!botToken || !chatId) {
    37→      console.error('[Telegram] Configuration missing:', {
    38→        hasToken: !!botToken,
    39→        hasChatId: !!chatId,
    40→        tokenPrefix: botToken ? botToken.substring(0, 10) + '...' : 'missing',
    41→        chatId: chatId || 'missing'
    42→      });
    43→      return false;
    44→    }
    45→
    46→    try {
    47→      const message: TelegramMessage = {
    48→        chat_id: chatId,
    49→        text,
    50→        parse_mode: parseMode,
    51→        disable_web_page_preview: false,
    52→      };
    53→
    54→      // Add thread ID if configured (for sending to specific topics in groups)
    55→      if (threadId) {
    56→        message.message_thread_id = parseInt(threadId);
    57→      }
    58→
    59→      console.log('[Telegram] Sending message:', {
    60→        chatId: chatId,
    61→        threadId: threadId,
    62→        messageLength: text.length,
    63→        parseMode
    64→      });
    65→
    66→      const response = await fetch(
    67→        `https://api.telegram.org/bot${botToken}/sendMessage`,
    68→        {
    69→          method: 'POST',
    70→          headers: {
    71→            'Content-Type': 'application/json',
    72→          },
    73→          body: JSON.stringify(message),
    74→        }
    75→      );
    76→
    77→      if (!response.ok) {
    78→        const error = await response.json();
    79→        console.error('[Telegram] API error:', {
    80→          status: response.status,
    81→          error
    82→        });
    83→        return false;
    84→      }
    85→
    86→      const result = await response.json();
    87→      console.log('[Telegram] Message sent successfully:', {
    88→        messageId: result.result?.message_id,
    89→        chatId: result.result?.chat?.id
    90→      });
    91→
    92→      return true;
    93→    } catch (error) {
    94→      console.error('[Telegram] Error sending message:', error);
    95→      return false;
    96→    }
    97→  }
    98→
    99→  /**

boundaries.\n\n<untrusted-data-70c3f340-8853-48ee-8b69-45f2afc41034>\n[{\"pg_get_functiondef\":\"CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION net.http_post(url text, body jsonb DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb, params jsonb DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb, headers jsonb DEFAULT '{\\\"Content-Type\\\": \\\"application/json\\\"}'::jsonb, timeout_milliseconds integer DEFAULT 5000)\\n RETURNS bigint\\n LANGUAGE plpgsql\\n PARALLEL SAFE\\nAS $function$\\ndeclare\\n    request_id bigint;\\n    params_array text[];\\n    content_type text;\\nbegin\\n\\n    -- Exctract the content_type from headers\\n    select\\n        header_value into content_type\\n    from\\n        jsonb_each_text(coalesce(headers, '{}'::jsonb)) r(header_name, header_value)\\n    where\\n        lower(header_name) = 'content-type'\\n    limit\\n        1;\\n\\n    -- If the user provided new headers and omitted the content type\\n    -- add it back in automatically\\n    if content_type is null then\\n        select headers || '{\\\"Content-Type\\\": \\\"application/json\\\"}'::jsonb into headers;\\n    end if;\\n\\n    -- Confirm that the content-type is set as \\\"application/json\\\"\\n    if content_type <> 'application/json' then\\n        raise exception 'Content-Type header must be \\\"application/json\\\"';\\n    end if;\\n\\n    select\\n        coalesce(array_agg(net._urlencode_string(key) || '=' || net._urlencode_string(value)), '{}')\\n    into\\n        params_array\\n    from\\n        jsonb_each_text(params);\\n\\n    -- Add to the request queue\\n    insert into net.http_request_queue(method, url, headers, body, timeout_milliseconds)\\n    values (\\n        'POST',\\n        net._encode_url_with_params_array(url, params_array),\\n        headers,\\n        convert_to(body::text, 'UTF8'),\\n        timeout_milliseconds\\n    )\\n    returning id\\n    into request_id;\\n\\n    return request_id;\\nend\\n$function$\\n\"}]\n</untrusted-data-70c3f340-8853-48ee-8b69-45f2afc41034>\n\nUse this data to  `./app/auth/page.tsx`

```text
     1→'use client'
     2→
     3→import { useState, useEffect } from 'react'
     4→import { useRouter, useSearchParams } from 'next/navigation'
     5→import Image from 'next/image'
     6→import { LoginForm } from '@/components/auth/LoginForm'
     7→import { SignUpForm } from '@/components/auth/SignUpForm'
     8→import { ForgotPasswordForm } from '@/components/auth/ForgotPasswordForm'
     9→import { useAuth } from '@/contexts/AuthContext'
    10→
    11→
    12→export default function AuthPage() {
    13→  const [isLogin, setIsLogin] = useState(true)
    14→  const [showForgotPassword, setShowForgotPassword] = useState(false)
    15→  const { user, loading } = useAuth()
    16→  const router = useRouter()
    17→  const searchParams = useSearchParams()
    18→
    19→  useEffect(() => {
    20→    if (user && !loading) {
    21→      const redirectTo = searchParams.get('redirectTo')
    22→      // Only allow relative paths to prevent open redirect
    23→      if (redirectTo && redirectTo.startsWith('/')) {
    24→        router.push(redirectTo)
    25→      } else {
    26→        router.push('/')
    27→      }
    28→    }
    29→  }, [user, loading, router, searchParams])
    30→
    31→  const toggleMode = () => {
    32→    setIsLogin(!isLogin)
    33→    setShowForgotPassword(false)
    34→  }
    35→
    36→  const handleForgotPassword = () => {
    37→    setShowForgotPassword(true)
    38→  }
    39→
    40→  const handleBackToLogin = () => {
    41→    setShowForgotPassword(false)
    42→  }
    43→
    44→  if (loading) {
    45→    return (
    46→      <div className="min-h-screen flex items-center justify-center" style={{ backgroundColor: '#f6feff' }}>
    47→        <div className="animate-spin rounded-full h-32 w-32 border-b-2" style={{ borderBottomColor: '#3e8692' }}></div>
    48→      </div>
    49→    )
    50→  }
    51→
    52→  if (user) {
    53→    return null // Will redirect
    54→  }
    55→
    56→  return (
    57→    <div className="min-h-screen flex items-center justify-center py-12 px-4 sm:px-6 lg:px-8" style={{ backgroundColor: '#f6feff' }}>
    58→      <div className="max-w-md w-full space-y-8">
    59→        <div className="text-center">
    60→          {/* Logo */}
    61→          <div className="flex justify-center mb-8">
    62→            <Image
    63→              src="/images/logo.png"
    64→              alt="KOL Campaign Manager Logo"
    65→              width={120}
    66→              height={120}
    67→              priority
    68→            />
    69→          </div>
    70→        </div>
    71→        
    72→        {showForgotPassword ? (
    73→          <ForgotPasswordForm onBackToLogin={handleBackToLogin} />
    74→        ) : isLogin ? (
    75→          <LoginForm onToggleMode={toggleMode} onForgotPassword={handleForgotPassword} />
    76→        ) : (
    77→          <SignUpForm onToggleMode={toggleMode} />
    78→        )}
    79→      </div>
    80→    </div>
    81→  )
    82→}
```
</details></tool-use>
