     1→'use client'
     2→
     3→import { useState } from 'react'
     4→import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button'
     5→import { Input } from '@/components/ui/input'
     6→import { Label } from '@/components/ui/label'
     7→import { Card, CardContent, CardDescription, CardHeader, CardTitle } from '@/components/ui/card'
     8→import { Alert, AlertDescription } from '@/components/ui/alert'
     9→import { Select, SelectContent, SelectItem, SelectTrigger, SelectValue } from '@/components/ui/select'
    10→import { supabase } from '@/lib/supabase'
    11→import { Loader2 } from 'lucide-react'
    12→import { GoogleSignInButton } from './GoogleSignInButton'
    13→
    14→interface SignUpFormProps {
    15→  onToggleMode: () => void
    16→}
    17→
    18→export function SignUpForm({ onToggleMode }: SignUpFormProps) {
    19→  const [email, setEmail] = useState('')
    20→  const [password, setPassword] = useState('')
    21→  const [confirmPassword, setConfirmPassword] = useState('')
    22→  const [name, setName] = useState('')
    23→  const [role, setRole] = useState<'super_admin' | 'admin' | 'member' | 'client'>('member')
    24→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false)
    25→  const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null)
    26→  const [success, setSuccess] = useState(false)
    27→
    28→  const handleSignUp = async (e: React.FormEvent) => {
    29→    e.preventDefault()
    30→    setLoading(true)
    31→    setError(null)
    32→
    33→    if (password !== confirmPassword) {
    34→      setError('Passwords do not match')
    35→      setLoading(false)
    36→      return
    37→    }
    38→
    39→    if (password.length < 6) {
    40→      setError('Password must be at least 6 characters long')
    41→      setLoading(false)
    42→      return
    43→    }
    44→
    45→    try {
    46→      const { error } = await supabase.auth.signUp({
    47→        email,
    48→        password,
    49→        options: {
    50→          data: {
    51→            name: name.trim(),
    52→            role: role,
    53→          },
    54→        },
    55→      })
    56→
    57→      if (error) {
    58→        setError(error.message)
    59→      } else {
    60→        setSuccess(true)
    61→      }
    62→    } catch (err) {
    63→      setError('An unexpected error occurred')
    64→    } finally {
    65→      setLoading(false)
    66→    }
    67→  }
    68→
    69→  if (success) {
    70→    return (
    71→      <Card className="w-full max-w-md">
    72→        <CardHeader className="space-y-1">
    73→          <CardTitle className="text-2xl text-center">Check your email</CardTitle>
    74→          <CardDescription className="text-center">
    75→            We've sent you a confirmation link to verify your account
    76→          </CardDescription>
    77→        </CardHeader>
    78→        <CardContent>
    79→          <Button 
    80→            onClick={onToggleMode} 
    81→            className="w-full hover:opacity-90"
    82→            style={{ backgroundColor: '#3e8692', color: 'white' }}
    83→          >
    84→            Back to Sign In
    85→          </Button>
    86→        </CardContent>
    87→      </Card>
    88→    )
    89→  }
    90→
    91→  return (
    92→    <Card className="w-full max-w-md">
    93→      <CardHeader className="space-y-1">
    94→        <CardTitle className="text-2xl text-center">Create an account</CardTitle>
    95→        <CardDescription className="text-center">
    96→          Enter your information to get started
    97→        </CardDescription>
    98→      </CardHeader>
    99→      <CardContent>
   100→        <form onSubmit={handleSignUp} className="space-y-4">
   101→          {error && (
   102→            <Alert variant="destructive">
   103→              <AlertDescription>{error}</AlertDescription>
   104→            </Alert>
   105→          )}
   106→          
   107→          <div className="space-y-2">
   108→            <Label htmlFor="name">Name</Label>
   109→            <Input
   110→              id="name"
   111→              placeholder="Enter your name"
   112→              value={name}
   113→              onChange={(e) => setName(e.target.value)}
   114→              required
   115→              className="focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-[#3e8692] focus:border-[#3e8692] auth-input"
   116→            />
   117→          </div>
   118→          
   119→          <div className="space-y-2">
   120→            <Label htmlFor="email">Email</Label>
   121→            <Input
   122→              id="email"
   123→              type="email"
   124→              placeholder="Enter your email"
   125→              value={email}
   126→              onChange={(e) => setEmail(e.target.value)}
   127→              required
   128→              className="focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-[#3e8692] focus:border-[#3e8692] auth-input"
   129→            />
   130→          </div>
   131→          
   132→          <div className="space-y-2">
   133→            <Label htmlFor="password">Password</Label>
   134→            <Input
   135→              id="password"
   136→              type="password"
   137→              placeholder="Create a password"
   138→              value={password}
   139→              onChange={(e) => setPassword(e.target.value)}
   140→              required
   141→              className="focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-[#3e8692] focus:border-[#3e8692] auth-input"
   142→            />
   143→          </div>
   144→          
   145→          <div className="space-y-2">
   146→            <Label htmlFor="confirmPassword">Confirm Password</Label>
   147→            <Input
   148→              id="confirmPassword"
   149→              type="password"
   150→              placeholder="Confirm your password"
   151→              value={confirmPassword}
   152→              onChange={(e) => setConfirmPassword(e.target.value)}
   153→              required
   154→              className="focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-[#3e8692] focus:border-[#3e8692] auth-input"
   155→            />
   156→          </div>
   157→          
   158→          <div className="space-y-2">
   159→            <Label htmlFor="role">Role</Label>
   160→            <Select value={role} onValueChange={(value) => setRole(value as 'super_admin' | 'admin' | 'member' | 'client')}>
   161→              <SelectTrigger className="focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-[#3e8692] focus:border-[#3e8692] auth-input">
   162→                <SelectValue placeholder="Select your role" />
   163→              </SelectTrigger>
   164→              <SelectContent>
   165→                <SelectItem value="member">Member</SelectItem>
   166→                <SelectItem value="client">Client</SelectItem>
   167→                <SelectItem value="admin">Admin</SelectItem>
   168→              </SelectContent>
   169→            </Select>
   170→          </div>
   171→          
   172→          <Button
   173→            type="submit"
   174→            className="w-full hover:opacity-90"
   175→            disabled={loading}
   176→            style={{ backgroundColor: '#3e8692', color: 'white' }}
   177→          >
   178→            {loading && <Loader2 className="mr-2 h-4 w-4 animate-spin" />}
   179→            Create Account
   180→          </Button>
   181→
   182→          <div className="relative my-4">
   183→            <div className="absolute inset-0 flex items-center">
   184→              <span className="w-full border-t" />
   185→            </div>
   186→            <div className="relative flex justify-center text-xs uppercase">
   187→              <span className="bg-white px-2 text-gray-500">or</span>

   190→
   191→          <GoogleSignInButton mode="signup" />

   193→          <div className="text-center text-sm text-gray-600">

   195→            <button

   198→              className="hover:underline"

   200→            >
   201→              Sign in
   202→            </button>
   203→          </div>
   204→        </form>
   205→      </CardContent>
   206→    </Card>
   207→  )
   208→}

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-05 07:22:22Z)**_

Here's the current auth setup:

**`/auth` page** — toggles between 3 forms:
- **LoginForm** — email/password + Google sign-in + forgot password link
- **SignUpForm** — name, email, password, confirm password, role selector (Member/Client/Admin) + Google sign-in
- **ForgotPasswordForm** — email-based password reset

**Sign-up flow:**
1. User fills in name, email, password, and selects a role
2. Calls `supabase.auth.signUp()` with name and role in user metadata
3. On success, shows "Check your email" confirmation
4. Email confirmation link verifies the account
5. After login, redirects to `/` or the `redirectTo` query param

**Notable details:**
- Teal (#3e8692) branding throughout
- Role dropdown lets anyone pick Member, Client, or Admin (no super_admin option)
- Google OAuth is available for both login and signup
- Logo at `/images/logo.png`
- Light teal background (`#f6feff`)

What would you like to change?

---

_**User (2026-03-05 07:22:46Z)**_

So anyone can sign up at the moment and access all our data?

---

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-05 07:22:54Z)**_



Yes, that's the current setup. Anyone who signs up gets access. The RLS policies on most tables just check for `authenticated` role, so any signed-up user can read/write data.

There are a few ways to lock this down:

1. **Disable public sign-up** in Supabase Auth settings — only allow invites from the dashboard or admin
2. **Restrict sign-up to specific email domains** (e.g., `@holohive.io` only)
3. **Add an approval flow** — new users sign up but can't access anything until an admin approves them
4. **Remove the sign-up form entirely** — only show login, manage users through Supabase dashboard or an admin page

What approach do you prefer?

---

_**User (2026-03-05 07:23:32Z)**_

Which one do you think fits our system the best

---

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-05 07:23:40Z)**_



Based on your system, I'd recommend **Option 1 + removing the sign-up form**.

Here's why:

- You have a small internal team (7 users) — you don't need self-service sign-up
- You already have an **admin page** and role system (`super_admin`, `admin`, `member`, `client`) — new users should be added by admins with the correct role, not self-assigned
- The role selector on sign-up is a security issue — anyone could pick "Admin"
- Google OAuth for login is fine to keep, but new Google accounts shouldn't auto-create users

**What I'd do:**
1. **Remove the sign-up form** from the auth page (keep login + forgot password + Google sign-in)
2. **Disable public sign-up** in Supabase dashboard (Auth > Settings > toggle off "Enable sign ups")
3. New team members get added via **Supabase dashboard invite** or your existing **admin/team page**

This is the simplest, most secure approach for an internal tool. Want me to implement it?

Perfect! Now let me create a comprehensive summary. Let me compile all the information:

## Auth and Admin System Analysis

Based on my exploration of the codebase, here's a comprehensive breakdown of the auth and admin system:

---

### 1. AuthContext (`/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/contexts/AuthContext.tsx`)

**Lines 1-105: Full code**

This file defines the authentication context that manages user sessions and profiles:

- **AuthContextType interface** (lines 8-15):
  - `user: User | null` - Supabase auth user object
  - `userProfile: UserProfile | null` - Custom user profile with role info
  - `session: Session | null` - Active session
  - `loading: boolean` - Loading state
  - `signOut()` and `refreshUserProfile()` methods

- **User Loading Flow** (lines 40-52):
  - Uses `UserService.getOrCreateUserProfile()` to handle OAuth users
  - Extracts user name from `user_metadata.full_name`, `user_metadata.name`, or email
  - Default role for OAuth users: `'member'`
  - Sets `is_active: true` by default

- **Session Management** (lines 60-88):
  - Gets initial session via `supabase.auth.getSession()`
  - Subscribes to `onAuthStateChange` events
  - Loads user profile when session changes
  - Cleans up subscription on unmount

- **SignOut** (lines 90-93):
  - Calls `supabase.auth.signOut()`
  - Clears `userProfile`

---

### 2. User Service (`/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/userService.ts`)

**Lines 1-193: Full code**

Core service for user management operations:

**Key Methods:**
- `getOrCreateUserProfile()` (lines 35-55): Creates user profile for OAuth users if it doesn't exist, defaults to `'member'` role
- `getUserProfile(userId)` (lines 60-78): Fetch single user by ID
- `getAllUsers()` (lines 83-100): Fetch all users (admin only)
- `updateUserProfile(userId, updates)` (lines 105-122): Update user fields
- `updateUserRole()` (lines 127-129): Change user role
- `isCurrentUserSuperAdmin()` (lines 134-145): Check if current user is super_admin AND is_active
- `isCurrentUserAdmin()` (lines 164-175): Check if current user is admin or super_admin AND is_active
- `deactivateUser()` / `activateUser()` (lines 150-159): Toggle is_active status

**UserProfile Type** (from database.types.ts):
```typescript
Row: {
  created_at: string | null
  email: string
  id: string
  is_active: boolean
  name: string
  profile_photo_url: string | null
  role: string
  telegram_id: string | null
  updated_at: string | null
  x_id: string | null
}
```

---

### 3. Team Page (`/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/team/page.tsx`)

**Lines 1-373: Full code**

User management interface for team members:

**Data Fetching** (lines 86-118):
- Queries `users` table excluding guests: `.neq('role', 'guest')`
- Orders by `created_at` descending
- Displays 6 columns: name, email, role, join date, status, telegram/X connection

**Role Management** (lines 47-84):
- Only super_admin can change roles via dropdown
- Allows changing to: `super_admin`, `admin`, `member`, `guest`
- Shows loading spinner during update
- Uses `UserService.updateUserRole()`

**Display Fields** (lines 311-363):
- Join Date (created_at)
- Status (is_active: boolean)
- Telegram (telegram_id present?)
- X ID (x_id with link to X.com profile)
- Last Updated (updated_at)

**Access Control** (lines 274-307):
- Only super_admin can see role dropdown
- Super_admin can't change own role
- Non-super_admin users see role as read-only badge

---

### 4. Protected Route Component (`/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/components/auth/ProtectedRoute.tsx`)

**Lines 1-51: Full code**

Route protection mechanism:

- **Authentication Check** (lines 16-20): Redirects to `/auth` if not logged in
- **Role-Based Access** (lines 23-29): 
  - If `requiredRoles` provided, checks if `userProfile.role` is in the list
  - Redirects to home `/` if role not allowed
- **Loading State** (lines 31-37): Shows spinner while loading
- Returns `null` during redirect to prevent flash of content

---

### 5. Login Form (`/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/components/auth/LoginForm.tsx`)

**Lines 1-134: Full code**

Email/password authentication:

- **Login** (lines 24-43): 
  - Uses `supabase.auth.signInWithPassword()`
  - Shows error messages from Supabase
- **UI** (lines 45-134):
  - Email and password inputs
  - Error alert display
  - Forgot password link
  - Google sign-in button (embedded)
  - Sign up link

---

### 6. Google Sign-In Button (`/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/components/auth/GoogleSignInButton.tsx`)

**Lines 1-81: Full code**

OAuth authentication:

- **OAuth Flow** (lines 16-41):
  - `supabase.auth.signInWithOAuth({ provider: 'google' })`
  - Redirects to `/auth/callback` on completion
  - Sets `access_type: 'offline'` and `prompt: 'consent'` for OAuth options
  - Supports both `'signin'` and `'signup'` modes
- **UI** (lines 43-80):
  - Google logo SVG
  - Loading spinner during auth
  - Error message display

---

### 7. Users Table Schema (`/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/sql/001_create_users_table.sql`)

**Lines 1-88: Full code**

Database table definition:

```sql
CREATE TABLE users (
  id UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id) PRIMARY KEY,
  email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
  name TEXT NOT NULL,
  role TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (role IN ('admin', 'member', 'client')),
  is_active BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT true,
  created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
);
```

**Extended Fields** (added later, visible in database.types.ts):
- `profile_photo_url` - Profile image URL
- `telegram_id` - Telegram user ID for notifications
- `x_id` - X (Twitter) handle

**Triggers & Functions** (lines 12-25):
- Auto-update `updated_at` on row modification
- Trigger: `update_users_updated_at`

**User Registration Trigger** (lines 63-87):
- Fires when new `auth.users` record is created
- Automatically creates corresponding `users` row
- Extracts name from auth metadata
- Defaults role to `'member'`

---

### 8. RLS Policies (Row Level Security)

**Lines 27-60 in 001_create_users_table.sql:**

```sql
-- Users can view their own record
CREATE POLICY "Users can view own record" ON users
  FOR SELECT USING (auth.uid() = id);

-- Users can update their own record (except role and is_active)
CREATE POLICY "Users can update own record" ON users
  FOR UPDATE USING (auth.uid() = id);

-- Admins can view all records
CREATE POLICY "Admins can view all users" ON users
  FOR SELECT USING (
    EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM users 
      WHERE id = auth.uid() AND role = 'admin'
    )
  );

-- Admins can update all records
CREATE POLICY "Admins can update all users" ON users
  FOR UPDATE USING (
    EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM users 
      WHERE id = auth.uid() AND role = 'admin'
    )
  );

-- Enable insert for auth trigger
CREATE POLICY "Enable insert for auth trigger" ON users
  FOR INSERT 
  WITH CHECK (auth.uid() = id);
```

**Policy Summary:**
- Users can read/write only their own record
- Admins can read all records
- Admins can write all records (via role check)
- Auth trigger can insert new users

---

### 9. Admin Pages (`/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/admin/`)

**Changelog Management** (`app/admin/changelog/page.tsx`, lines 1-481):

- **Access Control** (lines 39, 42-46):
  - **Super_admin only** - Redirects non-super_admins to home
  - Guard at mount: `userProfile?.role === 'super_admin'`

- **Admin Actions** (lines 65-229):
  1. **Create Changelog** - Version, title, content, publish status
  2. **Edit Changelog** - Modify existing entries
  3. **Delete Changelog** - Remove entries
  4. **Toggle Publish** - Draft ↔ Published states
  5. **Telegram Notifications** - Send to HH Operations chat on publish (lines 115-123, 202-214)

- **Publish Logic** (lines 191-229):
  - Can publish draft or unpublish published
  - Sends Telegram notification when publishing
  - Sets `published_at` timestamp

**Field Options Management** (`app/admin/field-options/page.tsx`, lines 1-411):

- **Admin Features**:
  1. **Create Field Options** - Add dropdown values
  2. **Reorder Options** - Drag-and-drop interface (lines 121-169)
  3. **Toggle Active/Inactive** - Toggle `is_active` status
  4. **Delete Options** - Remove field options
  5. **Display Order** - Set sort order for dropdowns

**Admin Layout** (`app/admin/layout.tsx`, lines 1-3):
- Uses client layout (reuses clients/layout.tsx)

---

### 10. Role Hierarchy

**Available Roles:**
1. **`super_admin`** - Full system access (changelog, field options, user roles)
2. **`admin`** - Can view all users, has admin-level access
3. **`member`** - Default role for new users, limited access
4. **`guest`** - Lowest access (filtered out from team page)

**Permissions Model:**
- Super_admin role required for admin pages (changelog, field options)
- Admin/Super_admin can access team management
- Super_admin can modify user roles
- Role-based access enforced via `ProtectedRoute` component

---

### 11. Key Observations

**No Approval System Currently**:
- No `is_approved` or `approval_status` field in users table
- Users are active by default (`is_active: true`)
- No approval workflow in the UI

**Missing Status Fields**:
- Only `is_active` boolean exists (not a detailed status)
- No `invitation_status` or `email_verified` tracking
- No `approved_by` or `approval_date` fields

**Roles Limited**:
- Original schema had only `'admin', 'member', 'client'`
- Now extended with `'super_admin'` in application code
- Only used in application, not enforced at DB level

**Session Management**:
- Uses Supabase session tokens
- Auth context automatically refreshes on page load
- No manual token refresh logic in UI

---

This system has a solid foundation for role-based access control but lacks a formal user approval/invitation workflow. It relies on OAuth (Google) and email/password auth with automatic user profile creation.

 `./contexts/AuthContext.tsx`

```text
     1→'use client'
     2→
     3→import { createContext, useContext, useEffect, useState } from 'react'
     4→import { User, Session } from '@supabase/supabase-js'
     5→import { supabase } from '@/lib/supabase'
     6→import { UserService, UserProfile } from '@/lib/userService'
     7→
     8→interface AuthContextType {
     9→  user: User | null
    10→  userProfile: UserProfile | null
    11→  session: Session | null
    12→  loading: boolean
    13→  signOut: () => Promise<void>
    14→  refreshUserProfile: () => Promise<void>
    15→}
    16→
    17→const AuthContext = createContext<AuthContextType>({
    18→  user: null,
    19→  userProfile: null,
    20→  session: null,
    21→  loading: true,
    22→  signOut: async () => {},
    23→  refreshUserProfile: async () => {},
    24→})
    25→
    26→export const useAuth = () => {
    27→  const context = useContext(AuthContext)
    28→  if (!context) {
    29→    throw new Error('useAuth must be used within an AuthProvider')
    30→  }
    31→  return context
    32→}
    33→
    34→export const AuthProvider = ({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) => {
    35→  const [user, setUser] = useState<User | null>(null)
    36→  const [userProfile, setUserProfile] = useState<UserProfile | null>(null)
    37→  const [session, setSession] = useState<Session | null>(null)
    38→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true)
    39→
    40→  const loadUserProfile = async (authUser: User) => {
    41→    // Use getOrCreateUserProfile to handle OAuth users who don't have a profile yet
    42→    const name = authUser.user_metadata?.full_name ||
    43→                 authUser.user_metadata?.name ||
    44→                 authUser.email?.split('@')[0]
    45→    const profile = await UserService.getOrCreateUserProfile(
    46→      authUser.id,
    47→      authUser.email || '',
    48→      name,
    49→      'member' // Default role for OAuth users
    50→    )
    51→    setUserProfile(profile)
    52→  }
    53→
    54→  const refreshUserProfile = async () => {
    55→    if (user) {
    56→      await loadUserProfile(user)
    57→    }
    58→  }
    59→
    60→  useEffect(() => {
    61→    // Get initial session
    62→    supabase.auth.getSession().then(({ data: { session } }) => {
    63→      setSession(session)
    64→      setUser(session?.user ?? null)
    65→      if (session?.user) {
    66→        loadUserProfile(session.user)
    67→      } else {
    68→        setUserProfile(null)
    69→      }
    70→      setLoading(false)
    71→    })
    72→
    73→    // Listen for auth changes
    74→    const {
    75→      data: { subscription },
    76→    } = supabase.auth.onAuthStateChange((_event, session) => {
    77→      setSession(session)
    78→      setUser(session?.user ?? null)
    79→      if (session?.user) {
    80→        loadUserProfile(session.user)
    81→      } else {
    82→        setUserProfile(null)
    83→      }
    84→      setLoading(false)
    85→    })
    86→
    87→    return () => subscription.unsubscribe()
    88→  }, [])
    89→
    90→  const signOut = async () => {
    91→    await supabase.auth.signOut()
    92→    setUserProfile(null)
    93→  }
    94→
    95→  const value = {
    96→    user,
    97→    userProfile,
    98→    session,
    99→    loading,
   100→    signOut,
   101→    refreshUserProfile,
   102→  }
   103→
   104→  return <AuthContext.Provider value={value}>{children}</AuthContext.Provider>
   105→}
```
</details></tool-use>

   198→
   199→  return (
   200→    <div className="min-h-[calc(100vh-64px)] w-full bg-gray-50">
   201→      <div className="w-full">
   202→        <div className="space-y-6">
   203→          <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
   204→            <div>
   205→              <h2 className="text-2xl font-bold text-gray-900">Team Members</h2>
   206→              <p className="text-gray-600">Manage your team members</p>
   207→            </div>
   208→          </div>
   209→          
   210→          <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
   211→            <div className="relative flex-1 max-w-sm">
   212→              <Search className="absolute left-3 top-1/2 transform -translate-y-1/2 h-4 w-4 text-gray-400" />
   213→              <Input
   214→                placeholder="Search team members..."
   215→                className="pl-10 auth-input"
   216→                value={searchTerm}
   217→                onChange={(e) => setSearchTerm(e.target.value)}
   218→              />
   219→            </div>
   220→          </div>
   221→
   222→          {filteredTeamMembers.length === 0 ? (
   223→            <div className="text-center py-12">
   224→              <Shield className="h-12 w-12 text-gray-400 mx-auto mb-4" />
   225→              <h3 className="text-lg font-medium text-gray-900 mb-2">
   226→                {searchTerm ? 'No team members found' : 'No team members yet'}
   227→              </h3>
   228→              <p className="text-gray-600">
   229→                {searchTerm 
   230→                  ? 'Try adjusting your search terms.' 
   231→                  : 'Team members will appear here.'
   232→                }
   233→              </p>
   234→            </div>
   235→          ) : (
   236→            <div className="grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3 gap-6">
   237→              {filteredTeamMembers.map((member) => (
   238→                <Card key={member.id}>
   239→                  <CardHeader className="pb-4">
   240→                    <div className="flex flex-col items-center text-center">
   241→                      {member.profile_photo_url ? (
   242→                        <div className="w-16 h-16 rounded-full overflow-hidden mb-3 relative">
   243→                          <img 
   244→                            src={member.profile_photo_url} 
   245→                            alt={`${member.name || 'User'} profile`}
   246→                            className="w-full h-full object-cover"
   247→                            onError={(e) => {
   248→                              // Fallback to initials if image fails to load
   249→                              const target = e.target as HTMLImageElement;
   250→                              target.style.display = 'none';
   251→                              target.nextElementSibling?.classList.remove('hidden');
   252→                            }}
   253→                          />
   254→                          <div className="w-16 h-16 bg-gradient-to-br from-[#3e8692] to-[#2d6470] rounded-full flex items-center justify-center absolute top-0 left-0 hidden">
   255→                            <span className="text-white font-bold text-xl">
   256→                              {getUserInitials(member.name || member.email)}
   257→                            </span>
   258→                          </div>
   259→                        </div>
   260→                      ) : (
   261→                        <div className="w-16 h-16 bg-gradient-to-br from-[#3e8692] to-[#2d6470] rounded-full flex items-center justify-center mb-3">
   262→                          <span className="text-white font-bold text-xl">
   263→                            {getUserInitials(member.name || member.email)}
   264→                          </span>
   265→                        </div>
   266→                      )}
   267→                      <div className="mb-2">
   268→                        <h3 className="font-semibold text-gray-900 text-lg">
   269→                          {member.name || 'Unnamed User'}
   270→                        </h3>
   271→                        <p className="text-sm text-gray-500">{member.email}</p>
   272→                      </div>
   273→                      <div className="flex items-center space-x-2">
   274→                        {isSuperAdmin && member.id !== userProfile?.id ? (
   275→                          <div className="relative">
   276→                            {updatingRoleId === member.id && (
   277→                              <div className="absolute inset-0 flex items-center justify-center bg-white/80 rounded z-10">
   278→                                <Loader2 className="h-4 w-4 animate-spin text-[#3e8692]" />
   279→                              </div>
   280→                            )}
   281→                            <Select
   282→                              value={member.role}
   283→                              onValueChange={(value) => handleRoleChange(member.id, value)}
   284→                              disabled={updatingRoleId === member.id}
   285→                            >
   286→                              <SelectTrigger className="h-7 text-xs w-[120px]">
   287→                                <SelectValue />
   288→                              </SelectTrigger>
   289→                              <SelectContent>
   290→                                <SelectItem value="super_admin">Super Admin</SelectItem>
   291→                                <SelectItem value="admin">Admin</SelectItem>
   292→                                <SelectItem value="member">Member</SelectItem>
   293→                                <SelectItem value="guest">Guest</SelectItem>
   294→                              </SelectContent>
   295→                            </Select>
   296→                          </div>
   297→                        ) : (
   298→                          <span className={`inline-flex items-center px-2 py-1 rounded-full text-xs font-medium ${
   299→                            member.role === 'super_admin'
   300→                              ? 'bg-purple-100 text-purple-800'
   301→                              : 'bg-blue-100 text-blue-800'
   302→                          }`}>
   303→                            {member.role === 'super_admin'
   304→                              ? 'Super Admin'
   305→                              : member.role.charAt(0).toUpperCase() + member.role.slice(1)}
   306→                          </span>
   307→                        )}
   308→                      </div>
   309→                    </div>
   310→                  </CardHeader>
   311→                  <CardContent className="space-y-3">
   312→                    {/* Join Date */}
   313→                    <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
   314→                      <span className="text-sm text-gray-600">Join Date</span>
   315→                      <span className="font-medium text-gray-900">
   316→                        {formatDate(member.created_at)}
   317→                      </span>
   318→                    </div>
   319→
   320→                    {/* Status */}
   321→                    <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
   322→                      <span className="text-sm text-gray-600">Status</span>
   323→                      <span className="font-medium text-gray-900">
   324→                        {member.is_active ? 'Active' : 'Inactive'}
   325→                      </span>
   326→                    </div>
   327→
   328→                    {/* Telegram Status */}
   329→                    <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
   330→                      <span className="text-sm text-gray-600">Telegram</span>
   331→                      <span className={`font-medium ${member.telegram_id ? 'text-green-600' : 'text-red-600'}`}>
   332→                        {member.telegram_id ? 'Connected' : 'Disconnected'}
   333→                      </span>
   334→                    </div>
   335→
   336→                    {/* X ID */}
   337→                    <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
   338→                      <span className="text-sm text-gray-600">X</span>
   339→                      <span className={`font-medium ${member.x_id ? 'text-gray-900' : 'text-red-600'}`}>
   340→                        {member.x_id ? (
   341→                          <a 
   342→                            href={`https://x.com/${member.x_id}`} 
   343→                            target="_blank" 
   344→                            rel="noopener noreferrer"
   345→                            className="hover:underline"
   346→                          >
   347→                            @{member.x_id}
   348→                          </a>
   349→                        ) : (
   350→                          'Not set'
   351→                        )}
   352→                      </span>
   353→                    </div>
   354→
   355→                    {/* Last Updated */}
   356→                    {member.updated_at && (
   357→                      <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
   358→                        <span className="text-sm text-gray-600">Last Updated</span>
   359→                        <span className="font-medium text-gray-900">
   360→                          {formatDate(member.updated_at)}
   361→                        </span>
   362→                      </div>
   363→                    )}
   364→                  </CardContent>
   365→                </Card>
   366→              ))}
   367→            </div>
   368→          )}
   369→        </div>
   370→      </div>
   371→    </div>
   372→  );
   373→}
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./components/auth/ProtectedRoute.tsx`

```text
     1→'use client'
     2→
     3→import { useEffect } from 'react'
     4→import { useRouter } from 'next/navigation'
     5→import { useAuth } from '@/contexts/AuthContext'
     6→
     7→interface ProtectedRouteProps {
     8→  children: React.ReactNode
     9→  requiredRoles?: string[]
    10→}
    11→
    12→export function ProtectedRoute({ children, requiredRoles }: ProtectedRouteProps) {
    13→  const { user, userProfile, loading } = useAuth()
    14→  const router = useRouter()
    15→
    16→  useEffect(() => {
    17→    if (!loading && !user) {
    18→      router.push('/auth')
    19→    }
    20→  }, [user, loading, router])
    21→
    22→  // Check role-based access after loading completes
    23→  useEffect(() => {
    24→    if (!loading && user && userProfile && requiredRoles && requiredRoles.length > 0) {
    25→      if (!requiredRoles.includes(userProfile.role)) {
    26→        router.push('/') // Redirect to home if role not allowed
    27→      }
    28→    }
    29→  }, [user, userProfile, loading, requiredRoles, router])
    30→
    31→  if (loading) {
    32→    return (
    33→      <div className="min-h-screen flex items-center justify-center bg-gray-50">
    34→        <div className="animate-spin rounded-full h-32 w-32 border-b-2" style={{ borderBottomColor: '#3e8692' }}></div>
    35→      </div>
    36→    )
    37→  }
    38→
    39→  if (!user) {
    40→    return null // Will redirect
    41→  }
    42→
    43→  // Check role access
    44→  if (requiredRoles && requiredRoles.length > 0 && userProfile) {
    45→    if (!requiredRoles.includes(userProfile.role)) {
    46→      return null // Will redirect
    47→    }
    48→  }
    49→
    50→  return <>{children}</>
    51→}
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
     1→'use client'
     2→
     3→import { useState } from 'react'
     4→import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button'
     5→import { Input } from '@/components/ui/input'
     6→import { Label } from '@/components/ui/label'
     7→import { Card, CardContent, CardDescription, CardHeader, CardTitle } from '@/components/ui/card'
     8→import { Alert, AlertDescription } from '@/components/ui/alert'
     9→import { supabase } from '@/lib/supabase'
    10→import { Loader2 } from 'lucide-react'
    11→import { GoogleSignInButton } from './GoogleSignInButton'
    12→
    13→interface LoginFormProps {
    14→  onToggleMode: () => void
    15→  onForgotPassword: () => void
    16→}
    17→
    18→export function LoginForm({ onToggleMode, onForgotPassword }: LoginFormProps) {
    19→  const [email, setEmail] = useState('')
    20→  const [password, setPassword] = useState('')
    21→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false)
    22→  const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null)
    23→
    24→  const handleLogin = async (e: React.FormEvent) => {
    25→    e.preventDefault()
    26→    setLoading(true)
    27→    setError(null)
    28→
    29→    try {
    30→      const { error } = await supabase.auth.signInWithPassword({
    31→        email,
    32→        password,
    33→      })
    34→
    35→      if (error) {
    36→        setError(error.message)
    37→      }
    38→    } catch (err) {
    39→      setError('An unexpected error occurred')
    40→    } finally {
    41→      setLoading(false)
    42→    }
    43→  }
    44→
    45→  return (
    46→    <Card className="w-full max-w-md">
    47→      <CardHeader className="space-y-1">
    48→        <CardTitle className="text-2xl text-center">Welcome back</CardTitle>
    49→        <CardDescription className="text-center">
    50→          Enter your credentials to access your account
    51→        </CardDescription>
    52→      </CardHeader>
    53→      <CardContent>
    54→        <form onSubmit={handleLogin} className="space-y-4">
    55→          {error && (
    56→            <Alert variant="destructive">
    57→              <AlertDescription>{error}</AlertDescription>
    58→            </Alert>
    59→          )}
    60→          
    61→          <div className="space-y-2">
    62→            <Label htmlFor="email">Email</Label>
    63→            <Input
    64→              id="email"
    65→              type="email"
    66→              placeholder="Enter your email"
    67→              value={email}
    68→              onChange={(e) => setEmail(e.target.value)}
    69→              required
    70→              className="focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-[#3e8692] focus:border-[#3e8692] auth-input"
    71→            />
    72→          </div>
    73→          
    74→          <div className="space-y-2">
    75→            <Label htmlFor="password">Password</Label>
    76→            <Input
    77→              id="password"
    78→              type="password"
    79→              placeholder="Enter your password"
    80→              value={password}
    81→              onChange={(e) => setPassword(e.target.value)}
    82→              required
    83→              className="focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-[#3e8692] focus:border-[#3e8692] auth-input"
    84→            />
    85→          </div>
    86→          
    87→          <Button
    88→            type="submit"
    89→            className="w-full hover:opacity-90"
    90→            disabled={loading}
    91→            style={{ backgroundColor: '#3e8692', color: 'white' }}
    92→          >
    93→            {loading && <Loader2 className="mr-2 h-4 w-4 animate-spin" />}
    94→            Sign In
    95→          </Button>
    96→
    97→          <div className="relative my-4">
    98→            <div className="absolute inset-0 flex items-center">
    99→              <span className="w-full border-t" />
   100→            </div>
   101→            <div className="relative flex justify-center text-xs uppercase">
   102→              <span className="bg-white px-2 text-gray-500">or</span>
   103→            </div>
   104→          </div>
   105→
   106→          <GoogleSignInButton mode="signin" />
   107→
   108→          <div className="text-center">
   109→            <button
   110→              type="button"
   111→              onClick={onForgotPassword}
   112→              className="text-sm hover:underline"
   113→              style={{ color: '#3e8692' }}
   114→            >
   115→              Forgot your password?
   116→            </button>
   117→          </div>
   118→          
   119→          <div className="text-center text-sm text-gray-600">
   120→            Don't have an account?{' '}
   121→            <button
   122→              type="button"
   123→              onClick={onToggleMode}
   124→              className="hover:underline"
   125→              style={{ color: '#3e8692' }}
   126→            >
   127→              Sign up
   128→            </button>
   129→          </div>
   130→        </form>
   131→      </CardContent>
   132→    </Card>
   133→  )
   134→}

 `./components/auth/GoogleSignInButton.tsx`

```text
     1→'use client'
     2→
     3→import { useState } from 'react'
     4→import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button'
     5→import { supabase } from '@/lib/supabase'
     6→import { Loader2 } from 'lucide-react'
     7→
     8→interface GoogleSignInButtonProps {
     9→  mode?: 'signin' | 'signup'
    10→}
    11→
    12→export function GoogleSignInButton({ mode = 'signin' }: GoogleSignInButtonProps) {
    13→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false)
    14→  const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null)
    15→
    16→  const handleGoogleSignIn = async () => {
    17→    setLoading(true)
    18→    setError(null)
    19→
    20→    try {
    21→      const { error } = await supabase.auth.signInWithOAuth({
    22→        provider: 'google',
    23→        options: {
    24→          redirectTo: `${window.location.origin}/auth/callback`,
    25→          queryParams: {
    26→            access_type: 'offline',
    27→            prompt: 'consent',
    28→          },
    29→        },
    30→      })
    31→
    32→      if (error) {
    33→        setError(error.message)
    34→        setLoading(false)
    35→      }
    36→      // If successful, user will be redirected to Google
    37→    } catch (err) {
    38→      setError('An unexpected error occurred')
    39→      setLoading(false)
    40→    }
    41→  }
    42→
    43→  return (
    44→    <div className="space-y-2">
    45→      <Button
    46→        type="button"
    47→        variant="outline"
    48→        className="w-full"
    49→        onClick={handleGoogleSignIn}
    50→        disabled={loading}
    51→      >
    52→        {loading ? (
    53→          <Loader2 className="mr-2 h-4 w-4 animate-spin" />
    54→        ) : (
    55→          <svg className="mr-2 h-4 w-4" viewBox="0 0 24 24">
    56→            <path
    57→              fill="#4285F4"
    58→              d="M22.56 12.25c0-.78-.07-1.53-.2-2.25H12v4.26h5.92c-.26 1.37-1.04 2.53-2.21 3.31v2.77h3.57c2.08-1.92 3.28-4.74 3.28-8.09z"
    59→            />
    60→            <path
    61→              fill="#34A853"
    62→              d="M12 23c2.97 0 5.46-.98 7.28-2.66l-3.57-2.77c-.98.66-2.23 1.06-3.71 1.06-2.86 0-5.29-1.93-6.16-4.53H2.18v2.84C3.99 20.53 7.7 23 12 23z"
    63→            />
    64→            <path
    65→              fill="#FBBC05"
    66→              d="M5.84 14.09c-.22-.66-.35-1.36-.35-2.09s.13-1.43.35-2.09V7.07H2.18C1.43 8.55 1 10.22 1 12s.43 3.45 1.18 4.93l2.85-2.22.81-.62z"
    67→            />
    68→            <path
    69→              fill="#EA4335"
    70→              d="M12 5.38c1.62 0 3.06.56 4.21 1.64l3.15-3.15C17.45 2.09 14.97 1 12 1 7.7 1 3.99 3.47 2.18 7.07l3.66 2.84c.87-2.6 3.3-4.53 6.16-4.53z"
    71→            />
    72→          </svg>
    73→        )}
    74→        {mode === 'signup' ? 'Sign up with Google' : 'Continue with Google'}
    75→      </Button>
    76→      {error && (
    77→        <p className="text-sm text-red-500 text-center">{error}</p>
    78→      )}
    79→    </div>
    80→  )
    81→}
    82→
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
     1→'use client';
     2→
     3→import { useState, useEffect } from 'react';
     4→import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button';
     5→import { Input } from '@/components/ui/input';
     6→import { Label } from '@/components/ui/label';
     7→import { Card, CardContent, CardDescription, CardHeader, CardTitle } from '@/components/ui/card';
     8→import { Table, TableBody, TableCell, TableHead, TableHeader, TableRow } from '@/components/ui/table';
     9→import { Dialog, DialogContent, DialogDescription, DialogFooter, DialogHeader, DialogTitle, DialogTrigger } from '@/components/ui/dialog';
    10→import { Alert, AlertDescription } from '@/components/ui/alert';
    11→import { Badge } from '@/components/ui/badge';
    12→import { FieldOptionsService, FieldOption, CreateFieldOptionData } from '@/lib/fieldOptionsService';
    13→import { useToast } from '@/hooks/use-toast';
    14→import { Plus, Edit, Trash2, GripVertical } from 'lucide-react';
    15→import { DndContext, closestCenter, KeyboardSensor, PointerSensor, useSensor, useSensors, DragEndEvent } from '@dnd-kit/core';
    16→import { arrayMove, SortableContext, sortableKeyboardCoordinates, verticalListSortingStrategy, useSortable } from '@dnd-kit/sortable';
    17→import { CSS } from '@dnd-kit/utilities';
    18→
    19→// Sortable row component
    20→interface SortableRowProps {
    21→  option: FieldOption;
    22→  onToggleActive: (option: FieldOption) => void;
    23→  onDelete: (id: string) => void;
    24→}
    25→
    26→function SortableRow({ option, onToggleActive, onDelete }: SortableRowProps) {
    27→  const {
    28→    attributes,
    29→    listeners,
    30→    setNodeRef,
    31→    transform,
    32→    transition,
    33→    isDragging,
    34→  } = useSortable({ id: option.id });
    35→
    36→  const style = {
    37→    transform: CSS.Transform.toString(transform),
    38→    transition,
    39→    opacity: isDragging ? 0.5 : 1,
    40→  };
    41→
    42→  return (
    43→    <TableRow ref={setNodeRef} style={style}>
    44→      <TableCell className="w-12">
    45→        <div {...attributes} {...listeners} className="cursor-grab active:cursor-grabbing text-gray-400 hover:text-gray-600">
    46→          <GripVertical className="h-5 w-5" />
    47→        </div>
    48→      </TableCell>
    49→      <TableCell className="font-medium">{option.option_value}</TableCell>
    50→      <TableCell>{option.display_order}</TableCell>
    51→      <TableCell>
    52→        <Badge variant={option.is_active ? 'default' : 'secondary'} style={option.is_active ? { backgroundColor: '#3e8692' } : {}}>
    53→          {option.is_active ? 'Active' : 'Inactive'}
    54→        </Badge>
    55→      </TableCell>
    56→      <TableCell>
    57→        {new Date(option.created_at).toLocaleDateString()}
    58→      </TableCell>
    59→      <TableCell className="text-right">
    60→        <div className="flex justify-end gap-2">
    61→          <Button
    62→            variant="outline"
    63→            size="sm"
    64→            onClick={() => onToggleActive(option)}
    65→            className="hover:opacity-90"
    66→          >
    67→            {option.is_active ? 'Deactivate' : 'Activate'}
    68→          </Button>
    69→          <Button
    70→            variant="outline"
    71→            size="sm"
    72→            onClick={() => onDelete(option.id)}
    73→            className="hover:bg-red-50 hover:text-red-700"
    74→          >
    75→            <Trash2 className="h-4 w-4" />
    76→          </Button>
    77→        </div>
    78→      </TableCell>
    79→    </TableRow>
    80→  );
    81→}
    82→
    83→export default function FieldOptionsPage() {
    84→  const [fieldOptions, setFieldOptions] = useState<FieldOption[]>([]);
    85→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);
    86→  const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null);
    87→  const [isAddDialogOpen, setIsAddDialogOpen] = useState(false);
    88→  const [editingOption, setEditingOption] = useState<FieldOption | null>(null);
    89→  const [newOption, setNewOption] = useState<CreateFieldOptionData>({
    90→    field_name: 'in_house',
    91→    option_value: '',
    92→    display_order: 0
    93→  });
    94→  const { toast } = useToast();
    95→
    96→  // Drag and drop sensors
    97→  const sensors = useSensors(
    98→    useSensor(PointerSensor),
    99→    useSensor(KeyboardSensor, {
   100→      coordinateGetter: sortableKeyboardCoordinates,
   101→    })
   102→  );
   103→
   104→  useEffect(() => {
   105→    fetchFieldOptions();
   106→  }, []);
   107→
   108→  const fetchFieldOptions = async () => {
   109→    try {
   110→      setLoading(true);
   111→      const options = await FieldOptionsService.getAllFieldOptions();
   112→      setFieldOptions(options);
   113→    } catch (err) {
   114→      setError('Failed to fetch field options');
   115→      console.error('Error fetching field options:', err);
   116→    } finally {
   117→      setLoading(false);
   118→    }
   119→  };
   120→
   121→  const handleDragEnd = async (event: DragEndEvent) => {
   122→    const { active, over } = event;
   123→
   124→    if (!over) return;
   125→
   126→    if (active.id !== over.id) {
   127→      const oldIndex = inHouseOptions.findIndex(o => o.id === active.id);
   128→      const newIndex = inHouseOptions.findIndex(o => o.id === over.id);
   129→
   130→      const reorderedOptions = arrayMove(inHouseOptions, oldIndex, newIndex);
   131→
   132→      // Optimistically update local state
   133→      const updatedOptions = reorderedOptions.map((opt, index) => ({
   134→        ...opt,
   135→        display_order: index + 1
   136→      }));
   137→
   138→      // Create a map for faster lookup
   139→      const updatedMap = new Map(updatedOptions.map(opt => [opt.id, opt]));
   140→
   141→      setFieldOptions(prev =>
   142→        prev.map(opt => {
   143→          // If this option was reordered, use the updated version
   144→          if (updatedMap.has(opt.id)) {
   145→            return updatedMap.get(opt.id)!;
   146→          }
   147→          // Otherwise keep the original
   148→          return opt;
   149→        })
   150→      );
   151→
   152→      // Save to database in background
   153→      try {
   154→        await FieldOptionsService.reorderFieldOptions(
   155→          'in_house',
   156→          reorderedOptions.map(o => o.id)
   157→        );
   158→      } catch (error) {
   159→        console.error('Error reordering options:', error);
   160→        toast({
   161→          title: 'Error',
   162→          description: 'Failed to reorder options',
   163→          variant: 'destructive',
   164→        });
   165→        // Revert by fetching from server
   166→        await fetchFieldOptions();
   167→      }
   168→    }
   169→  };
   170→
   171→  const handleAddOption = async () => {
   172→    try {
   173→      if (!newOption.option_value.trim()) {
   174→        toast({
   175→          title: 'Error',
   176→          description: 'Option value is required',
   177→          variant: 'destructive',
   178→        });
   179→        return;
   180→      }
   181→
   182→      await FieldOptionsService.createFieldOption(newOption);
   183→      await fetchFieldOptions();
   184→      setIsAddDialogOpen(false);
   185→      setNewOption({
   186→        field_name: 'in_house',
   187→        option_value: '',
   188→        display_order: 0
   189→      });
   190→      
   191→      toast({
   192→        title: 'Success',
   193→        description: 'Field option added successfully',
   194→      });
   195→    } catch (err) {
   196→      toast({
   197→        title: 'Error',
   198→        description: 'Failed to add field option',
   199→        variant: 'destructive',
   200→      });
   201→    }
   202→  };
   203→
   204→  const handleUpdateOption = async (id: string, updateData: { option_value: string; display_order: number }) => {
   205→    try {
   206→      await FieldOptionsService.updateFieldOption(id, updateData);
   207→      await fetchFieldOptions();
   208→      
   209→      toast({
   210→        title: 'Success',
   211→        description: 'Field option updated successfully',
   212→      });
   213→    } catch (err) {
   214→      toast({
   215→        title: 'Error',
   216→        description: 'Failed to update field option',
   217→        variant: 'destructive',
   218→      });
   219→    }
   220→  };
   221→
   222→  const handleDeleteOption = async (id: string) => {
   223→    try {
   224→      await FieldOptionsService.deleteFieldOption(id);
   225→      await fetchFieldOptions();
   226→      
   227→      toast({
   228→        title: 'Success',
   229→        description: 'Field option deleted successfully',
   230→      });
   231→    } catch (err) {
   232→      toast({
   233→        title: 'Error',
   234→        description: 'Failed to delete field option',
   235→        variant: 'destructive',
   236→      });
   237→    }
   238→  };
   239→
   240→  const handleToggleActive = async (option: FieldOption) => {
   241→    try {
   242→      await FieldOptionsService.updateFieldOption(option.id, {
   243→        is_active: !option.is_active
   244→      });
   245→      await fetchFieldOptions();
   246→      
   247→      toast({
   248→        title: 'Success',
   249→        description: `Field option ${option.is_active ? 'deactivated' : 'activated'} successfully`,
   250→      });
   251→    } catch (err) {
   252→      toast({
   253→        title: 'Error',
   254→        description: 'Failed to update field option status',
   255→        variant: 'destructive',
   256→      });
   257→    }
   258→  };
   259→
   260→  const inHouseOptions = fieldOptions
   261→    .filter(option => option.field_name === 'in_house')
   262→    .sort((a, b) => a.display_order - b.display_order);
   263→
   264→  if (loading) {
   265→    return (
   266→      <div className="min-h-[calc(100vh-64px)] w-full bg-gray-50">
   267→        <div className="w-full">
   268→          <div className="space-y-6">
   269→            <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
   270→              <div>
   271→                <h2 className="text-2xl font-bold text-gray-900">Field Options</h2>
   272→                <p className="text-gray-600">Manage dynamic dropdown options for KOL fields</p>
   273→              </div>
   274→            </div>
   275→            <div className="animate-pulse">
   276→              <div className="h-64 bg-gray-200 rounded"></div>
   277→            </div>
   278→          </div>
   279→        </div>
   280→      </div>
   281→    );
   282→  }
   283→
   284→  return (
   285→    <div className="min-h-[calc(100vh-64px)] w-full bg-gray-50">
   286→      <div className="w-full">
   287→        <div className="space-y-6">
   288→          <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
   289→            <div>
   290→              <h2 className="text-2xl font-bold text-gray-900">Field Options</h2>
   291→              <p className="text-gray-600">Manage dynamic dropdown options for KOL fields</p>
   292→            </div>
   293→            <Dialog open={isAddDialogOpen} onOpenChange={setIsAddDialogOpen}>
   294→              <DialogTrigger asChild>
   295→                <Button style={{ backgroundColor: '#3e8692', color: 'white' }} className="hover:opacity-90">
   296→                  <Plus className="h-4 w-4 mr-2" />
   297→                  Add Option
   298→                </Button>
   299→              </DialogTrigger>
   300→              <DialogContent>
   301→                <DialogHeader>
   302→                  <DialogTitle>Add New Field Option</DialogTitle>
   303→                  <DialogDescription>
   304→                    Add a new option for the in-house field dropdown.
   305→                  </DialogDescription>
   306→                </DialogHeader>
   307→                <div className="space-y-4">
   308→                  <div>
   309→                    <Label htmlFor="field_name">Field Name</Label>
   310→                    <Input
   311→                      id="field_name"
   312→                      value={newOption.field_name}
   313→                      onChange={(e) => setNewOption(prev => ({ ...prev, field_name: e.target.value }))}
   314→                      disabled
   315→                      className="auth-input"
   316→                    />
   317→                  </div>
   318→                  <div>
   319→                    <Label htmlFor="option_value">Option Value</Label>
   320→                    <Input
   321→                      id="option_value"
   322→                      value={newOption.option_value}
   323→                      onChange={(e) => setNewOption(prev => ({ ...prev, option_value: e.target.value }))}
   324→                      placeholder="e.g., Yes, No, Contractor"
   325→                      className="auth-input"
   326→                    />
   327→                  </div>
   328→                  <div>
   329→                    <Label htmlFor="display_order">Display Order</Label>
   330→                    <Input
   331→                      id="display_order"
   332→                      type="number"
   333→                      value={newOption.display_order}
   334→                      onChange={(e) => setNewOption(prev => ({ ...prev, display_order: parseInt(e.target.value) || 0 }))}
   335→                      className="auth-input"
   336→                    />
   337→                  </div>
   338→                </div>
   339→                <DialogFooter>
   340→                  <Button variant="outline" onClick={() => setIsAddDialogOpen(false)}>
   341→                    Cancel
   342→                  </Button>
   343→                  <Button onClick={handleAddOption} style={{ backgroundColor: '#3e8692', color: 'white' }} className="hover:opacity-90">
   344→                    Add Option
   345→                  </Button>
   346→                </DialogFooter>
   347→              </DialogContent>
   348→            </Dialog>
   349→          </div>
   350→
   351→          {error && (
   352→            <Alert variant="destructive">
   353→              <AlertDescription>{error}</AlertDescription>
   354→            </Alert>
   355→          )}
   356→
   357→          <Card>
   358→            <CardHeader>
   359→              <CardTitle>In-House Field Options</CardTitle>
   360→              <CardDescription>
   361→                Manage the dropdown options for the in-house field. These options will appear in the KOLs table and forms.
   362→              </CardDescription>
   363→            </CardHeader>
   364→            <CardContent>
   365→              <DndContext
   366→                sensors={sensors}
   367→                collisionDetection={closestCenter}
   368→                onDragEnd={handleDragEnd}
   369→              >
   370→                <Table>
   371→                  <TableHeader>
   372→                    <TableRow>
   373→                      <TableHead className="w-12"></TableHead>
   374→                      <TableHead>Option Value</TableHead>
   375→                      <TableHead>Display Order</TableHead>
   376→                      <TableHead>Status</TableHead>
   377→                      <TableHead>Created</TableHead>
   378→                      <TableHead className="text-right">Actions</TableHead>
   379→                    </TableRow>
   380→                  </TableHeader>
   381→                  <SortableContext
   382→                    items={inHouseOptions.map(o => o.id)}
   383→                    strategy={verticalListSortingStrategy}
   384→                  >
   385→                    <TableBody>
   386→                      {inHouseOptions.map((option) => (
   387→                        <SortableRow
   388→                          key={option.id}
   389→                          option={option}
   390→                          onToggleActive={handleToggleActive}
   391→                          onDelete={handleDeleteOption}
   392→                        />
   393→                      ))}
   394→                    </TableBody>
   395→                  </SortableContext>
   396→                </Table>
   397→              </DndContext>
   398→
   399→              {inHouseOptions.length === 0 && (
   400→                <div className="text-center py-8 text-gray-500">
   401→                  No field options found. Add your first option to get started.
   402→                </div>
   403→              )}
   404→            </CardContent>
   405→          </Card>
   406→        </div>
   407→      </div>
   408→    </div>
   409→  );
   410→}
   411→
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
     1→import { supabase } from './supabase'
     2→import { Database } from './database.types'
     3→
     4→type UserProfile = Database['public']['Tables']['users']['Row']
     5→type UserInsert = Database['public']['Tables']['users']['Insert']
     6→type UserUpdate = Database['public']['Tables']['users']['Update']
     7→
     8→export class UserService {
     9→  /**
    10→   * Create a new user profile (for OAuth users)
    11→   */
    12→  static async createUserProfile(userData: UserInsert): Promise<UserProfile | null> {
    13→    try {
    14→      const { data, error } = await supabase
    15→        .from('users')
    16→        .insert(userData)
    17→        .select()
    18→        .single()
    19→
    20→      if (error) {
    21→        console.error('Error creating user profile:', error)
    22→        return null
    23→      }
    24→
    25→      return data
    26→    } catch (error) {
    27→      console.error('Error creating user profile:', error)
    28→      return null
    29→    }
    30→  }
    31→
    32→  /**
    33→   * Get or create user profile (useful for OAuth flows)
    34→   */
    35→  static async getOrCreateUserProfile(
    36→    userId: string,
    37→    email: string,
    38→    name?: string,
    39→    role: 'super_admin' | 'admin' | 'member' | 'guest' = 'member'
    40→  ): Promise<UserProfile | null> {
    41→    // First try to get existing profile
    42→    const existingProfile = await this.getUserProfile(userId)
    43→    if (existingProfile) {
    44→      return existingProfile
    45→    }
    46→
    47→    // Create new profile for OAuth user
    48→    return this.createUserProfile({
    49→      id: userId,
    50→      email,
    51→      name: name || email.split('@')[0],
    52→      role,
    53→      is_active: true,
    54→    })
    55→  }
    56→
    57→  /**
    58→   * Get user profile by ID
    59→   */
    60→  static async getUserProfile(userId: string): Promise<UserProfile | null> {
    61→    try {
    62→      const { data, error } = await supabase
    63→        .from('users')
    64→        .select('*')
    65→        .eq('id', userId)
    66→        .single()
    67→
    68→      if (error) {
    69→        console.error('Error fetching user profile:', error)
    70→        return null
    71→      }
    72→
    73→      return data
    74→    } catch (error) {
    75→      console.error('Error fetching user profile:', error)
    76→      return null
    77→    }
    78→  }
    79→
    80→  /**
    81→   * Get all users (admin only)
    82→   */
    83→  static async getAllUsers(): Promise<UserProfile[]> {
    84→    try {
    85→      const { data, error } = await supabase
    86→        .from('users')
    87→        .select('*')
    88→        .order('created_at', { ascending: false })
    89→
    90→      if (error) {
    91→        console.error('Error fetching users:', error)
    92→        return []
    93→      }
    94→
    95→      return data || []
    96→    } catch (error) {
    97→      console.error('Error fetching users:', error)
    98→      return []
    99→    }
   100→  }
   101→
   102→  /**
   103→   * Update user profile
   104→   */
   105→  static async updateUserProfile(userId: string, updates: UserUpdate): Promise<boolean> {
   106→    try {
   107→      const { error } = await supabase
   108→        .from('users')
   109→        .update(updates)
   110→        .eq('id', userId)
   111→
   112→      if (error) {
   113→        console.error('Error updating user profile:', error)
   114→        return false
   115→      }
   116→
   117→      return true
   118→    } catch (error) {
   119→      console.error('Error updating user profile:', error)
   120→      return false
   121→    }
   122→  }
   123→
   124→  /**
   125→   * Update user role (super_admin only)
   126→   */
   127→  static async updateUserRole(userId: string, newRole: 'super_admin' | 'admin' | 'member' | 'guest'): Promise<boolean> {
   128→    return this.updateUserProfile(userId, { role: newRole })
   129→  }
   130→
   131→  /**
   132→   * Check if current user is super admin
   133→   */
   134→  static async isCurrentUserSuperAdmin(): Promise<boolean> {
   135→    try {
   136→      const { data: { user } } = await supabase.auth.getUser()
   137→      if (!user) return false
   138→
   139→      const profile = await this.getUserProfile(user.id)
   140→      return profile?.role === 'super_admin' && profile?.is_active === true
   141→    } catch (error) {
   142→      console.error('Error checking super admin status:', error)
   143→      return false
   144→    }
   145→  }
   146→
   147→  /**
   148→   * Deactivate user (admin only)
   149→   */
   150→  static async deactivateUser(userId: string): Promise<boolean> {
   151→    return this.updateUserProfile(userId, { is_active: false })
   152→  }
   153→
   154→  /**
   155→   * Activate user (admin only)
   156→   */
   157→  static async activateUser(userId: string): Promise<boolean> {
   158→    return this.updateUserProfile(userId, { is_active: true })
   159→  }
   160→
   161→  /**
   162→   * Check if current user is admin
   163→   */
   164→  static async isCurrentUserAdmin(): Promise<boolean> {
   165→    try {
   166→      const { data: { user } } = await supabase.auth.getUser()
   167→      if (!user) return false
   168→
   169→      const profile = await this.getUserProfile(user.id)
   170→      return (profile?.role === 'admin' || profile?.role === 'super_admin') && profile?.is_active === true
   171→    } catch (error) {
   172→      console.error('Error checking admin status:', error)
   173→      return false
   174→    }
   175→  }
   176→
   177→  /**
   178→   * Get current user's profile
   179→   */
   180→  static async getCurrentUserProfile(): Promise<UserProfile | null> {
   181→    try {
   182→      const { data: { user } } = await supabase.auth.getUser()
   183→      if (!user) return null
   184→
   185→      return await this.getUserProfile(user.id)
   186→    } catch (error) {
   187→      console.error('Error getting current user profile:', error)
   188→      return null
   189→    }
   190→  }
   191→}
   192→
   193→export type { UserProfile, UserInsert, UserUpdate }
```
</details></tool-use>

Let me try with proper path quoting:

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 2026-03-05 07:28:44Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./sql/001_create_users_table.sql`

```text
     1→-- Create users table
     2→CREATE TABLE users (
     3→  id UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id) PRIMARY KEY,
     4→  email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
     5→  name TEXT NOT NULL,
     6→  role TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (role IN ('admin', 'member', 'client')),
     7→  is_active BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT true,
     8→  created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
     9→  updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
    10→);
    11→
    12→-- Create a function to automatically update the updated_at column
    13→CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION update_updated_at_column()
    14→RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
    15→BEGIN
    16→  NEW.updated_at = NOW();
    17→  RETURN NEW;
    18→END;
    19→$$ language 'plpgsql';
    20→
    21→-- Create a trigger to automatically update updated_at
    22→CREATE TRIGGER update_users_updated_at 
    23→  BEFORE UPDATE ON users 
    24→  FOR EACH ROW 
    25→  EXECUTE FUNCTION update_updated_at_column();
    26→
    27→-- Enable Row Level Security (RLS)
    28→ALTER TABLE users ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
    29→
    30→-- Create policies for RLS
    31→-- Users can view their own record
    32→CREATE POLICY "Users can view own record" ON users
    33→  FOR SELECT USING (auth.uid() = id);
    34→
    35→-- Users can update their own record (except role and is_active)
    36→CREATE POLICY "Users can update own record" ON users
    37→  FOR UPDATE USING (auth.uid() = id);
    38→
    39→-- Admins can view all records
    40→CREATE POLICY "Admins can view all users" ON users
    41→  FOR SELECT USING (
    42→    EXISTS (
    43→      SELECT 1 FROM users 
    44→      WHERE id = auth.uid() AND role = 'admin'
    45→    )
    46→  );
    47→
    48→-- Admins can update all records
    49→CREATE POLICY "Admins can update all users" ON users
    50→  FOR UPDATE USING (
    51→    EXISTS (
    52→      SELECT 1 FROM users 
    53→      WHERE id = auth.uid() AND role = 'admin'
    54→    )
    55→  );
    56→
    57→-- Enable insert for auth trigger (allows trigger to create user records)
    58→CREATE POLICY "Enable insert for auth trigger" ON users
    59→  FOR INSERT 
    60→  WITH CHECK (auth.uid() = id);
    61→
    62→-- Function to handle new user registration
    63→CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION handle_new_user()
    64→RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
    65→BEGIN
    66→  INSERT INTO public.users (id, email, name, role)
    67→  VALUES (
    68→    NEW.id,
    69→    NEW.email,
    70→    COALESCE(
    71→      NEW.raw_user_meta_data->>'name', 
    72→      CONCAT(
    73→        COALESCE(NEW.raw_user_meta_data->>'first_name', ''), 
    74→        ' ', 
    75→        COALESCE(NEW.raw_user_meta_data->>'last_name', '')
    76→      ),
    77→      'User'
    78→    ),
    79→    COALESCE(NEW.raw_user_meta_data->>'role', 'member')
    80→  );
    81→  RETURN NEW;
    82→END;
    83→$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER;
    84→
    85→-- Trigger to automatically create user record when auth user is created
    86→CREATE TRIGGER on_auth_user_created
    87→  AFTER INSERT ON auth.users
    88→  FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION handle_new_user();
```
</details></tool-use>
