```text
     1→'use client';
     2→
     3→import { useState, useEffect } from 'react';
     4→import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button';
     5→import { Input } from '@/components/ui/input';
     6→import { Label } from '@/components/ui/label';
     7→import { Textarea } from '@/components/ui/textarea';
     8→import { Card, CardContent } from '@/components/ui/card';
     9→import { Dialog, DialogContent, DialogDescription, DialogFooter, DialogHeader, DialogTitle } from '@/components/ui/dialog';
    10→import { Alert, AlertDescription } from '@/components/ui/alert';
    11→import { Badge } from '@/components/ui/badge';
    12→import { Switch } from '@/components/ui/switch';
    13→import { Skeleton } from '@/components/ui/skeleton';
    14→import { ChangelogService, Changelog, CreateChangelogData } from '@/lib/changelogService';
    15→import { useAuth } from '@/contexts/AuthContext';
    16→import { useToast } from '@/hooks/use-toast';
    17→import { useRouter } from 'next/navigation';
    18→import { Plus, Edit, Trash2, Eye, EyeOff, Sparkles, ShieldAlert } from 'lucide-react';
    19→
    20→export default function ChangelogAdminPage() {
    21→  const { userProfile } = useAuth();
    22→  const router = useRouter();
    23→  const { toast } = useToast();
    24→
    25→  const [changelogs, setChangelogs] = useState<Changelog[]>([]);
    26→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);
    27→  const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null);
    28→  const [isDialogOpen, setIsDialogOpen] = useState(false);
    29→  const [editingChangelog, setEditingChangelog] = useState<Changelog | null>(null);
    30→  const [formData, setFormData] = useState<CreateChangelogData>({
    31→    version: '',
    32→    title: '',
    33→    content: '',
    34→    is_published: false
    35→  });
    36→  const [saving, setSaving] = useState(false);
    37→
    38→  // Check if user is super_admin
    39→  const isSuperAdmin = userProfile?.role === 'super_admin';
    40→
    41→  useEffect(() => {
    42→    if (userProfile && !isSuperAdmin) {
    43→      // Redirect non-super_admins
    44→      router.push('/');
    45→      return;
    46→    }
    47→    if (userProfile && isSuperAdmin) {
    48→      fetchChangelogs();
    49→    }
    50→  }, [userProfile, isSuperAdmin, router]);
    51→
    52→  const fetchChangelogs = async () => {
    53→    try {
    54→      setLoading(true);
    55→      const data = await ChangelogService.getAllChangelogs();
    56→      setChangelogs(data);
    57→    } catch (err) {
    58→      setError('Failed to fetch changelogs');
    59→      console.error('Error fetching changelogs:', err);
    60→    } finally {
    61→      setLoading(false);
    62→    }
    63→  };
    64→
    65→  const openCreateDialog = () => {
    66→    setEditingChangelog(null);
    67→    setFormData({
    68→      version: '',
    69→      title: '',
    70→      content: '',
    71→      is_published: false
    72→    });
    73→    setIsDialogOpen(true);
    74→  };
    75→
    76→  const openEditDialog = (changelog: Changelog) => {
    77→    setEditingChangelog(changelog);
    78→    setFormData({
    79→      version: changelog.version,
    80→      title: changelog.title,
    81→      content: changelog.content,
    82→      is_published: changelog.is_published
    83→    });
    84→    setIsDialogOpen(true);
    85→  };
    86→
    87→  const handleSave = async () => {
    88→    try {
    89→      if (!formData.version.trim() || !formData.title.trim() || !formData.content.trim()) {
    90→        toast({
    91→          title: 'Error',
    92→          description: 'Version, title, and content are required',
    93→          variant: 'destructive',
    94→        });
    95→        return;
    96→      }
    97→
    98→      setSaving(true);
    99→
   100→      if (editingChangelog) {
   101→        const wasPublished = editingChangelog.is_published;
   102→        await ChangelogService.updateChangelog(editingChangelog.id, {
   103→          version: formData.version,
   104→          title: formData.title,
   105→          content: formData.content,
   106→          is_published: formData.is_published,
   107→          published_at: formData.is_published ? new Date().toISOString() : null
   108→        });
   109→
   110→        // Send Telegram notification if switching from draft to published
   111→        if (!wasPublished && formData.is_published) {
   112→          try {
   113→            const notificationMessage = `<b>New Changelog Published</b>\n\n<b>Version:</b> v${formData.version}\n<b>Title:</b> ${formData.title}\n\n${formData.content.substring(0, 500)}${formData.content.length > 500 ? '...' : ''}`;
   114→
   115→            await fetch('/api/telegram/send', {
   116→              method: 'POST',
   117→              headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
   118→              body: JSON.stringify({ message: notificationMessage })
   119→            });
   120→          } catch (telegramErr) {
   121→            console.error('Failed to send Telegram notification:', telegramErr);
   122→          }
   123→        }
   124→
   125→        toast({
   126→          title: 'Success',
   127→          description: 'Changelog updated successfully',
   128→        });
   129→      } else {
   130→        await ChangelogService.createChangelog({
   131→          version: formData.version,
   132→          title: formData.title,
   133→          content: formData.content,
   134→          is_published: formData.is_published,
   135→          published_at: formData.is_published ? new Date().toISOString() : null
   136→        });
   137→
   138→        // Send Telegram notification if created as published
   139→        if (formData.is_published) {
   140→          try {
   141→            const notificationMessage = `<b>New Changelog Published</b>\n\n<b>Version:</b> v${formData.version}\n<b>Title:</b> ${formData.title}\n\n${formData.content.substring(0, 500)}${formData.content.length > 500 ? '...' : ''}`;
   142→
   143→            await fetch('/api/telegram/send', {
   144→              method: 'POST',
   145→              headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
   146→              body: JSON.stringify({ message: notificationMessage })
   147→            });
   148→          } catch (telegramErr) {
   149→            console.error('Failed to send Telegram notification:', telegramErr);
   150→          }
   151→        }
   152→
   153→        toast({
   154→          title: 'Success',
   155→          description: 'Changelog created successfully',
   156→        });
   157→      }
   158→
   159→      await fetchChangelogs();
   160→      setIsDialogOpen(false);
   161→    } catch (err) {
   162→      toast({
   163→        title: 'Error',
   164→        description: 'Failed to save changelog',
   165→        variant: 'destructive',
   166→      });
   167→    } finally {
   168→      setSaving(false);
   169→    }
   170→  };
   171→
   172→  const handleDelete = async (id: string) => {
   173→    if (!confirm('Are you sure you want to delete this changelog?')) return;
   174→
   175→    try {
   176→      await ChangelogService.deleteChangelog(id);
   177→      await fetchChangelogs();
   178→      toast({
   179→        title: 'Success',
   180→        description: 'Changelog deleted successfully',
   181→      });
   182→    } catch (err) {
   183→      toast({
   184→        title: 'Error',
   185→        description: 'Failed to delete changelog',
   186→        variant: 'destructive',
   187→      });
   188→    }
   189→  };
   190→
   191→  const handleTogglePublish = async (changelog: Changelog) => {
   192→    try {
   193→      if (changelog.is_published) {
   194→        await ChangelogService.unpublishChangelog(changelog.id);
   195→        toast({
   196→          title: 'Success',
   197→          description: 'Changelog unpublished',
   198→        });
   199→      } else {
   200→        await ChangelogService.publishChangelog(changelog.id);
   201→
   202→        // Send Telegram notification to HH Operations chat
   203→        try {
   204→          const notificationMessage = `<b>New Changelog Published</b>\n\n<b>Version:</b> v${changelog.version}\n<b>Title:</b> ${changelog.title}\n\n${changelog.content.substring(0, 500)}${changelog.content.length > 500 ? '...' : ''}`;
   205→
   206→          await fetch('/api/telegram/send', {
   207→            method: 'POST',
   208→            headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
   209→            body: JSON.stringify({ message: notificationMessage })
   210→          });
   211→        } catch (telegramErr) {
   212→          console.error('Failed to send Telegram notification:', telegramErr);
   213→          // Don't fail the publish if notification fails
   214→        }
   215→
   216→        toast({
   217→          title: 'Success',
   218→          description: 'Changelog published',
   219→        });
   220→      }
   221→      await fetchChangelogs();
   222→    } catch (err) {

Perfect! Now let me create a comprehensive summary. Let me compile all the information:

## Auth and Admin System Analysis

Based on my exploration of the codebase, here's a comprehensive breakdown of the auth and admin system:

---

### 1. AuthContext (`/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/contexts/AuthContext.tsx`)

**Lines 1-105: Full code**

This file defines the authentication context that manages user sessions and profiles:

- **AuthContextType interface** (lines 8-15):
  - `user: User | null` - Supabase auth user object
  - `userProfile: UserProfile | null` - Custom user profile with role info
  - `session: Session | null` - Active session
  - `loading: boolean` - Loading state
  - `signOut()` and `refreshUserProfile()` methods

- **User Loading Flow** (lines 40-52):
  - Uses `UserService.getOrCreateUserProfile()` to handle OAuth users
  - Extracts user name from `user_metadata.full_name`, `user_metadata.name`, or email
  - Default role for OAuth users: `'member'`
  - Sets `is_active: true` by default

- **Session Management** (lines 60-88):
  - Gets initial session via `supabase.auth.getSession()`
  - Subscribes to `onAuthStateChange` events
  - Loads user profile when session changes
  - Cleans up subscription on unmount

- **SignOut** (lines 90-93):
  - Calls `supabase.auth.signOut()`
  - Clears `userProfile`

---

### 2. User Service (`/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/userService.ts`)

**Lines 1-193: Full code**

Core service for user management operations:

**Key Methods:**
- `getOrCreateUserProfile()` (lines 35-55): Creates user profile for OAuth users if it doesn't exist, defaults to `'member'` role
- `getUserProfile(userId)` (lines 60-78): Fetch single user by ID
- `getAllUsers()` (lines 83-100): Fetch all users (admin only)
- `updateUserProfile(userId, updates)` (lines 105-122): Update user fields
- `updateUserRole()` (lines 127-129): Change user role
- `isCurrentUserSuperAdmin()` (lines 134-145): Check if current user is super_admin AND is_active
- `isCurrentUserAdmin()` (lines 164-175): Check if current user is admin or super_admin AND is_active
- `deactivateUser()` / `activateUser()` (lines 150-159): Toggle is_active status

**UserProfile Type** (from database.types.ts):
```typescript
Row: {
  created_at: string | null
  email: string
  id: string
  is_active: boolean
  name: string
  profile_photo_url: string | null
  role: string
  telegram_id: string | null
  updated_at: string | null
  x_id: string | null
}
```

---

### 3. Team Page (`/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/team/page.tsx`)

**Lines 1-373: Full code**

User management interface for team members:

**Data Fetching** (lines 86-118):
- Queries `users` table excluding guests: `.neq('role', 'guest')`
- Orders by `created_at` descending
- Displays 6 columns: name, email, role, join date, status, telegram/X connection

**Role Management** (lines 47-84):
- Only super_admin can change roles via dropdown
- Allows changing to: `super_admin`, `admin`, `member`, `guest`
- Shows loading spinner during update
- Uses `UserService.updateUserRole()`

**Display Fields** (lines 311-363):
- Join Date (created_at)
- Status (is_active: boolean)
- Telegram (telegram_id present?)
- X ID (x_id with link to X.com profile)
- Last Updated (updated_at)

**Access Control** (lines 274-307):
- Only super_admin can see role dropdown
- Super_admin can't change own role
- Non-super_admin users see role as read-only badge

---

### 4. Protected Route Component (`/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/components/auth/ProtectedRoute.tsx`)

**Lines 1-51: Full code**

Route protection mechanism:

- **Authentication Check** (lines 16-20): Redirects to `/auth` if not logged in
- **Role-Based Access** (lines 23-29): 
  - If `requiredRoles` provided, checks if `userProfile.role` is in the list
  - Redirects to home `/` if role not allowed
- **Loading State** (lines 31-37): Shows spinner while loading
- Returns `null` during redirect to prevent flash of content

---

### 5. Login Form (`/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/components/auth/LoginForm.tsx`)

**Lines 1-134: Full code**

Email/password authentication:

- **Login** (lines 24-43): 
  - Uses `supabase.auth.signInWithPassword()`
  - Shows error messages from Supabase
- **UI** (lines 45-134):
  - Email and password inputs
  - Error alert display
  - Forgot password link
  - Google sign-in button (embedded)
  - Sign up link

---

### 6. Google Sign-In Button (`/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/components/auth/GoogleSignInButton.tsx`)

**Lines 1-81: Full code**

OAuth authentication:

- **OAuth Flow** (lines 16-41):
  - `supabase.auth.signInWithOAuth({ provider: 'google' })`
  - Redirects to `/auth/callback` on completion
  - Sets `access_type: 'offline'` and `prompt: 'consent'` for OAuth options
  - Supports both `'signin'` and `'signup'` modes
- **UI** (lines 43-80):
  - Google logo SVG
  - Loading spinner during auth
  - Error message display

---

### 7. Users Table Schema (`/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/sql/001_create_users_table.sql`)

**Lines 1-88: Full code**

Database table definition:

```sql
CREATE TABLE users (
  id UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id) PRIMARY KEY,
  email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
  name TEXT NOT NULL,
  role TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (role IN ('admin', 'member', 'client')),
  is_active BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT true,
  created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW()
);
```

**Extended Fields** (added later, visible in database.types.ts):
- `profile_photo_url` - Profile image URL
- `telegram_id` - Telegram user ID for notifications
- `x_id` - X (Twitter) handle

**Triggers & Functions** (lines 12-25):
- Auto-update `updated_at` on row modification
- Trigger: `update_users_updated_at`

**User Registration Trigger** (lines 63-87):
- Fires when new `auth.users` record is created
- Automatically creates corresponding `users` row
- Extracts name from auth metadata
- Defaults role to `'member'`

---

### 8. RLS Policies (Row Level Security)

**Lines 27-60 in 001_create_users_table.sql:**

```sql
-- Users can view their own record
CREATE POLICY "Users can view own record" ON users
  FOR SELECT USING (auth.uid() = id);

-- Users can update their own record (except role and is_active)
CREATE POLICY "Users can update own record" ON users
  FOR UPDATE USING (auth.uid() = id);

-- Admins can view all records
CREATE POLICY "Admins can view all users" ON users
  FOR SELECT USING (
    EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM users 
      WHERE id = auth.uid() AND role = 'admin'
    )
  );

-- Admins can update all records
CREATE POLICY "Admins can update all users" ON users
  FOR UPDATE USING (
    EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM users 
      WHERE id = auth.uid() AND role = 'admin'
    )
  );

-- Enable insert for auth trigger
CREATE POLICY "Enable insert for auth trigger" ON users
  FOR INSERT 
  WITH CHECK (auth.uid() = id);
```

**Policy Summary:**
- Users can read/write only their own record
- Admins can read all records
- Admins can write all records (via role check)
- Auth trigger can insert new users

---

### 9. Admin Pages (`/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/admin/`)

**Changelog Management** (`app/admin/changelog/page.tsx`, lines 1-481):

- **Access Control** (lines 39, 42-46):
  - **Super_admin only** - Redirects non-super_admins to home
  - Guard at mount: `userProfile?.role === 'super_admin'`

- **Admin Actions** (lines 65-229):
  1. **Create Changelog** - Version, title, content, publish status
  2. **Edit Changelog** - Modify existing entries
  3. **Delete Changelog** - Remove entries
  4. **Toggle Publish** - Draft ↔ Published states
  5. **Telegram Notifications** - Send to HH Operations chat on publish (lines 115-123, 202-214)

- **Publish Logic** (lines 191-229):
  - Can publish draft or unpublish published
  - Sends Telegram notification when publishing
  - Sets `published_at` timestamp

**Field Options Management** (`app/admin/field-options/page.tsx`, lines 1-411):

- **Admin Features**:
  1. **Create Field Options** - Add dropdown values
  2. **Reorder Options** - Drag-and-drop interface (lines 121-169)
  3. **Toggle Active/Inactive** - Toggle `is_active` status
  4. **Delete Options** - Remove field options
  5. **Display Order** - Set sort order for dropdowns

**Admin Layout** (`app/admin/layout.tsx`, lines 1-3):
- Uses client layout (reuses clients/layout.tsx)

---

### 10. Role Hierarchy

**Available Roles:**
1. **`super_admin`** - Full system access (changelog, field options, user roles)
2. **`admin`** - Can view all users, has admin-level access
3. **`member`** - Default role for new users, limited access
4. **`guest`** - Lowest access (filtered out from team page)

**Permissions Model:**
- Super_admin role required for admin pages (changelog, field options)
- Admin/Super_admin can access team management
- Super_admin can modify user roles
- Role-based access enforced via `ProtectedRoute` component

---

### 11. Key Observations

**No Approval System Currently**:
- No `is_approved` or `approval_status` field in users table
- Users are active by default (`is_active: true`)
- No approval workflow in the UI

**Missing Status Fields**:
- Only `is_active` boolean exists (not a detailed status)
- No `invitation_status` or `email_verified` tracking
- No `approved_by` or `approval_date` fields

**Roles Limited**:
- Original schema had only `'admin', 'member', 'client'`
- Now extended with `'super_admin'` in application code
- Only used in application, not enforced at DB level

**Session Management**:
- Uses Supabase session tokens
- Auth context automatically refreshes on page load
- No manual token refresh logic in UI

---

This system has a solid foundation for role-based access control but lacks a formal user approval/invitation workflow. It relies on OAuth (Google) and email/password auth with automatic user profile creation.

```text
Now I have all the information needed. Here is the complete implementation plan.

---

## Approval Flow Implementation Plan

### Overview

This plan converts the app from "open access on sign-up" to "admin-approved access" by leveraging the existing `is_active` boolean field on the `users` table. New users will be created with `is_active = false`, blocked from the app by `ProtectedRoute`, and shown a pending approval screen. Admins approve users from the Team page, which triggers `is_active = true`. A Telegram notification fires on every new user row creation via `pg_net`, matching the existing `notify_contact_submission` pattern.

---

### Step 1: Database Migration -- Change default and update trigger

**File to create:** `sql/migrations/026_user_approval_flow.sql`

The current `handle_new_user()` trigger does not explicitly set `is_active`, so it inherits the column default of `true`. Two changes are needed:

1. **Change column default** from `true` to `false`:
```sql
ALTER TABLE public.users ALTER COLUMN is_active SET DEFAULT false;
```

2. **Update the `handle_new_user()` trigger function** to explicitly set `is_active = false` and NOT read the role from user metadata (preventing users from self-assigning roles):
```sql
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION handle_new_user()
RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
BEGIN
  INSERT INTO public.users (id, email, name, role, is_active)
  VALUES (
    NEW.id,
    NEW.email,
    COALESCE(
      NEW.raw_user_meta_data->>'name',
      CONCAT(
        COALESCE(NEW.raw_user_meta_data->>'first_name', ''),
        ' ',
        COALESCE(NEW.raw_user_meta_data->>'last_name', '')
      ),
      'User'
    ),
    'member',
    false
  );
  RETURN NEW;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER;
```

Key changes vs. current trigger:
- Role is hardcoded to `'member'` instead of reading from `raw_user_meta_data->>'role'` (closes the security hole where users pick their own role via sign-up form metadata).
- `is_active` is explicitly set to `false`.

3. **Ensure existing users remain active** (no data migration needed since all 8 current users already have `is_active = true`; the ALTER DEFAULT only affects new rows).

4. **Create the Telegram notification trigger** for new user sign-ups, following the exact `notify_contact_submission` pattern:

```sql
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION notify_new_user_signup()
RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
DECLARE
  telegram_url text := 'https://api.telegram.org/bot7996189688:AAF73zNcxoStIGaNxzYdV_75XYd0LhQ6fZw/sendMessage';
  message_text text;
  payload jsonb;
BEGIN
  message_text := '<b>New User Sign-Up (Pending Approval)</b>' || chr(10) || chr(10) ||
    '<b>Name:</b> ' || coalesce(NEW.name, 'N/A') || chr(10) ||
    '<b>Email:</b> ' || coalesce(NEW.email, 'N/A') || chr(10) ||
    '<b>Role:</b> ' || NEW.role || chr(10) || chr(10) ||
    '<a href="https://app.holohive.io/team">Approve/Reject in Team Page</a>';

  payload := jsonb_build_object(
    'chat_id', '-1002681460515',
    'text', message_text,
    'parse_mode', 'HTML',
    'message_thread_id', 2
  );

  PERFORM net.http_post(
    url := telegram_url,
    body := payload,
    headers := '{"Content-Type": "application/json"}'::jsonb
  );

  RETURN NEW;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER;

CREATE TRIGGER on_new_user_signup
  AFTER INSERT ON public.users
  FOR EACH ROW
  EXECUTE FUNCTION notify_new_user_signup();
```

This uses the same bot token, chat_id (`-1002681460515`), and thread_id (`2`) as the existing `notify_contact_submission` function. The message includes a direct link to the Team page for quick approval.

---

### Step 2: Update `UserService.getOrCreateUserProfile()` in `lib/userService.ts`

**Current code** (line 48-54):
```typescript
return this.createUserProfile({
  id: userId,
  email,
  name: name || email.split('@')[0],
  role,
  is_active: true,  // <-- PROBLEM: overrides to true
})
```

**Change to:**
```typescript
return this.createUserProfile({
  id: userId,
  email,
  name: name || email.split('@')[0],
  role: 'member',     // Always member, admin assigns real role
  is_active: false,    // Pending approval
})
```

Also update the function signature to remove the `role` parameter since it is no longer user-selectable:
```typescript
static async getOrCreateUserProfile(
  userId: string,
  email: string,
  name?: string
): Promise<UserProfile | null> {
```

And update the call site in `AuthContext.tsx` (line 45-51) to remove the role argument:
```typescript
const profile = await UserService.getOrCreateUserProfile(
  authUser.id,
  authUser.email || '',
  name
)
```

**Important edge case:** The DB trigger `handle_new_user()` fires on `auth.users` INSERT, so for email sign-ups the `public.users` row already exists by the time `getOrCreateUserProfile` runs. The `getUserProfile()` call on line 42 will find the existing row and return it, so `createUserProfile` only runs for edge cases where the trigger row is missing. Either way, `is_active` will be `false`.

---

### Step 3: Update `ProtectedRoute` component -- `/components/auth/ProtectedRoute.tsx`

Add an `is_active` check that shows a "Pending Approval" screen instead of the app. The key logic:

```typescript
export function ProtectedRoute({ children, requiredRoles }: ProtectedRouteProps) {
  const { user, userProfile, loading, signOut } = useAuth()
  const router = useRouter()

  useEffect(() => {
    if (!loading && !user) {
      router.push('/auth')
    }
  }, [user, loading, router])

  useEffect(() => {
    if (!loading && user && userProfile && requiredRoles && requiredRoles.length > 0) {
      if (!requiredRoles.includes(userProfile.role)) {
        router.push('/')
      }
    }
  }, [user, userProfile, loading, requiredRoles, router])

  if (loading) {
    return (/* existing spinner */)
  }

  if (!user) {
    return null
  }

  // NEW: Check if user is pending approval
  if (userProfile && !userProfile.is_active) {
    return <PendingApprovalScreen onSignOut={signOut} />
  }

  // existing role check...
  if (requiredRoles && requiredRoles.length > 0 && userProfile) {
    if (!requiredRoles.includes(userProfile.role)) {
      return null
    }
  }

  return <>{children}</>
}
```

The `PendingApprovalScreen` component (defined inline or as a separate component in the same file):

```tsx
function PendingApprovalScreen({ onSignOut }: { onSignOut: () => Promise<void> }) {
  return (
    <div className="min-h-screen flex items-center justify-center bg-gray-50">
      <div className="max-w-md w-full text-center p-8">
        <div className="w-16 h-16 mx-auto mb-6 rounded-full flex items-center justify-center"
             style={{ backgroundColor: '#3e869215' }}>
          <svg className="w-8 h-8" style={{ color: '#3e8692' }} /* clock/hourglass icon */ />
        </div>
        <h2 className="text-2xl font-bold text-gray-900 mb-2">Account Pending Approval</h2>
        <p className="text-gray-600 mb-6">
          Your account has been created and is waiting for an administrator to approve it.
          You will be able to access the app once approved.
        </p>
        <button
          onClick={onSignOut}
          className="px-6 py-2 rounded-md text-white hover:opacity-90"
          style={{ backgroundColor: '#3e8692' }}
        >
          Sign Out
        </button>
      </div>
    </div>
  )
}
```

Uses teal branding (`#3e8692`). Includes a sign-out button so the user is not trapped.

---

### Step 5: Update Team Page -- `/app/team/page.tsx`

Add a "Pending Approval" section at the top of the page, visible to super_admin and admin users. Changes:

1. **Split `filteredTeamMembers` into two lists:**
```typescript
const pendingMembers = filteredTeamMembers.filter(m => !m.is_active);
const activeMembers = filteredTeamMembers.filter(m => m.is_active);
```

2. **Add approve/reject handlers:**
```typescript
const handleApproveUser = async (userId: string) => {
  setUpdatingRoleId(userId);
  const success = await UserService.activateUser(userId);
  if (success) {
    setTeamMembers(prev =>
      prev.map(m => m.id === userId ? { ...m, is_active: true } : m)
    );
    toast({ title: 'User approved', description: 'User can now access the app.' });
  } else {
    toast({ title: 'Error', description: 'Failed to approve user.', variant: 'destructive' });
  }
  setUpdatingRoleId(null);
};

const handleRejectUser = async (userId: string) => {
  // Delete the user row (or keep inactive -- depends on preference)
  // Simplest: just leave as is_active=false. For reject, could delete from auth.users
  // For now, deactivate is already the default state. A "reject" could remove the row.
  setUpdatingRoleId(userId);
  const { error } = await supabase.from('users').delete().eq('id', userId);
  if (!error) {
    setTeamMembers(prev => prev.filter(m => m.id !== userId));
    toast({ title: 'User rejected', description: 'User has been removed.' });
  }
  setUpdatingRoleId(null);
};
```

3. **Render a pending approval section** above the active members grid:
```tsx
{pendingMembers.length > 0 && (isSuperAdmin || userProfile?.role === 'admin') && (
  <div className="space-y-4">
    <h3 className="text-lg font-semibold text-gray-900 flex items-center gap-2">
      <span className="w-2 h-2 rounded-full bg-amber-500" />
      Pending Approval ({pendingMembers.length})
    </h3>
    <div className="grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3 gap-4">
      {pendingMembers.map(member => (
        <Card key={member.id} className="border-amber-200 bg-amber-50/50">
          {/* Same card header with avatar/name/email */}
          <CardContent>
            <p className="text-sm text-gray-500 mb-3">
              Signed up {formatDate(member.created_at)}
            </p>
            <div className="flex gap-2">
              {/* Role selector for assigning before approval */}
              <Select value={member.role} onValueChange={(v) => handleRoleChange(member.id, v)}>
                <SelectTrigger className="h-8 text-xs flex-1">
                  <SelectValue />
                </SelectTrigger>
                <SelectContent>
                  <SelectItem value="member">Member</SelectItem>
                  <SelectItem value="admin">Admin</SelectItem>
                  <SelectItem value="client">Client</SelectItem>
                </SelectContent>
              </Select>
              <Button size="sm" onClick={() => handleApproveUser(member.id)}
                      style={{ backgroundColor: '#3e8692' }} className="text-white">
                Approve
              </Button>
              <Button size="sm" variant="outline" onClick={() => handleRejectUser(member.id)}
                      className="text-red-600 border-red-200 hover:bg-red-50">
                Reject
              </Button>
            </div>
          </CardContent>
        </Card>
      ))}
    </div>
  </div>
)}
```

4. **Update `fetchTeamMembers`** to also fetch inactive users (currently it fetches all users, which already includes `is_active=false` users, so no change needed).

5. **Allow admin role** (not just super_admin) to approve users. Add an `isAdmin` state:
```typescript
const [isAdmin, setIsAdmin] = useState(false);
// In useEffect:
const isAdm = await UserService.isCurrentUserAdmin();
setIsAdmin(isAdm);
```

---

### Step 6: Update Auth Page and Callback

**`/app/auth/page.tsx`** -- Currently on line 21-28, after login it redirects to `/` which will hit `ProtectedRoute`. If the user is inactive, they will see the pending screen. No change needed here; the flow works naturally.

**`/app/auth/callback/route.ts`** -- Currently redirects to `origin` (home) after OAuth. Same as above, `ProtectedRoute` handles it. No change needed.

---

### Summary of All Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| **New SQL migration** | Change `is_active` default to `false`, update `handle_new_user()` to hardcode `role='member'` and `is_active=false`, add `notify_new_user_signup()` trigger with pg_net Telegram notification |
| `/lib/userService.ts` | Set `is_active: false` and `role: 'member'` in `getOrCreateUserProfile()`, remove role parameter |
| `/contexts/AuthContext.tsx` | Remove role argument from `getOrCreateUserProfile()` call (line 49) |
| `/components/auth/ProtectedRoute.tsx` | Add `is_active` check, render `PendingApprovalScreen` with sign-out button for inactive users |
| `/components/auth/SignUpForm.tsx` | Remove role `<Select>`, remove role from sign-up metadata, update success message to mention approval |
| `/app/team/page.tsx` | Split users into pending/active, add approve/reject buttons with role assignment, allow admin role to approve |

### Sequencing

1. Apply the database migration first (it is safe -- only changes default for new rows, existing users unaffected).
2. Update `userService.ts` and `AuthContext.tsx` (these go together).
3. Update `ProtectedRoute.tsx` (the gating mechanism).
4. Update `SignUpForm.tsx` (cosmetic -- removes role picker).
5. Update `team/page.tsx` (admin UI for approval).

Steps 2-5 can be done in any order since they are all client-side, but deploying the DB migration first ensures new sign-ups immediately get `is_active=false`.

### Edge Cases

- **Existing users:** All 8 current users have `is_active=true`, so they are unaffected.
- **OAuth race condition:** The DB trigger fires first and creates the row with `is_active=false`. When `getOrCreateUserProfile()` runs, it finds the existing row via `getUserProfile()` and returns it (with `is_active=false`). The user then sees the pending screen.
- **Email sign-up flow:** User signs up, gets email confirmation link, confirms email, logs in, and sees pending screen. The Telegram notification fires on the initial `public.users` INSERT (triggered by `auth.users` INSERT).
- **Admin self-lockout prevention:** The pending screen check in ProtectedRoute explicitly checks `userProfile.is_active`, so existing active admins are never affected. The sign-out button on the pending screen prevents users from being stuck.

---

### Critical Files for Implementation

- `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/components/auth/ProtectedRoute.tsx` - Core gating logic: add is_active check and pending approval screen
- `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/userService.ts` - Change getOrCreateUserProfile() to set is_active=false and hardcode member role
- `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/team/page.tsx` - Add pending users section with approve/reject/role-assign UI
- `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/components/auth/SignUpForm.tsx` - Remove role selector, update success message
- `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/sql/001_create_users_table.sql` - Reference for DB trigger pattern; new migration will alter this trigger

_**Agent - sidechain (2026-03-05 07:30:05Z)**_

Design an approval flow for user sign-ups in a Next.js + Supabase app. Here's the current state:

**Current System:**
- Users table has: id, email, name, role, is_active (default true), created_at, updated_at, profile_photo_url, telegram_id, x_id
- Roles: super_admin, admin, member, client, guest
- AuthContext loads user profile via UserService.getOrCreateUserProfile() which creates profile with role='member', is_active=true for OAuth users
- There's an auto-trigger on auth.users INSERT that creates a users row with role='member'
- ProtectedRoute component checks auth and optionally required roles, but does NOT check is_active or any approval status
- Team page at /app/team/page.tsx shows all users with role management (super_admin only)
- Sign-up form lets anyone pick Member/Client/Admin role
- Google OAuth also auto-creates users

**Requirements:**
- New users who sign up (email or Google OAuth) should NOT have access until approved by an admin
- Admins should be able to approve/reject pending users from the Team page
- Rejected/pending users should see a "pending approval" screen after login, not the app
- Keep it simple - use existing is_active field (default to false for new signups) or add a status field

**Key files:**
- `contexts/AuthContext.tsx` - loads user profile, session management
- `lib/userService.ts` - getOrCreateUserProfile(), getAllUsers(), activateUser(), deactivateUser()
- `components/auth/ProtectedRoute.tsx` - route protection, checks auth and roles
- `components/auth/SignUpForm.tsx` - email/password sign-up with role selector
- `components/auth/GoogleSignInButton.tsx` - OAuth sign-up
- `app/team/page.tsx` - team management, role changes (super_admin only)
- `sql/001_create_users_table.sql` - users table, RLS policies, auth trigger
- Database trigger `handle_new_user()` auto-creates users row on auth.users INSERT

**Constraints:**
- Use the existing `is_active` field (set to false by default for new users)
- ProtectedRoute should block inactive users and show a pending approval screen
- Team page should show pending users and let admins approve/reject
- Remove the role selector from sign-up form (admins assign roles)
- Send Telegram notification when new user signs up (same pattern as contact_submissions - use pg_net trigger to call Telegram API directly)
- Teal branding (#3e8692)

Design a concrete implementation plan.

<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./contexts/AuthContext.tsx`

```text
     1→'use client'
     2→
     3→import { createContext, useContext, useEffect, useState } from 'react'
     4→import { User, Session } from '@supabase/supabase-js'
     5→import { supabase } from '@/lib/supabase'
     6→import { UserService, UserProfile } from '@/lib/userService'
     7→
     8→interface AuthContextType {
     9→  user: User | null
    10→  userProfile: UserProfile | null
    11→  session: Session | null
    12→  loading: boolean
    13→  signOut: () => Promise<void>
    14→  refreshUserProfile: () => Promise<void>
    15→}
    16→
    17→const AuthContext = createContext<AuthContextType>({
    18→  user: null,
    19→  userProfile: null,
    20→  session: null,
    21→  loading: true,
    22→  signOut: async () => {},
    23→  refreshUserProfile: async () => {},
    24→})
    25→
    26→export const useAuth = () => {
    27→  const context = useContext(AuthContext)
    28→  if (!context) {
    29→    throw new Error('useAuth must be used within an AuthProvider')
    30→  }
    31→  return context
    32→}
    33→
    34→export const AuthProvider = ({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) => {
    35→  const [user, setUser] = useState<User | null>(null)
    36→  const [userProfile, setUserProfile] = useState<UserProfile | null>(null)
    37→  const [session, setSession] = useState<Session | null>(null)
    38→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true)
    39→
    40→  const loadUserProfile = async (authUser: User) => {
    41→    // Use getOrCreateUserProfile to handle OAuth users who don't have a profile yet
    42→    const name = authUser.user_metadata?.full_name ||
    43→                 authUser.user_metadata?.name ||
    44→                 authUser.email?.split('@')[0]
    45→    const profile = await UserService.getOrCreateUserProfile(
    46→      authUser.id,
    47→      authUser.email || '',
    48→      name,
    49→      'member' // Default role for OAuth users
    50→    )
    51→    setUserProfile(profile)
    52→  }
    53→
    54→  const refreshUserProfile = async () => {
    55→    if (user) {
    56→      await loadUserProfile(user)
    57→    }
    58→  }
    59→
    60→  useEffect(() => {
    61→    // Get initial session
    62→    supabase.auth.getSession().then(({ data: { session } }) => {
    63→      setSession(session)
    64→      setUser(session?.user ?? null)
    65→      if (session?.user) {
    66→        loadUserProfile(session.user)
    67→      } else {
    68→        setUserProfile(null)
    69→      }
    70→      setLoading(false)
    71→    })
    72→
    73→    // Listen for auth changes
    74→    const {
    75→      data: { subscription },
    76→    } = supabase.auth.onAuthStateChange((_event, session) => {
    77→      setSession(session)
    78→      setUser(session?.user ?? null)
    79→      if (session?.user) {
    80→        loadUserProfile(session.user)
    81→      } else {
    82→        setUserProfile(null)
    83→      }
    84→      setLoading(false)
    85→    })
    86→
    87→    return () => subscription.unsubscribe()
    88→  }, [])
    89→
    90→  const signOut = async () => {
    91→    await supabase.auth.signOut()
    92→    setUserProfile(null)
    93→  }
    94→
    95→  const value = {
    96→    user,
    97→    userProfile,
    98→    session,
    99→    loading,
   100→    signOut,
   101→    refreshUserProfile,
   102→  }
   103→
   104→  return <AuthContext.Provider value={value}>{children}</AuthContext.Provider>
   105→}
```
</details></tool-use>

     1→import { supabase } from './supabase'

     4→type UserProfile = Database['public']['Tables']['users']['Row']
     5→type UserInsert = Database['public']['Tables']['users']['Insert']
     6→type UserUpdate = Database['public']['Tables']['users']['Update']
     7→
     8→export class UserService {
     9→  /**
    10→   * Create a new user profile (for OAuth users)
    11→   */
    12→  static async createUserProfile(userData: UserInsert): Promise<UserProfile | null> {
    13→    try {
    14→      const { data, error } = await supabase
    15→        .from('users')
    16→        .insert(userData)
    17→        .select()
    18→        .single()
    19→
    20→      if (error) {
    21→        console.error('Error creating user profile:', error)
    22→        return null
    23→      }
    24→
    25→      return data
    26→    } catch (error) {
    27→      console.error('Error creating user profile:', error)
    28→      return null
    29→    }
    30→  }
    31→
    32→  /**
    33→   * Get or create user profile (useful for OAuth flows)
    34→   */
    35→  static async getOrCreateUserProfile(
    36→    userId: string,
    37→    email: string,
    38→    name?: string,
    39→    role: 'super_admin' | 'admin' | 'member' | 'guest' = 'member'
    40→  ): Promise<UserProfile | null> {
    41→    // First try to get existing profile
    42→    const existingProfile = await this.getUserProfile(userId)
    43→    if (existingProfile) {
    44→      return existingProfile
    45→    }
    46→
    47→    // Create new profile for OAuth user
    48→    return this.createUserProfile({
    49→      id: userId,
    50→      email,
    51→      name: name || email.split('@')[0],
    52→      role,
    53→      is_active: true,
    54→    })
    55→  }
    56→
    57→  /**
    58→   * Get user profile by ID
    59→   */
    60→  static async getUserProfile(userId: string): Promise<UserProfile | null> {
    61→    try {
    62→      const { data, error } = await supabase
    63→        .from('users')
    64→        .select('*')
    65→        .eq('id', userId)
    66→        .single()
    67→
    68→      if (error) {
    69→        console.error('Error fetching user profile:', error)
    70→        return null
    71→      }
    72→
    73→      return data
    74→    } catch (error) {
    75→      console.error('Error fetching user profile:', error)
    76→      return null
    77→    }
    78→  }
    79→
    80→  /**
    81→   * Get all users (admin only)
    82→   */
    83→  static async getAllUsers(): Promise<UserProfile[]> {
    84→    try {
    85→      const { data, error } = await supabase
    86→        .from('users')
    87→        .select('*')
    88→        .order('created_at', { ascending: false })
    89→
    90→      if (error) {
    91→        console.error('Error fetching users:', error)
    92→        return []
    93→      }
    94→
    95→      return data || []
    96→    } catch (error) {
    97→      console.error('Error fetching users:', error)
    98→      return []
    99→    }
   100→  }
   101→
   102→  /**
   103→   * Update user profile
   104→   */
   105→  static async updateUserProfile(userId: string, updates: UserUpdate): Promise<boolean> {
   106→    try {
   107→      const { error } = await supabase
   108→        .from('users')
   109→        .update(updates)
   110→        .eq('id', userId)
   111→
   112→      if (error) {
   113→        console.error('Error updating user profile:', error)
   114→        return false
   115→      }
   116→
   117→      return true
   118→    } catch (error) {
   119→      console.error('Error updating user profile:', error)
   120→      return false
   121→    }
   122→  }
   123→
   124→  /**
   125→   * Update user role (super_admin only)
   126→   */
   127→  static async updateUserRole(userId: string, newRole: 'super_admin' | 'admin' | 'member' | 'guest'): Promise<boolean> {
   128→    return this.updateUserProfile(userId, { role: newRole })
   129→  }
   130→
   131→  /**
   132→   * Check if current user is super admin
   133→   */
   134→  static async isCurrentUserSuperAdmin(): Promise<boolean> {
   135→    try {
   136→      const { data: { user } } = await supabase.auth.getUser()
   137→      if (!user) return false
   138→
   139→      const profile = await this.getUserProfile(user.id)
   140→      return profile?.role === 'super_admin' && profile?.is_active === true
   141→    } catch (error) {
   142→      console.error('Error checking super admin status:', error)
   143→      return false
   144→    }
   145→  }
   146→
   147→  /**
   148→   * Deactivate user (admin only)
   149→   */
   150→  static async deactivateUser(userId: string): Promise<boolean> {
   151→    return this.updateUserProfile(userId, { is_active: false })
   152→  }
   153→
   154→  /**
   155→   * Activate user (admin only)
   156→   */
   157→  static async activateUser(userId: string): Promise<boolean> {
   158→    return this.updateUserProfile(userId, { is_active: true })
   159→  }
   160→
   161→  /**
   162→   * Check if current user is admin
   163→   */
   164→  static async isCurrentUserAdmin(): Promise<boolean> {
   165→    try {
   166→      const { data: { user } } = await supabase.auth.getUser()
   167→      if (!user) return false
   168→
   169→      const profile = await this.getUserProfile(user.id)
   170→      return (profile?.role === 'admin' || profile?.role === 'super_admin') && profile?.is_active === true
   171→    } catch (error) {
   172→      console.error('Error checking admin status:', error)
   173→      return false
   174→    }
   175→  }
   176→
   177→  /**
   178→   * Get current user's profile
   179→   */
   180→  static async getCurrentUserProfile(): Promise<UserProfile | null> {
   181→    try {
   182→      const { data: { user } } = await supabase.auth.getUser()
   183→      if (!user) return null
   184→
   185→      return await this.getUserProfile(user.id)
   186→    } catch (error) {
   187→      console.error('Error getting current user profile:', error)
   188→      return null
   189→    }
   190→  }
   191→}

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./components/auth/ProtectedRoute.tsx`

```text
     1→'use client'
     2→
     3→import { useEffect } from 'react'
     4→import { useRouter } from 'next/navigation'
     5→import { useAuth } from '@/contexts/AuthContext'
     6→
     7→interface ProtectedRouteProps {
     8→  children: React.ReactNode
     9→  requiredRoles?: string[]
    10→}
    11→
    12→export function ProtectedRoute({ children, requiredRoles }: ProtectedRouteProps) {
    13→  const { user, userProfile, loading } = useAuth()
    14→  const router = useRouter()
    15→
    16→  useEffect(() => {
    17→    if (!loading && !user) {
    18→      router.push('/auth')
    19→    }
    20→  }, [user, loading, router])
    21→
    22→  // Check role-based access after loading completes
    23→  useEffect(() => {
    24→    if (!loading && user && userProfile && requiredRoles && requiredRoles.length > 0) {
    25→      if (!requiredRoles.includes(userProfile.role)) {
    26→        router.push('/') // Redirect to home if role not allowed
    27→      }
    28→    }
    29→  }, [user, userProfile, loading, requiredRoles, router])
    30→
    31→  if (loading) {
    32→    return (
    33→      <div className="min-h-screen flex items-center justify-center bg-gray-50">
    34→        <div className="animate-spin rounded-full h-32 w-32 border-b-2" style={{ borderBottomColor: '#3e8692' }}></div>
    35→      </div>
    36→    )
    37→  }
    38→
    39→  if (!user) {
    40→    return null // Will redirect
    41→  }
    42→
    43→  // Check role access
    44→  if (requiredRoles && requiredRoles.length > 0 && userProfile) {
    45→    if (!requiredRoles.includes(userProfile.role)) {
    46→      return null // Will redirect
    47→    }
    48→  }
    49→
    50→  return <>{children}</>
    51→}
```
</details></tool-use>

 `./components/auth/SignUpForm.tsx`

```text
     1→'use client'
     2→
     3→import { useState } from 'react'
     4→import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button'
     5→import { Input } from '@/components/ui/input'
     6→import { Label } from '@/components/ui/label'
     7→import { Card, CardContent, CardDescription, CardHeader, CardTitle } from '@/components/ui/card'
     8→import { Alert, AlertDescription } from '@/components/ui/alert'
     9→import { Select, SelectContent, SelectItem, SelectTrigger, SelectValue } from '@/components/ui/select'
    10→import { supabase } from '@/lib/supabase'
    11→import { Loader2 } from 'lucide-react'
    12→import { GoogleSignInButton } from './GoogleSignInButton'
    13→
    14→interface SignUpFormProps {
    15→  onToggleMode: () => void
    16→}
    17→
    18→export function SignUpForm({ onToggleMode }: SignUpFormProps) {
    19→  const [email, setEmail] = useState('')
    20→  const [password, setPassword] = useState('')
    21→  const [confirmPassword, setConfirmPassword] = useState('')
    22→  const [name, setName] = useState('')
    23→  const [role, setRole] = useState<'super_admin' | 'admin' | 'member' | 'client'>('member')
    24→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false)
    25→  const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null)
    26→  const [success, setSuccess] = useState(false)
    27→
    28→  const handleSignUp = async (e: React.FormEvent) => {
    29→    e.preventDefault()
    30→    setLoading(true)
    31→    setError(null)
    32→
    33→    if (password !== confirmPassword) {
    34→      setError('Passwords do not match')
    35→      setLoading(false)
    36→      return
    37→    }
    38→
    39→    if (password.length < 6) {
    40→      setError('Password must be at least 6 characters long')
    41→      setLoading(false)
    42→      return
    43→    }
    44→
    45→    try {
    46→      const { error } = await supabase.auth.signUp({
    47→        email,
    48→        password,
    49→        options: {
    50→          data: {
    51→            name: name.trim(),
    52→            role: role,
    53→          },
    54→        },
    55→      })
    56→
    57→      if (error) {
    58→        setError(error.message)
    59→      } else {
    60→        setSuccess(true)
    61→      }
    62→    } catch (err) {
    63→      setError('An unexpected error occurred')
    64→    } finally {
    65→      setLoading(false)
    66→    }
    67→  }
    68→
    69→  if (success) {
    70→    return (
    71→      <Card className="w-full max-w-md">
    72→        <CardHeader className="space-y-1">
    73→          <CardTitle className="text-2xl text-center">Check your email</CardTitle>
    74→          <CardDescription className="text-center">
    75→            We've sent you a confirmation link to verify your account
    76→          </CardDescription>
    77→        </CardHeader>
    78→        <CardContent>
    79→          <Button 
    80→            onClick={onToggleMode} 
    81→            className="w-full hover:opacity-90"
    82→            style={{ backgroundColor: '#3e8692', color: 'white' }}
    83→          >
    84→            Back to Sign In
    85→          </Button>
    86→        </CardContent>
    87→      </Card>
    88→    )
    89→  }
    90→
    91→  return (
    92→    <Card className="w-full max-w-md">
    93→      <CardHeader className="space-y-1">
    94→        <CardTitle className="text-2xl text-center">Create an account</CardTitle>
    95→        <CardDescription className="text-center">
    96→          Enter your information to get started
    97→        </CardDescription>
    98→      </CardHeader>
    99→      <CardContent>
   100→        <form onSubmit={handleSignUp} className="space-y-4">
   101→          {error && (
   102→            <Alert variant="destructive">
   103→              <AlertDescription>{error}</AlertDescription>
   104→            </Alert>
   105→          )}
   106→          
   107→          <div className="space-y-2">
   108→            <Label htmlFor="name">Name</Label>
   109→            <Input
   110→              id="name"
   111→              placeholder="Enter your name"
   112→              value={name}
   113→              onChange={(e) => setName(e.target.value)}
   114→              required
   115→              className="focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-[#3e8692] focus:border-[#3e8692] auth-input"
   116→            />
   117→          </div>
   118→          
   119→          <div className="space-y-2">
   120→            <Label htmlFor="email">Email</Label>
   121→            <Input
   122→              id="email"
   123→              type="email"
   124→              placeholder="Enter your email"
   125→              value={email}
   126→              onChange={(e) => setEmail(e.target.value)}
   127→              required
   128→              className="focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-[#3e8692] focus:border-[#3e8692] auth-input"
   129→            />
   130→          </div>
   131→          
   132→          <div className="space-y-2">
   133→            <Label htmlFor="password">Password</Label>
   134→            <Input
   135→              id="password"
   136→              type="password"
   137→              placeholder="Create a password"
   138→              value={password}
   139→              onChange={(e) => setPassword(e.target.value)}
   140→              required
   141→              className="focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-[#3e8692] focus:border-[#3e8692] auth-input"
   142→            />
   143→          </div>
   144→          
   145→          <div className="space-y-2">
   146→            <Label htmlFor="confirmPassword">Confirm Password</Label>
   147→            <Input
   148→              id="confirmPassword"
   149→              type="password"
   150→              placeholder="Confirm your password"
   151→              value={confirmPassword}
   152→              onChange={(e) => setConfirmPassword(e.target.value)}
   153→              required
   154→              className="focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-[#3e8692] focus:border-[#3e8692] auth-input"
   155→            />
   156→          </div>
   157→          
   158→          <div className="space-y-2">
   159→            <Label htmlFor="role">Role</Label>
   160→            <Select value={role} onValueChange={(value) => setRole(value as 'super_admin' | 'admin' | 'member' | 'client')}>
   161→              <SelectTrigger className="focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-[#3e8692] focus:border-[#3e8692] auth-input">
   162→                <SelectValue placeholder="Select your role" />
   163→              </SelectTrigger>
   164→              <SelectContent>
   165→                <SelectItem value="member">Member</SelectItem>
   166→                <SelectItem value="client">Client</SelectItem>
   167→                <SelectItem value="admin">Admin</SelectItem>
   168→              </SelectContent>
   169→            </Select>
   170→          </div>
   171→          
   172→          <Button
   173→            type="submit"
   174→            className="w-full hover:opacity-90"
   175→            disabled={loading}
   176→            style={{ backgroundColor: '#3e8692', color: 'white' }}
   177→          >
   178→            {loading && <Loader2 className="mr-2 h-4 w-4 animate-spin" />}
   179→            Create Account
   180→          </Button>
   181→
   182→          <div className="relative my-4">
   183→            <div className="absolute inset-0 flex items-center">
   184→              <span className="w-full border-t" />
   185→            </div>
   186→            <div className="relative flex justify-center text-xs uppercase">
   187→              <span className="bg-white px-2 text-gray-500">or</span>
   188→            </div>
   189→          </div>
   190→
   191→          <GoogleSignInButton mode="signup" />
   192→
   193→          <div className="text-center text-sm text-gray-600">
   194→            Already have an account?{' '}
   195→            <button
   196→              type="button"
   197→              onClick={onToggleMode}
   198→              className="hover:underline"
   199→              style={{ color: '#3e8692' }}
   200→            >
   201→              Sign in
   202→            </button>
   203→          </div>
   204→        </form>
   205→      </CardContent>
   206→    </Card>
   207→  )
   208→}
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./app/team/page.tsx`

```text
     1→'use client';
     2→
     3→import React, { useState, useEffect } from 'react';
     4→import { Card, CardContent, CardHeader } from '@/components/ui/card';
     5→import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button';
     6→import { Input } from '@/components/ui/input';
     7→import { Skeleton } from '@/components/ui/skeleton';
     8→import { Select, SelectContent, SelectItem, SelectTrigger, SelectValue } from '@/components/ui/select';
     9→import { Search, Shield, Loader2 } from 'lucide-react';
    10→import { supabase } from '@/lib/supabase';
    11→import { useToast } from '@/hooks/use-toast';
    12→import { useAuth } from '@/contexts/AuthContext';
    13→import { UserService } from '@/lib/userService';
    14→
    15→interface TeamMember {
    16→  id: string;
    17→  name: string;
    18→  email: string;
    19→  role: string;
    20→  created_at: string | null;
    21→  updated_at: string | null;
    22→  is_active: boolean;
    23→  telegram_id?: string | null;
    24→  x_id?: string | null;
    25→  profile_photo_url?: string | null;
    26→}
    27→
    28→export default function TeamPage() {
    29→  const [teamMembers, setTeamMembers] = useState<TeamMember[]>([]);
    30→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);
    31→  const [searchTerm, setSearchTerm] = useState('');
    32→  const [isSuperAdmin, setIsSuperAdmin] = useState(false);
    33→  const [updatingRoleId, setUpdatingRoleId] = useState<string | null>(null);
    34→  const { toast } = useToast();
    35→  const { userProfile } = useAuth();
    36→
    37→  useEffect(() => {
    38→    fetchTeamMembers();
    39→    checkSuperAdminStatus();
    40→  }, []);
    41→
    42→  const checkSuperAdminStatus = async () => {
    43→    const isSuper = await UserService.isCurrentUserSuperAdmin();
    44→    setIsSuperAdmin(isSuper);
    45→  };
    46→
    47→  const handleRoleChange = async (userId: string, newRole: string) => {
    48→    if (!isSuperAdmin) return;
    49→
    50→    setUpdatingRoleId(userId);
    51→    try {
    52→      const success = await UserService.updateUserRole(
    53→        userId,
    54→        newRole as 'super_admin' | 'admin' | 'member' | 'guest'
    55→      );
    56→
    57→      if (success) {
    58→        setTeamMembers(prev =>
    59→          prev.map(member =>
    60→            member.id === userId ? { ...member, role: newRole } : member
    61→          )
    62→        );
    63→        toast({
    64→          title: 'Role updated',
    65→          description: 'User role has been updated successfully.',
    66→        });
    67→      } else {
    68→        toast({
    69→          title: 'Error',
    70→          description: 'Failed to update user role.',
    71→          variant: 'destructive',
    72→        });
    73→      }
    74→    } catch (error) {
    75→      console.error('Error updating role:', error);
    76→      toast({
    77→        title: 'Error',
    78→        description: 'Failed to update user role.',
    79→        variant: 'destructive',
    80→      });
    81→    } finally {
    82→      setUpdatingRoleId(null);
    83→    }
    84→  };
    85→
    86→  const fetchTeamMembers = async () => {
    87→    try {
    88→      setLoading(true);
    89→      
    90→      // Fetch all users except those with guest role
    91→      const { data: users, error } = await supabase
    92→        .from('users')
    93→        .select('*')
    94→        .neq('role', 'guest')
    95→        .order('created_at', { ascending: false });
    96→
    97→      if (error) {
    98→        console.error('Error fetching team members:', error);
    99→        toast({
   100→          title: 'Error',
   101→          description: 'Failed to fetch team members.',
   102→          variant: 'destructive',
   103→        });
   104→        return;
   105→      }
   106→
   107→      setTeamMembers(users || []);
   108→    } catch (error) {
   109→      console.error('Error fetching team members:', error);
   110→      toast({
   111→        title: 'Error',
   112→        description: 'Failed to fetch team members.',
   113→        variant: 'destructive',
   114→      });
   115→    } finally {
   116→      setLoading(false);
   117→    }
   118→  };
   119→
   120→  const filteredTeamMembers = teamMembers.filter(member =>
   121→    member.name?.toLowerCase().includes(searchTerm.toLowerCase()) ||
   122→    member.email?.toLowerCase().includes(searchTerm.toLowerCase())
   123→  );
   124→
   125→  const getUserInitials = (name: string) => {
   126→    return name
   127→      .split(' ')
   128→      .map(word => word.charAt(0).toUpperCase())
   129→      .join('')
   130→      .slice(0, 2);
   131→  };
   132→
   133→  const formatDate = (dateString: string | null) => {
   134→    if (!dateString) return 'Unknown date';
   135→    return new Date(dateString).toLocaleDateString('en-US', {
   136→      year: 'numeric',
   137→      month: 'short',
   138→      day: 'numeric'
   139→    });
   140→  };
   141→
   142→  if (loading) {
   143→    return (
   144→      <div className="min-h-[calc(100vh-64px)] w-full bg-gray-50">
   145→        <div className="w-full">
   146→          <div className="space-y-6">
   147→            <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
   148→              <div>
   149→                <h2 className="text-2xl font-bold text-gray-900">Team Members</h2>
   150→                <p className="text-gray-600">Manage your team members</p>
   151→              </div>
   152→            </div>
   153→            <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
   154→              <div className="relative flex-1 max-w-sm">
   155→                <Search className="absolute left-3 top-1/2 transform -translate-y-1/2 h-4 w-4 text-gray-400" />
   156→                <Input placeholder="Search team members..." className="pl-10 auth-input" disabled />
   157→              </div>
   158→            </div>
   159→            <div className="grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3 gap-6">
   160→              {Array.from({ length: 6 }).map((_, index) => (
   161→                <Card key={index}>
   162→                  <CardHeader className="pb-4">
   163→                    <div className="flex flex-col items-center text-center">
   164→                      <Skeleton className="h-16 w-16 rounded-full mb-3" />
   165→                      <div className="mb-2">
   166→                        <Skeleton className="h-6 w-32 mb-2" />
   167→                        <Skeleton className="h-4 w-48 mb-2" />
   168→                      </div>
   169→                      <Skeleton className="h-5 w-16 rounded-full" />
   170→                    </div>
   171→                  </CardHeader>
   172→                  <CardContent className="space-y-3">
   173→                    <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
   174→                      <Skeleton className="h-4 w-20" />
   175→                      <Skeleton className="h-4 w-24" />
   176→                    </div>
   177→                    <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
   178→                      <Skeleton className="h-4 w-16" />
   179→                      <Skeleton className="h-4 w-16" />
   180→                    </div>
   181→                    <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
   182→                      <Skeleton className="h-4 w-20" />
   183→                      <Skeleton className="h-4 w-24" />
   184→                    </div>
   185→                    <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
   186→                      <Skeleton className="h-4 w-24" />
   187→                      <Skeleton className="h-4 w-24" />
   188→                    </div>
   189→                  </CardContent>
   190→                </Card>
   191→              ))}
   192→            </div>
   193→          </div>
   194→        </div>
   195→      </div>
   196→    );
   197→  }
   198→
   199→  return (
   200→    <div className="min-h-[calc(100vh-64px)] w-full bg-gray-50">
   201→      <div className="w-full">
   202→        <div className="space-y-6">
   203→          <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
   204→            <div>
   205→              <h2 className="text-2xl font-bold text-gray-900">Team Members</h2>
   206→              <p className="text-gray-600">Manage your team members</p>
   207→            </div>
   208→          </div>
   209→          
   210→          <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
   211→            <div className="relative flex-1 max-w-sm">
   212→              <Search className="absolute left-3 top-1/2 transform -translate-y-1/2 h-4 w-4 text-gray-400" />
   213→              <Input
   214→                placeholder="Search team members..."
   215→                className="pl-10 auth-input"
   216→                value={searchTerm}
   217→                onChange={(e) => setSearchTerm(e.target.value)}
   218→              />
   219→            </div>
   220→          </div>
   221→
   222→          {filteredTeamMembers.length === 0 ? (
   223→            <div className="text-center py-12">
   224→              <Shield className="h-12 w-12 text-gray-400 mx-auto mb-4" />
   225→              <h3 className="text-lg font-medium text-gray-900 mb-2">
   226→                {searchTerm ? 'No team members found' : 'No team members yet'}
   227→              </h3>
   228→              <p className="text-gray-600">
   229→                {searchTerm 
   230→                  ? 'Try adjusting your search terms.' 
   231→                  : 'Team members will appear here.'
   232→                }
   233→              </p>
   234→            </div>
   235→          ) : (
   236→            <div className="grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3 gap-6">
   237→              {filteredTeamMembers.map((member) => (
   238→                <Card key={member.id}>
   239→                  <CardHeader className="pb-4">
   240→                    <div className="flex flex-col items-center text-center">
   241→                      {member.profile_photo_url ? (
   242→                        <div className="w-16 h-16 rounded-full overflow-hidden mb-3 relative">
   243→                          <img 
   244→                            src={member.profile_photo_url} 
   245→                            alt={`${member.name || 'User'} profile`}
   246→                            className="w-full h-full object-cover"
   247→                            onError={(e) => {
   248→                              // Fallback to initials if image fails to load
   249→                              const target = e.target as HTMLImageElement;
   250→                              target.style.display = 'none';
   251→                              target.nextElementSibling?.classList.remove('hidden');
   252→                            }}
   253→                          />
   254→                          <div className="w-16 h-16 bg-gradient-to-br from-[#3e8692] to-[#2d6470] rounded-full flex items-center justify-center absolute top-0 left-0 hidden">
   255→                            <span className="text-white font-bold text-xl">
   256→                              {getUserInitials(member.name || member.email)}
   257→                            </span>
   258→                          </div>
   259→                        </div>
   260→                      ) : (
   261→                        <div className="w-16 h-16 bg-gradient-to-br from-[#3e8692] to-[#2d6470] rounded-full flex items-center justify-center mb-3">
   262→                          <span className="text-white font-bold text-xl">
   263→                            {getUserInitials(member.name || member.email)}
   264→                          </span>
   265→                        </div>
   266→                      )}
   267→                      <div className="mb-2">
   268→                        <h3 className="font-semibold text-gray-900 text-lg">
   269→                          {member.name || 'Unnamed User'}
   270→                        </h3>
   271→                        <p className="text-sm text-gray-500">{member.email}</p>
   272→                      </div>
   273→                      <div className="flex items-center space-x-2">
   274→                        {isSuperAdmin && member.id !== userProfile?.id ? (
   275→                          <div className="relative">
   276→                            {updatingRoleId === member.id && (
   277→                              <div className="absolute inset-0 flex items-center justify-center bg-white/80 rounded z-10">
   278→                                <Loader2 className="h-4 w-4 animate-spin text-[#3e8692]" />
   279→                              </div>
   280→                            )}
   281→                            <Select
   282→                              value={member.role}
   283→                              onValueChange={(value) => handleRoleChange(member.id, value)}
   284→                              disabled={updatingRoleId === member.id}
   285→                            >
   286→                              <SelectTrigger className="h-7 text-xs w-[120px]">
   287→                                <SelectValue />
   288→                              </SelectTrigger>
   289→                              <SelectContent>
   290→                                <SelectItem value="super_admin">Super Admin</SelectItem>
   291→                                <SelectItem value="admin">Admin</SelectItem>
   292→                                <SelectItem value="member">Member</SelectItem>
   293→                                <SelectItem value="guest">Guest</SelectItem>
   294→                              </SelectContent>
   295→                            </Select>
   296→                          </div>
   297→                        ) : (
   298→                          <span className={`inline-flex items-center px-2 py-1 rounded-full text-xs font-medium ${
   299→                            member.role === 'super_admin'
   300→                              ? 'bg-purple-100 text-purple-800'
   301→                              : 'bg-blue-100 text-blue-800'
   302→                          }`}>
   303→                            {member.role === 'super_admin'
   304→                              ? 'Super Admin'
   305→                              : member.role.charAt(0).toUpperCase() + member.role.slice(1)}
   306→                          </span>
   307→                        )}
   308→                      </div>
   309→                    </div>
   310→                  </CardHeader>
   311→                  <CardContent className="space-y-3">
   312→                    {/* Join Date */}
   313→                    <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
   314→                      <span className="text-sm text-gray-600">Join Date</span>
   315→                      <span className="font-medium text-gray-900">
   316→                        {formatDate(member.created_at)}
   317→                      </span>
   318→                    </div>
   319→
   320→                    {/* Status */}
   321→                    <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
   322→                      <span className="text-sm text-gray-600">Status</span>
   323→                      <span className="font-medium text-gray-900">
   324→                        {member.is_active ? 'Active' : 'Inactive'}
   325→                      </span>
   326→                    </div>
   327→
   328→                    {/* Telegram Status */}
   329→                    <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
   330→                      <span className="text-sm text-gray-600">Telegram</span>
   331→                      <span className={`font-medium ${member.telegram_id ? 'text-green-600' : 'text-red-600'}`}>
   332→                        {member.telegram_id ? 'Connected' : 'Disconnected'}
   333→                      </span>
   334→                    </div>
   335→
   336→                    {/* X ID */}
   337→                    <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
   338→                      <span className="text-sm text-gray-600">X</span>
   339→                      <span className={`font-medium ${member.x_id ? 'text-gray-900' : 'text-red-600'}`}>
   340→                        {member.x_id ? (
   341→                          <a 
   342→                            href={`https://x.com/${member.x_id}`} 
   343→                            target="_blank" 
   344→                            rel="noopener noreferrer"
   345→                            className="hover:underline"
   346→                          >
   347→                            @{member.x_id}
   348→                          </a>
   349→                        ) : (
   350→                          'Not set'
   351→                        )}
   352→                      </span>
   353→                    </div>
   354→
   355→                    {/* Last Updated */}
   356→                    {member.updated_at && (
   357→                      <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
   358→                        <span className="text-sm text-gray-600">Last Updated</span>
   359→                        <span className="font-medium text-gray-900">
   360→                          {formatDate(member.updated_at)}
   361→                        </span>
   362→                      </div>
   363→                    )}
   364→                  </CardContent>
   365→                </Card>
   366→              ))}
   367→            </div>
   368→          )}
   369→        </div>
   370→      </div>
   371→    </div>
   372→  );
   373→}
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./components/auth/GoogleSignInButton.tsx`

```text
     1→'use client'
     2→
     3→import { useState } from 'react'
     4→import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button'
     5→import { supabase } from '@/lib/supabase'
     6→import { Loader2 } from 'lucide-react'
     7→
     8→interface GoogleSignInButtonProps {
     9→  mode?: 'signin' | 'signup'
    10→}
    11→
    12→export function GoogleSignInButton({ mode = 'signin' }: GoogleSignInButtonProps) {
    13→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false)
    14→  const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null)
    15→
    16→  const handleGoogleSignIn = async () => {
    17→    setLoading(true)
    18→    setError(null)
    19→
    20→    try {
    21→      const { error } = await supabase.auth.signInWithOAuth({
    22→        provider: 'google',
    23→        options: {
    24→          redirectTo: `${window.location.origin}/auth/callback`,
    25→          queryParams: {
    26→            access_type: 'offline',
    27→            prompt: 'consent',
    28→          },
    29→        },
    30→      })
    31→
    32→      if (error) {
    33→        setError(error.message)
    34→        setLoading(false)
    35→      }
    36→      // If successful, user will be redirected to Google
    37→    } catch (err) {
    38→      setError('An unexpected error occurred')
    39→      setLoading(false)
    40→    }
    41→  }
    42→
    43→  return (
    44→    <div className="space-y-2">
    45→      <Button
    46→        type="button"
    47→        variant="outline"
    48→        className="w-full"
    49→        onClick={handleGoogleSignIn}
    50→        disabled={loading}
    51→      >
    52→        {loading ? (
    53→          <Loader2 className="mr-2 h-4 w-4 animate-spin" />
    54→        ) : (
    55→          <svg className="mr-2 h-4 w-4" viewBox="0 0 24 24">
    56→            <path
    57→              fill="#4285F4"
    58→              d="M22.56 12.25c0-.78-.07-1.53-.2-2.25H12v4.26h5.92c-.26 1.37-1.04 2.53-2.21 3.31v2.77h3.57c2.08-1.92 3.28-4.74 3.28-8.09z"
    59→            />
    60→            <path
    61→              fill="#34A853"
    62→              d="M12 23c2.97 0 5.46-.98 7.28-2.66l-3.57-2.77c-.98.66-2.23 1.06-3.71 1.06-2.86 0-5.29-1.93-6.16-4.53H2.18v2.84C3.99 20.53 7.7 23 12 23z"
    63→            />
    64→            <path
    65→              fill="#FBBC05"
    66→              d="M5.84 14.09c-.22-.66-.35-1.36-.35-2.09s.13-1.43.35-2.09V7.07H2.18C1.43 8.55 1 10.22 1 12s.43 3.45 1.18 4.93l2.85-2.22.81-.62z"
    67→            />
    68→            <path
    69→              fill="#EA4335"
    70→              d="M12 5.38c1.62 0 3.06.56 4.21 1.64l3.15-3.15C17.45 2.09 14.97 1 12 1 7.7 1 3.99 3.47 2.18 7.07l3.66 2.84c.87-2.6 3.3-4.53 6.16-4.53z"
    71→            />
    72→          </svg>
    73→        )}
    74→        {mode === 'signup' ? 'Sign up with Google' : 'Continue with Google'}
    75→      </Button>
    76→      {error && (
    77→        <p className="text-sm text-red-500 text-center">{error}</p>
    78→      )}
    79→    </div>
    80→  )
    81→}
    82→
```
